blocks:
Sūrat al-Isrā'

Meccan, and it is 111 verses.

Sūrat al-Isrā'

It is Meccan, and it is said (that all is Meccan) except five verses, and it is said except eight (verses). And the number of its verses is one hundred and eleven verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) سُبْحانَ الَّذِي أَسْرى بِعَبْدِهِ لَيْلاً مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ الْأَقْصَى الَّذِي بارَكْنا حَوْلَهُ ** (1) Glory be to Him Who carried His servant on a night journey from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque whose environs We have blessed, meaning, to the dominion (ملكوت) of the Farthest Mosque (المسجد الأقصى) which is in the heaven (السّماءِ), as becomes apparent from the forthcoming narratives (الأخبار). لِنُرِيَهُ مِنْ آياتِنا إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ ** that We might show him some of Our signs. Indeed, He is the All-hearing, of the sayings of His servant, the All-seeing (الْبَصِيرُ) of his actions. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was sitting in the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام) and looked towards the heaven (السّماءِ) once and towards the Ka'bah once, then he said: سُبْحانَ الَّذِي أَسْرى بِعَبْدِهِ لَيْلاً مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ الْأَقْصَى ** (Glory be to Him Who carried His servant on a night journey from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque). He repeated that three times. Then he turned to Isma'il al-Ju'fi and said: "What do the people of Iraq say about this verse, O Iraqi?" He replied: "They say He carried him on a night journey from the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام) to Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس)." He (peace be upon him) said: "It is not as they say. Rather, He carried him on a night journey from this to this," and he pointed with his hand towards the heaven (السّماءِ), and said: "What is between them is a sanctuary (حرم)." And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the mosques (المساجد) that possess virtue (الفضل). He said: "The Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام) and the Mosque of the Messenger (مسجد الرّسُول)." It was asked: "And the Farthest Mosque (المسجد الأقصى)?" He said: "That is in the heaven (السّماء). To it, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was carried on a night journey." It was said: "Indeed, the people say that it is Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس)." He said: "The Mosque of Kufa (مسجد الكوفة) is superior to it." And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked how many times the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآلهِ وسلم ascended (عَرَج). He said: "Twice." And al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Gabriel (جبرئيل) came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with the Buraq (البراق), which was smaller than a mule and larger than a donkey, with restless ears, its eyes in its hoof, and its stride reached the extent of its gaze. And it is added in al-Kafi: When it reached a mountain, its forelegs shortened and its hind legs lengthened, and when it descended, its forelegs lengthened and its hind legs shortened. It had long eyelashes (أهدب) (١) and a mane (العُرف).

(2) The right one has two wings behind it.

In al-Uyun, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "That Allah, the Almighty, subjugated the Buraq (البراق) for me, and it is a beast (دابّة) from the beasts (دوابّ) of Paradise (الجنّةِ). It is neither short nor tall. If Allah had permitted it, it would have traversed the world (الدّنيا) and the Hereafter (الآخِرة) in a single stride (جرية واحدة), and it is the most beautiful of beasts in color."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Gabriel (جبرئيل), Michael (ميكائيل), and Israfil (إسرافيل) came with the Buraq (البراق) to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. So one took hold of the reins (اللجام), and one took hold of the stirrup (الركاب), and the third adjusted His clothes upon Him. Then it became restless (فتضعضعت)."

(3) Al-Buraq

Jibril slapped it, then he said: "Be still, O Buraq! For no prophet before him has ridden you, nor will anyone like him ride you after him." He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: So it ascended with him and raised him a height that was not great, while Jibril (peace be upon him) was with him, showing him the signs (الآيات) from the heaven and the earth. He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: While I was on my journey, behold, a caller called out from my right: "O Muhammad!" but I did not answer him and did not turn toward him. Then a caller called out from my left: "O Muhammad!" but I did not answer him and did not turn toward him. Then a woman met me, uncovering her arms, upon her was every adornment of the world (الدنيا). So she said: "O Muhammad, wait for me until I speak to you," but I did not turn toward her. Then I traveled on, and I heard a sound that frightened me, so I passed it by. Then Jibril descended with me. So he said: "Pray (صَلِّ)." So I prayed (صَلَّيْتُ). Then he said to me: "Do you know where you prayed (صَلَّيْتَ)?" So I said: "No." He said: "You prayed (صَلَّيْتَ) in Taybah (طيبة)."

(4) "And to it is your migration (مهاجرك)." Then I mounted, and we proceeded as long as Allah willed. Then He said to me: "Dismount and pray." So I prayed. Then He said to me: "Do you know where you prayed?" I said: "No." He said: "You prayed at Mount Sinai (طور سيناء), where Allah spoke to Moses, speaking to him (تَكْلِيماً)." Then I mounted, and we proceeded as long as Allah willed. Then He said to me: "Dismount and pray." So I dismounted and prayed. Then He said to me: "Do you know where you prayed?" I said: "No." He said: "You prayed at Bethlehem (بيت لحم), and Bethlehem is near Jerusalem (بيت المقدس), where Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon him) was born.

Then I mounted, and we proceeded until we reached Jerusalem (بيت المقدس). I tied the Buraq (البراق) to the ring where the Prophets (الأنبياء) used to tie [their mounts]. I entered the Mosque, with Gabriel (جبرئيل) beside me. We found Abraham, Moses, and Jesus (peace be upon them) among those Prophets of Allah whom He willed. They had been gathered for me. The prayer (الصلوة) was established, and I had no doubt that Gabriel would lead us. But when they stood in rows, Gabriel took hold of my upper arm and put me forward, and I led them in prayer, and there is no boast in that.

Then the keeper (الخازن) brought me three vessels: a vessel containing milk (لبن), a vessel containing water (ماء), and a vessel containing wine (خمر). I heard a speaker saying: "If he takes the water, he will drown, and his Ummah (أمّته) will drown. If he takes the wine, he will go astray, and his Ummah will go astray. If he takes the milk, he will be guided, and his Ummah will be guided." He said: So I took the milk and drank from it. Gabriel said to me: "You have been guided, and your Ummah has been guided."

Then he said to me: "What did you see during your journey (مَسيرك)?" I said: "A caller called out to me from my right." He said to me: "Did you answer him?" I said: "No, and I did not turn toward him." He said: "That was the caller of the Jews. Had you answered him, your Ummah would have become Jewish after you." Then he said: "What else did you see?" I said: "A caller called out to me from my left." He said to me: "Did you answer him?" I said: "No, and I did not turn toward him." He said: "That was the caller of the Christians. Had you answered him, your Ummah would have become Christian after you."

Then he said: "What met you?" I said: "I met a woman uncovering her forearms, adorned with all the adornments of the world (الدنيا). She said: 'O Muhammad, wait for me so that I may speak to you.'" He said: "Did you speak to her?" I said: "I did not speak to her, nor did I turn toward her." He said: "That was the world (الدنيا). Had you spoken to her, your Ummah would have chosen the world (الدنيا) over the Hereafter (الآخِرة)."

Then I heard a sound that startled me. Gabriel said to me: "Do you hear, O Muhammad?" I said: "Yes." He said: "This is a rock that was thrown onto the edge (شفير) of Hell (جهنم) seventy years ago, and this is the moment it settled." They said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not laugh until He was taken [in death].

He said: Then Gabriel ascended, and I ascended with him to the nearest heaven (السّماءِ الدنيا). Over it was an angel called Isma'il (اسمعيل), and he is the one responsible for the snatching (الخطفة) about which Allah the Almighty said: إِلَّا مَنْ خَطِفَ الْخَطْفَةَ فَأَتْبَعَهُ شِهابٌ ثاقِبٌ (18) except him who snatches a snatch, whereat a piercing flame pursues him. Under him were seventy thousand angels, and under every angel were seventy thousand angels. He said: "O Gabriel, who is this with you?" He said: "Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." He said: "Has he been sent?" He said: "Yes." Then he opened the gate. I greeted him, and he greeted me, and I sought forgiveness for him, and he sought forgiveness for me, and he said: "Welcome to the righteous brother and the righteous Prophet." The angels received me until I entered the nearest heaven. I did not meet any angel except that he was laughing and joyful, until I met an angel among the angels whose creation I had never seen greater than his, repulsive in appearance, manifest in anger. He offered me the same supplication (الدعاء) as they did, except that he did not laugh, and I did not see in him the joy (الاستبشار) that I saw in those angels who laughed.

So I said: "Who is this, O Gabriel, for I have been startled by him?" He said to me: "It is right that you should be startled by him, for all of us are startled by him. This is Malik (مالك), the keeper (خازن) of the Fire (النّار). He has never laughed, and ever since Allah entrusted him with Hell (جهنّم), he increases every day in anger and rage against the enemies of Allah and the people of His disobedience, and Allah takes vengeance upon them through him. Had he laughed at anyone before you, or were he to laugh at anyone after you, he would have laughed at you, but He does not laugh."

So I greeted him, and he returned the greeting to me and gave me glad tidings of Paradise (الجنّة). I said to Gabriel—and Gabriel is in the position that Allah described: مُطاعٍ ثَمَّ أَمِينٍ (21) obeyed there, and trustworthy— "Will you not command him to show me the Fire (النّار)?" So Gabriel said to him: "O Malik, show Muhammad the Fire." So he uncovered a covering from it and opened a gate from it. A blazing flame came out of it, soaring into the sky. It boiled and rose so high that I thought it would seize me because of what I saw. I said: "O Gabriel, tell him to return its covering to it." So he commanded it, saying: "Return." And it returned to the place from which it had emerged.

Then I proceeded and saw a man, dark-skinned (آدماً) and large-bodied (جسيماً). I said: "Who is this, O Gabriel?" He said: "This is your father Adam (peace be upon him)." And behold, his progeny (ذرّيته) were being presented to him, and he would say: "A good soul (روح طيب) and a good scent (ريح طيّبة) from a good body." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم recited Surah al-Mutaffifin (The Cheaters), starting from the seventeenth verse: كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتابَ الْأَبْرارِ لَفِي عِلِّيِّينَ (18) وَما أَدْراكَ ما عِلِّيُّونَ (19) كِتابٌ مَرْقُومٌ (20) يَشْهَدُهُ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ (21) (18) No indeed! The Book of the Pious is indeed in ‘Illiyyun. (19) And what will show you what ‘Illiyyun is? (20) It is a marked Book, (21) witnessed by the Hallowed [angels]. to the end of it. He said: So I greeted my father Adam, and he greeted me, and I sought forgiveness for him, and he sought forgiveness for me, and he said: "Welcome to the righteous son, the righteous Prophet, and the one sent in the righteous time (الزَمَن الصالح)."

Then I passed by an angel sitting on a seat, and behold, the entire world (الدّنيا) was between his knees, and behold, in his hand was a tablet (لوح) of light, in which he was looking at a writing (كتاباً) inscribed therein. He did not turn right or left, focusing on it with the appearance of one who is sorrowful (الحزين). I said: "Who is this, O Gabriel?" He said: "This is the Angel of Death (ملك الموت), constantly engaged in seizing souls (قبض الأرواح)." I said: "O Gabriel, bring me near him so that I may speak to him." So he brought me near him. I greeted him, and Gabriel said to him: "This is the Prophet of Mercy (نبيّ الرّحمة) whom Allah sent to His servants." So he welcomed me and greeted me with peace, and said: "Receive glad tidings, O Muhammad, for I see all goodness in your Ummah." I said: "All praise belongs to Allah, the Bestower (المنّان), the Possessor of Favors (النِّعم) upon His servants. That is from the bounty of my Lord and His mercy upon me." Gabriel said: "He is the most intensely working of the angels." I said: "Does he seize the soul of everyone who has died or will die hereafter?" He said: "Yes." I said: "And does he see them wherever they are, and witness them himself?" He said: "Yes."

The Angel of Death (ملك الموت) said: "The entire world (الدّنيا), in what Allah has subjected it to me and empowered me over it, is to me nothing but like a dirham (الدّرهم) in a man's palm, which he turns over however he wills. There is no dwelling except that I examine it five times every day, and I say when the people of the house weep over their dead: 'Do not weep over him, for I have a return visit to you, and another return visit, until none of you remains.'" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Death is sufficient as a calamity (طامّة) (1), O Gabriel." Gabriel said: "Indeed, what comes after death is a greater and greater calamity than death."

He said: Then I proceeded, and behold, I was with a people before whom were tables laden with good meat (لحمٍ طيّب) and foul meat (لحم خبيث). They were eating the foul meat and leaving the good meat. I said: "Who are these, O Gabriel?" He said: "These are those who consume the unlawful (الحرام) and abandon the lawful (الحلال), and they are from your Ummah, O Muhammad." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: Then I saw an angel among the angels whose affair Allah made wondrous: half of his body was fire (النار) and the other half was snow (ثلجاً). Neither did the fire melt the snow, nor did the snow extinguish the fire. He was calling out with a loud voice, saying: "Glory be to the One Who restrained the heat of this fire so that it does not melt the snow, and restrained the cold of this snow so that it does not extinguish the heat of this fire. O Allah, [O Composer (يا مؤلّفاً), variant reading] between the snow and the fire, unite the hearts of Your believing servants." I said: "Who is this, O Gabriel?" He said: "This is an angel whom Allah has entrusted with the sides (أكناف) (2) of the heaven and the edges (أطراف) of the earth. He is the most sincere of Allah's angels toward the inhabitants of the earth among His believing servants, supplicating for them with what you hear from him since he was created. And two angels call out in the heaven, one of them saying: 'O Allah, grant every spender a replacement (خلفاً) (3),' and the other saying: 'O Allah, grant every withholder destruction (تلفاً).'"

Then I proceeded, and behold, I was with a people who had lips (مشافر) (1) like the lips of camels. Flesh was being cut from their sides and thrown into their mouths. I said: "Who are these, O Gabriel?" He said: "These are the slanderers (الهمّازون) and the backbiters (اللمّازون)." Then I proceeded, and behold, I was with a people whose heads were being crushed (يرضخ) (2) with rocks. I said: "Who are these, O Gabriel?" He said: "These are those who sleep through the Isha prayer (صَلوة العشاء)."

Then I proceeded, and behold, I was with a people into whose mouths fire was being thrown, and it exited from their rear ends. I said: "Who are these, O Gabriel?" He said: "These are: الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوالَ الْيَتامى ظُلْماً إِنَّما يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ ناراً وَسَيَصْلَوْنَ سَعِيراً (10) Indeed, those who consume the property of orphans unjustly, are only consuming fire in their bellies, and they will soon enter a blaze.

Then I proceeded, and behold, I was with a people, one of whom wanted to stand up but could not due to the size of his belly. I said: "Who are these, O Gabriel?" He said: "These are: الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبا لا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَما يَقُومُ الَّذِي يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطانُ مِنَ الْمَسِ (275) Those who consume usury will not stand [from the dead] except as stands the one whom Satan has confounded with a touch. And behold, they were on the path of Pharaoh's folk, being exposed to the Fire morning and evening, saying: 'Our Lord, when will You establish the Hour (الساعة)?'"

He said: Then I proceeded, and behold, I was with women suspended by their breasts (بثُديهنّ) (3). I said: "Who are these, O Gabriel?" He said: "These are those who bequeath the wealth of their husbands to children who are not theirs." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The wrath of Allah is severe upon a woman who introduces into a people's lineage someone who is not from them, thereby exposing their private matters (عوراتهم) and consuming their treasures (خزائنهم)."

Then he said: We passed by angels of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, whom Allah created however He willed and shaped their faces however He willed. There was nothing in the layers (أطباق) of their bodies except that it glorified Allah and praised Him from every direction with different voices. Their voices were raised in praise (التحميد) and weeping out of fear (خشية) of Allah. I asked Gabriel about them, and he said: "They were created as you see. The angel among them next to his companion has never spoken to him, nor have they raised their heads to what is above them, nor lowered them to what is beneath them, out of fear of Allah and humility (خشوعاً)." So I greeted them, and they returned the greeting to me by nodding their heads, not looking at me out of humility. Gabriel said to them: "This is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the Prophet of Mercy (نبيّ الرّحمة). Allah sent Him to His servants as a Messenger and a Prophet, and He is the Seal of Prophethood (خاتم النّبوّة) and their Master. Will you not speak to Him?" He said: When they heard that from Gabriel, they turned to me with greetings of peace, honored me, and gave me glad tidings of goodness for me and for my Ummah.

He said: Then we ascended to the second heaven, and behold, there were two men resembling each other. I said: "Who are these, O Gabriel?" He said: "The two cousins, John (يحيى) and Jesus (عيسى)." I greeted them, and they greeted me, and I sought forgiveness for them, and they sought forgiveness for me, and they said: "Welcome to the righteous brother and the righteous Prophet." And behold, there were angels therein, upon whom was humility, and Allah had shaped their faces however He willed. There was no angel among them except that he glorified Allah and praised Him with different voices.

Then we ascended to the third heaven, and behold, there was a man whose beauty surpassed the rest of creation like the superiority of the night of the full moon over the rest of the stars. I said: "Who is this, O Gabriel?" He said: "This is your brother Joseph (يوسف)." I greeted him, and he greeted me, and I sought forgiveness for him, and he sought forgiveness for me, and he said: "Welcome to the righteous brother, the righteous Prophet, and the one sent in the righteous time (الزمن الصالح)." And behold, there were angels therein, possessing humility like what was described in the first heaven and the second heaven. Gabriel spoke to them concerning my matter what he had said to the others, and they did for me what the others had done.

Then we ascended to the fourth heaven, and behold, there was a man. I said: "Who is this, O Gabriel?" He said: "This is Idris (إدريس), whom Allah raised to a high station (مَكاناً عَلِيًّا)." I greeted him, and he greeted me, and I sought forgiveness for him, and he sought forgiveness for me. And behold, there were angels therein, upon whom was humility, like what was in the [other] heavens, and they gave me glad tidings of goodness for me and for my Ummah.

Then I saw an angel sitting on a throne, under his command were seventy thousand angels, and under every angel were seventy thousand angels. It occurred to the mind of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that he was [the one intended for leadership], so Gabriel shouted at him, saying: "Stand up!" So he is standing until the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).

Then we ascended to the fifth heaven, and behold, there was a man, an elderly man (كهل), large-eyed. I had never seen an elderly man more elderly than him. Around him was a group (ثلّة) of his Ummah, and their large number impressed me. I said: "Who is this, O Gabriel?" He said: "This is the Responder (المجيب) to his people, Aaron son of Amram (هرون بن عمران)." I greeted him, and he greeted me, and I sought forgiveness for him, and he sought forgiveness for me. And behold, there were angels therein, upon whom was humility, like what was in the [other] heavens.

Then we ascended to the sixth heaven, and behold, there was a man, dark-skinned (آدم), tall, as if he were made of hair; even if he wore two shirts, his hair would penetrate them. I heard him saying: "The Children of Israel (بنو إسرائيل) claim that I am the most honored of Adam's children before Allah, yet this man is more honored before Allah than I am." I said: "Who is this, O Gabriel?" He said: "Your brother Moses son of Amram (موسى بن عمران)." I greeted him, and he greeted me, and I sought forgiveness for him, and he sought forgiveness for me. And behold, there were angels therein, upon whom was humility, like what was in the [other] heavens.

He said: Then we ascended to the seventh heaven. I did not pass by any angel except that they said: "O Muhammad, perform cupping (احتجم) and command your Ummah to perform cupping (الحجامة)." And behold, there was a man whose head and beard were mixed with white hair (أشمط) (1), sitting on a chair. I said: "O Gabriel, who is this who is in the seventh heaven, at the gate of al-Bayt al-Ma'mur (بيتِ المعمور), in the proximity (جوار) of Allah?" He said: "O Muhammad, this is your father Abraham (peace be upon him). And this is your place and the place of those among your Ummah who are pious." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم recited: إِنَّ أَوْلَى النَّاسِ بِإِبْراهِيمَ لَلَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ وَهذَا النَّبِيُّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَاللهُ وَلِيُّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (68) Indeed, the nearest people to Abraham are those who follow him, and this Prophet and those who have faith, and Allah is the guardian of the faithful. I greeted him, and he greeted me, and he said: "Welcome to the righteous Prophet, the righteous son, and the one sent in the righteous time (الزمن الصالح)." And behold, there were angels therein, upon whom was humility, like what was in the [other] heavens, and they gave me glad tidings of goodness for me and for my Ummah.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: And I saw in the seventh heaven seas of light (نور) glittering, whose glitter almost snatched away the sight. And in it were dark seas and seas of snow (ثلج) that thundered. When I finished and saw these, I asked Gabriel, and he said: "Receive glad tidings, O Muhammad, and be grateful for the honor (كرامة) of your Lord, and thank Allah for what He has done for you." He said: So Allah strengthened me with His power and His help, until my speech to Gabriel became frequent, and it pleased me. Gabriel said: "O Muhammad, you magnify what you see. This is only a creation among the creations of your Lord. Indeed, between Allah and His creation are ninety thousand veils (حجاب). The closest of creation to Allah are I and Israfil (إسرافيل), and between us and Him are four veils: a veil of light (نور), a veil of darkness (ظلمة), a veil of cloud (الغمام), and a veil of water (ماء)."

He said: And I saw among the wonders that Allah created and subjected as He willed, a rooster (ديكاً) whose feet were in the depths (تخوم) of the seventh earth, and its head was near the Throne (العرش). And an angel among the angels of Allah the Almighty, whom He created as He willed, whose feet were in the depths of the seventh earth. Then he proceeded ascending until he emerged into the air toward the seventh heaven, and he continued ascending therein until his horn (قرنه) reached near the Throne. He was saying: "Glory be to my Lord, wherever I am, you do not know where your Lord is, due to the greatness of His status." And he has two wings on his shoulder; when he spreads them, they span beyond the East and the West. When it is dawn (السّحر), he spreads his two wings, flaps (وخفق) (2) them, and cries out (وصرخ) (3) with glorification (التسبيح), saying: "Glory be to Allah, the Sovereign (الملك), the Holy (القدّوس). Glory be to Allah, the Great (الكبير), the Most High (المتعال). There is no god but Allah, the Living (الحيّ), the Self-subsisting (القيّوم)."

And when he says that, all the roosters of the earth glorify [Allah], flap their wings, and begin to crow (بالصراخ). When that rooster in the heaven falls silent, all the roosters of the earth fall silent. That rooster has green down (زَغَب) (1) and white feathers, whiter than anything I have ever seen, and it also has green down under its white feathers, greener than anything I have ever seen.

He said: Then I proceeded with Gabriel and entered al-Bayt al-Ma'mur (البيت المعمور). I prayed two rak'ahs therein, and with me were people from my companions, some wearing new clothes (ثياب جدد) and others wearing worn-out clothes (ثياب خلقان) (2). The wearers of the new clothes entered, and the wearers of the worn-out clothes were held back. Then I exited, and two rivers were made subservient to me: a river called al-Kawthar (الكوثر) and a river called al-Rahmah (الرحمة). I drank from al-Kawthar and washed myself with al-Rahmah. Then both were made subservient to me until I entered Paradise (الجنّة). And behold, on its banks (حافتيها) (3) were my houses and the houses of my wives. And behold, its soil was like musk (المسك). And behold, a young woman (جارية) was immersing herself in the rivers of Paradise. I said: "To whom do you belong, O young woman?" She said: "To Zayd ibn Harithah (لزيد بن حارثة)." So I gave him the glad tidings of her when morning came. And behold, its birds were like the Bactrian camels (كالبُخت) (4), and behold, its pomegranates were like buckets (الدّلي).

(٥) ...the great ones. And behold, there was a tree such that if a bird were sent into its base, it would not circle it for seven hundred years, and there is no dwelling in Paradise (الجنّة) except that it has a branch from it. So I said: "What is this, O جبرئيل?" He said: "This is the tree طوبى." The Almighty said: طُوبى لَهُمْ وَحُسْنُ مَآبٍ ** (29) Blessedness is for them and a good destination. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: When I entered Paradise (الجنّة), I returned to myself and asked جبرئيل about those seas, their nature (هُوِيَّتُهَا), and their wonders. He said: "They are the curtains (سرادقات) of the veils (الحجب) with which Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, veiled Himself. Were it not for those veils, the light of the Throne (العرش) and everything in it would have been torn apart." Then I reached the Lote-Tree of the Utmost Boundary (سدرة المنتهى), and behold, a single leaf from it shaded an entire nation (أمّة) from among the nations. I was near it, as the Almighty said: قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنى ** (9) two bows' length or nearer. Then He called out to me: آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ رَبِّهِ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ ** (285) The Apostle has faith in what has been sent down to him from his Lord, and the faithful [as well]. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: We have already written that in Surat al-Baqarah. I say: We have already transmitted it from him there. He said: Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Lord, You have granted Your prophets virtues (فَضَائِل), so grant me [some]." Allah said: "I have already granted you, among what I have granted you, two phrases from beneath My Throne (عرشي): لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ (There is no might and no power except with Allah) and وَلَا مَنْجَى مِنْكَ إِلَّا إِلَيْكَ (There is no refuge from You except to You)." He said: And the angels taught me a saying (قولاً) that I should recite when I wake up in the morning and when I reach the evening: "O Allah, verily my injustice (ظُلْمِي) has woken up seeking refuge in Your pardon (عَفْوِكَ), and my sin (ذَنْبِي) has woken up seeking refuge in Your forgiveness (مَغْفِرَتِكَ), and my humiliation (ذُلِّي) has woken up seeking refuge in Your Might (عِزِّكَ), and my poverty (فَقْرِي) has woken up seeking refuge in Your Wealth (غِنَاكَ), and my worn-out face (وَجْهِي الْبَالِي) has woken up seeking refuge in Your Everlasting Face (وَجْهِكَ الْبَاقِي) which does not perish." And I recite that when I reach the evening. Then I heard the Adhan (الأذان), and behold, an angel was calling the Adhan who had not been seen in the heavens before that night. He said: الله أكبر الله أكبر (Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest). Allah said: "My servant has spoken the truth; I am the Greatest." He said: أشهد أن لا إله إلّا الله أشهد أن لا إله إلّا الله (I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, I bear witness that there is no god but Allah). He said: "My servant has spoken the truth; I am Allah, there is no god other than Me." He said: أشهد أنّ محمّداً رسول الله أشهد أن محمّداً رسول الله (I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah). He said: "My servant has spoken the truth; verily Muhammad is My servant and My Messenger. I sent him and chose him." He said: حيّ على الصّلوة حيّ على الصّلوة (Hasten to the prayer, hasten to the prayer). He said: "My servant has spoken the truth; he has called to My obligatory duty (فريضة). Whoever walks towards it desiring it and seeking reward [from Me], it will be an expiation for his past sins." He said: حيّ على الفلاح حيّ على الفلاح (Hasten to success, hasten to success). Allah said: "It is righteousness (الصّلاح), deliverance (النّجاح), and success (الفلاح)." Then I led the angels in prayer (أمّمت) in the heaven, just as I had led the prophets in prayer in Jerusalem (بيت المقدس). He said: Then a state of overwhelming awe (صبابة) overcame me, and I fell down prostrating. My Lord called out to me: "I have obligated fifty prayers upon every prophet who was before you, and I have obligated them upon you and upon your nation (أمّتك). So establish them, you among your nation." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: So I descended until I passed by Abraham (peace be upon him), and he did not ask me about anything, until I reached Moses (peace be upon him). He said: "What have you done, O Muhammad?" I said: "My Lord said: 'I have obligated fifty prayers upon every prophet who was before you, and I have obligated them upon you and upon your nation.'" Moses said: "O Muhammad, verily your nation is the last of the nations and the weakest of them, and verily your Lord will not reject anything you ask of Him, but your nation will not be able to perform them. Return to your Lord and ask Him for alleviation (التخفيف) for your nation." So I returned to my Lord until I reached the Lote-Tree of the Utmost Boundary (سدرة المنتهى), and I fell down prostrating. Then I said: "You have obligated fifty prayers upon me and upon my nation, and neither I nor my nation can bear that, so lighten the burden for me." So He removed ten from me. I returned to Moses (peace be upon him) and informed him. He said: "Return to your Lord; you cannot bear it." I returned to my Lord, and He removed ten from me. I returned to Moses (peace be upon him) and informed him. He said: "Return." And in every return to Him, I fell down prostrating, until it was reduced to ten prayers. I returned to Moses and informed him. He said: "You cannot bear it." I returned to my Lord, and He removed five from me. I returned to Moses and informed him. He said: "You cannot bear it." I said: "I have become ashamed before my Lord, but I will endure it." Then a caller (مناد) called out to me: "Since you have endured it, these five are [counted as] fifty; every prayer is [counted as] ten. And whoever among your nation intends a good deed (حَسَنَة) and performs it, ten [rewards] will be written for him; and if he does not perform it, one will be written for him. And whoever among your nation intends an evil deed (سيئة) and performs it, one [sin] will be written for him; and if he does not perform it, I will not write anything against him." Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: May Allah reward Moses well on behalf of this nation. This is the interpretation (تفسير) of the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: سُبْحانَ الَّذِي أَسْرى بِعَبْدِهِ ** (1) Immaculate is He who carried His servant on a night journey ...the Ayah (الآية). In al-Majalis, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was carried on the Night Journey (أُسْرِيَ) to Jerusalem (بيت المقدس), جبرئيل carried Him upon the البراق. They came to Jerusalem, and the prayer niches (محاريب) of the prophets were shown to Him, and He prayed in them. Then He was returned. On His return, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم passed by a caravan (عير) (1) belonging to Quraysh. They had water in vessels, and they had lost a camel (بعيراً) and were searching for it. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم drank from that water and poured out the rest of it. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم awoke in the morning, He said to Quraysh: "Verily, Allah the Exalted carried me on a Night Journey to Jerusalem and showed me the relics (آثار) and dwellings (منازل) of the prophets. And I passed by a caravan in such-and-such a place, and they had lost a camel. I drank from their water and poured out the rest of it." Abu Jahl said: "The opportunity has been granted to you, so ask him how many pillars (الأساطين) and lamps (القناديل) are in it." So they said: "O Muhammad, there are those here who have entered Jerusalem, so describe to us how many pillars, lamps, and prayer niches it has." جبرئيل came and suspended an image (صورة) of Jerusalem directly in front (تجاه) (2) of His face, and He began to inform them of whatever they asked Him about. When He informed them, they said: "Wait until the caravan arrives, and we will ask them about what you have said." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "The confirmation of this is that the caravan will appear before you with the rising of the sun, led by a grayish-white camel (جمل أورق) (3)." When the next day came, they approached, looking towards the mountain pass (العقبة) and saying: "The sun is about to rise now." While they were thus, the caravan appeared before them just as the disk [of the sun] rose, led by a grayish-white camel. They asked them about what the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had said. They replied: "Indeed, a camel of ours was lost in such-and-such a place, and we placed water [there], and when we woke up in the morning, the water had been poured out." This only increased them in arrogance (عتوّاً). And Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated something close to it. In Kashf al-Ghummah, narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked: "In what language did your Lord address you on the Night of the Ascension (المعراج)?" He said: "He addressed me in the language of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). I was inspired to say: 'O Lord, did You address me or Ali?'" He said: "O Ahmad, I am a thing (شيء) unlike [other] things, I am not measured against people, nor am I described by things. I created you from My Light (نوري) and I created Ali from Your Light (نورك). I looked into the secrets of your heart and found no one dearer to your heart than Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), so I addressed you in his tongue so that your heart might be reassured." The narratives concerning the story of the Ascension (المعراج) are numerous. Whoever desires them should seek them in their proper places. In them are secrets (أسرار) that none can discover except those firmly grounded in knowledge (الرّاسخون في العِلمِ). (2) وَآتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ ** (1) (2) We gave Moses the Book, وَجَعَلْناهُ هُدىً لِبَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ أَلَّا تَتَّخِذُوا ** (2) and made it a guidance for the Children of Israel: ‘Do not take And it was recited with the ya (بالياءِ). مِنْ دُونِي وَكِيلاً ** any guardian besides Me.’ —a Lord (ربّاً) to Whom you entrust your affairs. (3) ذُرِّيَّةَ مَنْ حَمَلْنا مَعَ نُوحٍ ** (3) (3) O descendants of those whom We carried with Noah! It is in the accusative case (نصبه) due to specification (الاختصاص) or vocative address (النّداءِ). إِنَّهُ كانَ عَبْداً شَكُوراً ** Indeed, he was a grateful servant. —one who was very grateful (كثير الشكر). In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked what was meant by His saying: إِنَّهُ كانَ عَبْداً شَكُوراً ** (Indeed, he was a grateful servant). He said: "Phrases (كلمات) in which he was thorough." He was asked: "And what are they?" He said: "When he woke up in the morning, he would say: 'I have woken up, I call You to witness that whatever blessing (نعمة) or well-being (عافية) I have woken up with, concerning religion (دين) or the world (دنيا), it is from You alone, You have no partner (شريكَ لَكَ). So all praise (الحمد) is due to You for that, and abundant thanks (الشكر كثيراً) are due to You.' He would say this three times when he woke up in the morning and three times when he reached the evening." In al-Faqih, al-Ilal, al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi: [There is a narration] close to this, with a variation in the wording of the remembrance (الذكر) and its number. (4) وَقَضَيْنا إِلى بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ ** (4) We decreed in the Book for the Children of Israel: —And We revealed to them a decreed, decisive revelation (وحياً مقضيّاً مبتوتاً) الْكِتابِ ** —in the Torah (التّوراة) لَتُفْسِدُنَ ** (1) فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَّتَيْنِ ** ‘You will surely cause corruption in the land twice, —two acts of corruption (إفسادتين) وَلَتَعْلُنَّ عُلُوًّا كَبِيراً ** (2). and you will surely perpetrate great tyranny.’ (5) فَإِذا جاءَ وَعْدُ أُولاهُما ** (5) So when the occasion for the first of the two came, —the promise of punishment for the first of the two [corruptions] بَعَثْنا عَلَيْكُمْ عِباداً لَنا. We aroused against you servants of Ours, In al-Jawami', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): He recited عَبيداً لَنَا (slaves of Ours). أُولِي بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ ** possessing great might, —possessors of severe strength (قوّة) and assault (بطش) in war. فَجاسُوا ** who ravaged —they moved about (تردّدوا) seeking you خِلالَ الدِّيارِ ** the habitations. —in the midst (وسطها) of the dwellings for killing, raiding (الغارة), and taking captive (السّبي). وَكانَ وَعْداً مَفْعُولاً ** It was a promise bound to be fulfilled. —And the promise of their punishment was bound to be fulfilled.

(6) ثُمَّ رَدَدْنا لَكُمُ الْكَرَّةَ ** (6) Then We gave you back the turn The dominion (الدولة) and the dominance (الغلبة) عَلَيْهِمْ ** over those whom We sent against you, وَأَمْدَدْناكُمْ بِأَمْوالٍ وَبَنِينَ وَجَعَلْناكُمْ أَكْثَرَ نَفِيراً ** and aided you with wealth and sons, and made you greater in number. than you were. And the النفير (mobilized force) is those who mobilize (يَنْفِرُ) with the man from his people, and those gathered (المجتمعُون) to go forth against the enemy.

(6) إِنْ أَحْسَنْتُمْ أَحْسَنْتُمْ لِأَنْفُسِكُمْ ** (6) If you do good, you do good to your own souls, (3) because its reward is for them (the souls), وَإِنْ أَسَأْتُمْ فَلَها ** and if you do evil, it is against them. then its consequence (وبالها) is upon them.

In al-Jawami', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): "I have not done good to anyone, nor have I done evil to him," and He recited the Ayah. It was said: The ل (lam) was mentioned only for the sake of parallelism (ازدواجاً).

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): and if you do evil, it is against them—[meaning] a Lord Who forgives.

فَإِذا جاءَ وَعْدُ الْآخِرَةِ ** When the time for the second [punishment] comes, and the contract (عقد) of the punishment of the last time [was established]—[We sent them] that they may make your faces wretched (لِيَسُوؤُا وُجُوهَكُمْ) We sent them that they may make your faces wretched (ليسوؤا وجوهكم), meaning: that they may make the traces of wretchedness (آثار المساءة) apparent upon them. So, [the phrase "We sent them"] was omitted because its mention earlier indicates it. And it was recited ليسوء (in the singular) (على التوحيد), meaning [referring to] the promise (الوعد) or the sending (البعث), and [it was also recited] with the ن (بالنّون). وَلِيَدْخُلُوا الْمَسْجِدَ ** and enter the Mosque (4) كَما دَخَلُوهُ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ as they entered it the first time, وَلِيُتَبِّرُوا ** and that they may destroy (وليهلِكوا) ما عَلَوْا what they overcame (ما غلبوه) and seized control of (واستولوا عليه), or the duration of their dominance (مدّة علوّهم) تَتْبِيراً. and utterly destroy whatever they came upon.**

(1) Meaning, truly, without a doubt, your successors will cause corruption in the lands where they dwell twice, and that is Jerusalem (بيت المقدس). By corruption (الفساد), He meant oppression (الظلم), taking wealth, killing the prophets (الأنبياء), and shedding blood (سفك الدّماء). M.N.

(2) Meaning, and you will surely act arrogantly (ولتستكبرنّ) and oppress people with great oppression (ظلماً عظيماً). The term العلوّ (exaltation/arrogance) here is similar to العتوّ (transgression), which is daringness against Allah, the Almighty (الله تعالى), and exposing oneself to His wrath (سخطه). M.N.

(3) Its meaning is that if you do good in your sayings and actions, the benefit of your goodness returns to you, and its reward reaches you. You will be granted victory over your enemies in this world (الدّنيا) and rewarded in the Hereafter (العقبى). M.N.

(4) Meaning, Jerusalem (بيت المقدس) and its surrounding areas. So, the Mosque (المسجد), which is المسجد الأقصى (the Farthest Mosque), is used metaphorically (كنّى) for the city, just as المسجد الحرام (the Sacred Mosque) is used metaphorically for the Sacred Precinct (الحرم). Its meaning is that they may seize control of the city, because they cannot enter the Mosque except after seizing control. M.N.

(7) If he ceases, [well and good], otherwise, we shall deal with him with the decisive sword. Abu Jahl said: "Then who will undertake speaking to him and arguing with him?" Abdullah ibn Abi Umayyah al-Makhzumi said: "I am [ready] for that. Do you not approve of me as a worthy (8) opponent (9) and a competent debater for him?" Abu Jahl said: "Yes." So they all came to him, and Abdullah ibn Abi Umayyah began, saying: "O Muhammad, you have made a tremendous claim and spoken a terrifying word. You allege that you are the Messenger of the Lord of the worlds. It is not fitting for the Lord of the worlds and the Creator of all creation that a messenger for Him should be like you—a human being like us, who eats as we eat and walks in the markets as we walk. This is the king of Rome, and this is the king of Persia; neither of them sends a messenger unless he is wealthy, of great standing, possessing palaces, houses, tents (فساطيط), pavilions, slaves, and servants. And the Lord of the worlds is above all of these; they are His servants. If you were a prophet, there would be an angel with you to confirm you, whom we could witness. Rather, if Allah intended to send a prophet to us, He would only send an angel to us, not a human being like us. You, O Muhammad, are nothing but a man bewitched, and you are not a prophet."

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Is there anything remaining of your speech?" He said: "Yes. If Allah intended to send a messenger to us, He would have sent the wealthiest among us and the one in the best condition. Why was this Qur’an, which you claim Allah sent down upon you and appointed you as a messenger with it, not sent down عَلى رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْقَرْيَتَيْنِ عَظِيمٍ (upon a great man of the two cities)?—either Al-Walid ibn Al-Mughirah in Mecca or Urwah ibn Mas'ud Ath-Thaqafi in Ta'if." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Is there anything remaining of your speech?" He said: "Yes. لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى تَفْجُرَ لَنا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ يَنْبُوعاً (We will not believe you until you make a spring gush forth for us from the earth) in this Mecca, for it is full of rough (1) stones and mountains that conceal (2) its ground and excavate it, and make springs flow in it, for we are in need of that. أَوْ تَكُونَ لَكَ جَنَّةٌ مِنْ نَخِيلٍ وَعِنَبٍ (or you have a garden of date palms and vines) so that you may eat from it and feed us, فَتُفَجِّرَ الْأَنْهارَ خِلالَها تَفْجِيراً (and make rivers gush forth in its midst, gushing forth). أَوْ تُسْقِطَ السَّماءَ كَما زَعَمْتَ عَلَيْنا كِسَفاً (or make the sky fall upon us in pieces, as you claim), for you told us: وَإِنْ يَرَوْا كِسْفاً مِنَ السَّماءِ سَاقِطاً يَقُولُوا سَحَابٌ مَرْكُومٌ (And if they see a fragment of the sky falling, they say, 'A mass of clouds'). Perhaps we would say that. Then he said: أَوْ تَأْتِيَ بِاللهِ وَالْمَلائِكَةِ قَبِيلاً (or bring Allah and the angels face to face), meaning, you bring Him and them, and they are confronting us. أَوْ يَكُونَ لَكَ بَيْتٌ مِنْ زُخْرُفٍ (or you have a house of gold) from which you give us and enrich us, for perhaps we would transgress, since you told us: كَلَّا إِنَّ الْإِنْسَانَ لَيَطْغَى أَنْ رَآهُ اسْتَغْنَى (No indeed! Man surely is rebellious when he considers himself self-sufficient). Then he said: أَوْ تَرْقى فِي السَّماءِ (or you ascend into the sky), meaning, you climb into the sky. وَلَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لِرُقِيِّكَ (And we will not believe in your ascension) in your climbing حَتَّى تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنَا كِتَاباً نَقْرَؤُهُ (until you bring down to us a book that we may read) from Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise, addressed to Abdullah ibn Abi Umayyah al-Makhzumi and those with him, [stating] that they should believe in Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib, for He is My Messenger, and confirm his statement, for it is from Me. Then, O Muhammad, I do not know whether, if you did all this, I would believe in you or not believe in you. Rather, even if you raised us to the sky and opened its gates and admitted us into it, we would say: إِنَّمَا سُكِّرَتْ أَبْصَارُنَا (Our eyes have only been dazzled) and we have been bewitched."

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Is there anything remaining of your speech, O Abdullah?" He said: "Is what I have presented to you not sufficient and conclusive? Nothing remains. Say what occurs to you and express yourself clearly if you have an argument, and bring us what we have asked of you." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Allah, You are the Hearer of every sound and the Knower of everything. You know what Your servants have said." Then Allah sent down upon Him: مَا لِهَذَا الرَّسُولِ يَأْكُلُ الطَّعَامَ وَيَمْشِي فِي الْأَسْوَاقِ (What sort of a messenger is this who eats food and walks in the markets?) up to His saying: قُصُوراً (palaces). And He sent down to him, O Muhammad: فَلَعَلَّكَ تَارِكٌ بَعْضَ مَا يُوحَى إِلَيْكَ وَضَائِقٌ بِهِ صَدْرُكَ (Perhaps you will omit some of what is revealed to you, and your breast will be constrained by it) the Ayah. And He sent down to him, O Muhammad: وَقَالُوا لَوْ لَا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ وَلَوْ أَنْزَلْنَا مَلَكاً لَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ (They say, 'Why has not an angel been sent down to him?' Had We sent down an angel, the matter would have been decided) the Ayah. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "As for your mention that I eat food as you eat," and He continued the narration as it will come in Surat Al-Furqan, if Allah wills. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "As for your saying that this is the king of Rome and this is the king of Persia, neither of them sends a messenger unless he is wealthy, of great standing, possessing palaces, houses, tents (فساطيط), pavilions, slaves, and servants, and the Lord of the worlds is above all of these, and they are His servants—verily, Allah possesses the governance (التدبير) and the judgment (الحكم). He does not act according to your assumption, nor your estimation, nor your suggestion. Rather, He does what He wills and يَحْكُمُ مَا يُرِيدُ (He decrees what He desires), and He is Praised. O Abdullah, Allah only sent His Prophet to teach people their religion and to call them to their Lord, and to exert himself in this throughout the hours of the night and day. If He were the owner of palaces in which He secluded Himself, and slaves and servants who concealed Him from the people, would the message not be lost and the affairs delayed? Do you not see how, when kings seclude themselves, vile deeds and corruption occur without their knowledge or awareness? O Abdullah, Allah only sent me, and I possess no wealth, so that He may make known to you His power and His strength, and that He is the Helper of His Messenger. You are unable to kill Him or prevent Him from His message. This is clearer evidence of His power and your incapacity. Soon Allah will grant me victory over you, and I shall inflict widespread killing and capture upon you. Then Allah will grant me victory over your lands, and the believers will take possession of them, excluding you and those who agree with you in your religion." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "As for your saying to me, 'If you were a prophet, there would be an angel with you to confirm you, whom we could witness,' " and He continued the narration as it passed in Surat Al-An'am, and then continued the narration with what will come in Surat Al-Furqan and Az-Zukhruf.

Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "As for your saying: لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى تَفْجُرَ لَنَا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ يَنْبُوعاً (We will not believe you until you make a spring gush forth for us from the earth) until the end of what you said—you have suggested to Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah, the Lord of the worlds, things, some of which, even if He brought them to you, would not be a proof of His prophethood. The Messenger of Allah is far above taking advantage of the ignorance of the ignorant and arguing against them with that which holds no proof. And some of which, if He brought them to you, would entail your destruction. Proofs and evidences are only brought forth to obligate the servants of Allah to believe in that which does not destroy them. You have only suggested your own destruction, and the Lord of the worlds is more merciful to His servants and more knowledgeable of their welfare than to destroy them by what they suggest. And some of which are impossible (المحال) things whose existence is neither sound nor permissible, and the Messenger of the Lord of the worlds makes this known to you, cuts off your excuses, restricts your path of opposition, and compels you by the proofs (حُجَج) of Allah to confirm Him, so that you have no escape (محيد) or refuge (محيص) from Him. And some of which you have already confessed about yourself that you are obstinate (معاند) and rebellious (متمرّد) in it, accepting no argument and listening to no proof. And whoever is like that, his remedy is the punishment of the Fire descending from His heaven, or in His Hellfire, or by the swords of His allies."

As for your saying, O Abdullah: لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى تَفْجُرَ لَنَا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ يَنْبُوعاً (We will not believe you until you make a spring gush forth for us from the earth) in this Mecca, for it is full of stones, rocks, and mountains that conceal (تكشح) its ground and excavate it, and make springs flow in it, for we are in need of that—you asked for this while you are ignorant of the proofs (دلايل) of Allah. O Abdullah, do you think that if I did this, you would become a prophet because of it? Consider Ta'if, where you have gardens (بساتين). Were there not difficult, unproductive places there that you repaired, leveled, and excavated (كشحتَها), making springs flow in them that you extracted? He said: "Yes." He said: "Do you have peers in this?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Did you and they become prophets because of that?" He said: "No." He said: "Likewise, this would not become a proof for Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding His prophethood if He did it. It is nothing more than your saying, 'We will not believe you until you stand up and walk on the earth,' or 'until you eat food as people eat.' " As for your saying, O Abdullah: أَوْ تَكُونَ لَكَ جَنَّةٌ مِنْ نَخِيلٍ وَعِنَبٍ (or you have a garden of date palms and vines) so that you may eat from it and feed us, فَتُفَجِّرَ الْأَنْهارَ خِلالَها تَفْجِيراً (and make rivers gush forth in its midst, gushing forth)—do you and your companions not have gardens (جنان) of date palms and vines in Ta'if from which you eat and feed others, and make rivers gush forth in their midst, gushing forth? Did you become prophets because of this? He said: "No." He said: "Then what is the matter with your suggesting things to the Messenger of Allah which, even if they were as you suggest, would not indicate His truthfulness? Rather, if He undertook them, His undertaking them would indicate His falsehood, because He would then be arguing with that which holds no proof, and deceiving the weak-minded away from their intellects and their religions. The Messenger of the Lord of the worlds is too glorious and exalted for this."

Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Abdullah, as for your saying: أَوْ تُسْقِطَ السَّماءَ كَمَا زَعَمْتَ عَلَيْنَا كِسَفاً (or make the sky fall upon us in pieces, as you claim)—for you said: وَإِنْ يَرَوْا كِسْفاً مِنَ السَّمَاءِ سَاقِطاً يَقُولُوا سَحَابٌ مَرْكُومٌ (And if they see a fragment of the sky falling, they say, 'A mass of clouds')—the falling of the sky upon you entails your destruction and death. You only desire by this that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم should destroy you. But the Messenger of the Lord of the worlds is more merciful to you than that, and He will not destroy you. Rather, He establishes the proofs (حجج) of Allah against you. The proofs of Allah for His Prophet are not solely according to the suggestions of His servants, because the servants are ignorant of what is permissible in terms of welfare (الصلاح) and what is not permissible of it, and of corruption (الفساد). Their suggestions may differ and contradict one another to the point that their occurrence becomes impossible (يستحيل). If it were dependent on their suggestions, it would be permissible for you to suggest that the sky fall upon you, and for someone else to suggest that the sky should not fall upon you, but rather that the earth should be raised to the sky and fall upon it. That would be contradictory and mutually exclusive, and its occurrence would be impossible. Allah does not conduct His governance (تدبيره) according to what necessitates the impossible (المحال)." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Abdullah, have you ever seen a physician whose remedy was according to the suggestions of the patients? Rather, he only does what he knows is beneficial for the patient, whether the sick person likes it or dislikes it. You are the sick, and Allah is your Physician. If you implement His remedy, He will cure you, and if you rebel against it, He will make you ill." Furthermore, O Abdullah, when have you ever seen a claimant of a right against a man, where a judge among their judges in the past obligated the evidence of his claim to be according to the suggestion of the defendant? If that were the case, no claim or right would ever be established for anyone against anyone else, and there would be no difference between an oppressor and an oppressed, or a truthful person and a liar. Then He said: "O Abdullah, as for your saying: أَوْ تَأْتِيَ بِاللهِ وَالْمَلائِكَةِ قَبِيلاً (or bring Allah and the angels face to face) confronting us and we witness them—this is one of the impossibilities (المحال) about which there is no ambiguity. Verily, My Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, is not like created beings who come and go, move, and confront something so that He can be brought. You have asked for this impossibility (المحال). This thing you have called for is only a description of your weak, deficient idols (أصنامكم) which neither hear, nor see, nor know, nor avail you or anyone else anything. O Abdullah, do you not have estates (ضياع) [and imaginary gardens (جنان خيال)] in Ta'if and property (عقار) in Mecca, and overseers (قوّام) over them?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Do you witness all their conditions yourself, or through envoys (سفراء) between you and your agents (معامليك)?" He said: "Through envoys." He said: "Consider if your agents, your tenants (أَكرَتُكَ), and your servants said to your envoys: 'We will not believe you in this mission unless you bring Abdullah ibn Abi Umayyah to us so that we may witness him [and hear] what you say about him directly (شفاهاً).' Would you permit them this, or would that be permissible for them in your view?" He said: "No." He said: "Then what is incumbent upon your envoys? Is it not that they should come to them from you with a correct sign (علامة) that indicates their truthfulness?" He said: "Yes." He said: "O Abdullah, consider your envoy: if, upon hearing this from them, he returned to you and said, 'Come with me, for they have suggested that I bring you,' would this not be an act of opposition to you, and would you not say to him, 'You are only a messenger, not an advisor or a commander'?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Then how is it that you suggest to the Messenger of the Lord of the worlds what your tenants and agents are not permitted to suggest to your envoy to them? How did you intend for the Messenger of the Lord of the worlds to incur blame upon Himself before His Lord by commanding and forbidding Him, while you do not permit such a thing for an envoy of yours to your tenants and overseers? This is a decisive argument (حجّة قاطعة) for the invalidation of everything you mentioned in all that you suggested."

As for your saying, O Abdullah: أَوْ يَكُونَ لَكَ بَيْتٌ مِنْ زُخْرُفٍ (or you have a house of gold)—which is gold—has it not reached you that the Aziz (Governor) of Egypt had houses of gold (زخرف)? He said: "Yes." He said: "Did he become a prophet because of that?" He said: "No." He said: "Likewise, this would not necessitate prophethood for Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم if He had it. And Muhammad does not take advantage of your ignorance regarding the proofs of Allah." As for your saying, O Abdullah: أَوْ تَرْقى فِي السَّماءِ (or you ascend into the sky), and then you said: وَلَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لِرُقِيِّكَ حَتَّى تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنَا كِتَاباً نَقْرَؤُهُ (And we will not believe in your ascension until you bring down to us a book that we may read)—O Abdullah, ascending to the sky is more difficult than descending from it. Since you confessed about yourself that you would not believe if I ascended, the ruling is the same for the descent. Then you said: حَتَّى تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنَا كِتَاباً نَقْرَؤُهُ (until you bring down to us a book that we may read), and after that, 'I do not know whether I will believe in you or not believe in you.' Thus, O Abdullah, you admit that you are obstinate even after the proof of Allah has been established against you. Therefore, there is no remedy for you except discipline (تأديبه) at the hands of His allies among mankind, or His angels, the Zabaniyah. And Allah has sent down to me a comprehensive word for the invalidation of everything you suggested. The Almighty said: Say, O Muhammad: سُبْحَانَ رَبِّي هَلْ كُنْتُ إِلَّا بَشَراً رَسُولاً (Immaculate is my Lord! Am I anything but a human messenger?). How far is My Lord from doing things according to what the ignorant suggest regarding what is permissible and what is not permissible! And هَلْ كُنْتُ إِلَّا بَشَراً رَسُولاً (Am I anything but a human messenger?). Nothing is incumbent upon me except establishing the proof of Allah which He granted me. It is not for me to command My Lord, nor forbid, nor suggest, so that I become like the messenger whom a king sent to a group of his opponents, and who then returned to the king commanding him to do to them what they had suggested to him.

(8) عَسى رَبُّكُمْ أَنْ يَرْحَمَكُمْ وَإِنْ عُدْتُمْ ** [meaning] another turn/time, عُدْنا ** (We shall return) [meaning] a third time to your punishment. وَجَعَلْنا جَهَنَّمَ لِلْكافِرِينَ حَصِيراً ** (and We have made hell a prison for the faithless) [meaning] a prison (محبساً) from which they will never be able to exit.

And the general populace (العامّة) interpreted the two corruptions (الافسادتين) as the killing of Zakariyya and Yahya, and the great haughtiness (العلوّ الكبير) as their arrogance against the obedience of Allah and their oppression of the people. And the servants possessing great might (العباد أولي بأس) [they interpreted as] Nebuchadnezzar (بخت نصر) and his soldiers. And the return of the turn (ردّ الكرة عليهم) [they interpreted as] the return of their captives to Syria (الشام) by Bahman ibn Isfandiyar (بَهْمَنْ بن إسفنديار) and his appointing Daniel (دانيال) over them. And the promise of the Hereafter (وعده الآخرة) [they interpreted as] Allah's subjugation of the Persians (الفرس) over them once again.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He interpreted the two corruptions (الإِفسادتين) as the killing of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) and the stabbing of al-Hasan (peace be upon him). And the great haughtiness (العلوّ الكبير) as the killing of al-Husayn (peace be upon him). And the servants possessing great might (العباد أولي بأس) as a people whom Allah will send forth before the emergence of the Qā’im (القائم), and they will not leave any avenger (وتراً) (1) for the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family) except that they kill him. And the promise of Allah [is] with the emergence of the Qā’im (peace be upon him). And the return of the turn (ردّ الكرّة عليهم) [is] with the emergence of al-Husayn (peace be upon him) among seventy of his companions, wearing gilded helmets (البيض المذهب), when the Proof (الحجة), the Qā’im, is among them.

And al-Ayyashi added: Then al-Husayn (peace be upon him) will rule them until his eyebrows fall down to his eyes.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): The first one who will return (يَكُرّ) to the world is al-Husayn ibn Ali, along with Yazid ibn Mu'awiyah and his companions. He will kill them exactly, feather for feather (حذو القذة بالقذّة). Then he recited this Ayah: ثُمَّ رَدَدْنا. (Then We returned).

And in another narration by al-Ayyashi from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The servants possessing great might (العباد أولي بأس) are the Qā’im and his companions (peace be upon them).

And al-Qummi [said regarding]: وَقَضَيْنا إِلى بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ فِي الْكِتابِ ** (We decreed for the Children of Israel in the Book) meaning: We informed them. Then the address to the Children of Israel ceased, and Allah addressed the Ummah of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) and said: لَتُفْسِدُنَّ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَّتَيْنِ ** (You will surely cause corruption in the land twice) meaning: so-and-so (فلاناً) and so-and-so (فلاناً) and their companions, and their breaking of the covenant. وَلَتَعْلُنَّ عُلُوًّا كَبِيراً ** (and you will surely display great haughtiness) meaning: what they claimed regarding the Caliphate (الخلافة). فَإِذا جاءَ وَعْدُ أُولاهُما (So when the time for the first of the two came) meaning: the Day of the Camel (يوم الجمل), بَعَثْنا عَلَيْكُمْ عِباداً لَنا أُولِي بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ ** (We aroused against you servants of Ours possessing great might) meaning: the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and his companions. فَجاسُوا خِلالَ الدِّيارِ (and they ransacked the houses) meaning: they sought you out and killed you. وَكانَ وَعْداً مَفْعُولاً ** (and it was a promise fulfilled) meaning: it was completed and came to pass. ثُمَّ رَدَدْنا لَكُمُ الْكَرَّةَ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (Then We gave you the turn to prevail over them) meaning: for Banu Umayyah (بني أميّة) against the Family of Muhammad (آل محمد). وَأَمْدَدْناكُمْ بِأَمْوالٍ وَبَنِينَ وَجَعَلْناكُمْ أَكْثَرَ نَفِيراً ** (and aided you with wealth and sons, and made you greater in number) than al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them), the two sons of Ali, and their companions. And they took the women of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family) captive. فَإِذا جاءَ وَعْدُ الْآخِرَةِ ** (So when the time for the second came) meaning: the Qā’im (القائم) and his companions, لِيَسُوؤُا وُجُوهَكُمْ ** (to sadden your faces) meaning: their faces will be blackened. وَلِيَدْخُلُوا الْمَسْجِدَ كَما دَخَلُوهُ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ** (and enter the Mosque just as they entered it the first time) meaning: the Messenger of Allah (may the blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) and his companions and the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). لِيُتَبِّرُوا ما عَلَوْا تَتْبِيراً ** (and utterly destroy whatever they captured) meaning: they will prevail over you and kill you. Then He turned to the Family of Muhammad (آل محمد) and said: عَسى رَبُّكُمْ أَنْ يَرْحَمَكُمْ ** (Maybe your Lord will have mercy on you) meaning: He will grant you victory over your enemy. Then He addressed Banu Umayyah (بني أميّة) and said: وَإِنْ عُدْتُمْ عُدْنا ** (and if you return, We shall return) meaning: if you return with the Sufyani (السفياني), We shall return with the Qā’im (القائم) from the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family). وَجَعَلْنا جَهَنَّمَ لِلْكافِرِينَ حَصِيراً (and We have made hell a prison for the faithless) [meaning] a confinement (حبساً) in which they will be confined.

(1) The mawtūr (الموتور) is one whose relative has been killed and whose blood revenge has not been attained. You say of it: watarahu yataruhu watran wa-tiran. (ص)

(٩) إِنَّ هذَا الْقُرْآنَ يَهْدِي لِلَّتِي هِيَ أَقْوَمُ ** (9) Indeed this Quran guides to what is most upright [It guides] to the way (الطريقة) which is the most upright (أقوم) of ways (الطرق) and the most severe in straightness (استقامة).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Meaning, He calls (يدعو).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): It guides to the Imam (peace be upon him).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated disconnectedly (مقطوعاً): The like of it.

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It guides to the Wilayah (guardianship/authority).

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from his grandfather al-Sajjad (peace be upon them both): The Imam from among us is not except one who is infallible (معصوماً). And infallibility (العصمة) is not in the outward appearance of creation (ظاهر الخلقة) such that He is known by it. Therefore, He is not except one designated by text (منصوصاً). So, it was asked: "What is the meaning of the infallible (المعصوم)?" He said: "He is the one who holds fast to the rope of Allah (حبل الله). And the rope of Allah is the Quran. And the Quran guides to the Imam. That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنَّ هذَا الْقُرْآنَ يَهْدِي لِلَّتِي هِيَ أَقْوَمُ (Indeed this Quran guides to what is most upright) وَيُبَشِّرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ الصَّالِحاتِ أَنَّ لَهُمْ أَجْراً كَبِيراً. (9) and gives the good news to the faithful who do righteous deeds that there is a great reward for them.

(١٠) وَأَنَّ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ أَعْتَدْنَا لَهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (10) and that We have prepared a painful punishment for those who do not believe in the Hereafter. It means He gives glad tidings to the believers with two glad tidings: their reward and the punishment of their enemies. (١١) وَيَدْعُ الْإِنْسَانُ بِالشَّرِّ دُعَاءَهُ بِالْخَيْرِ (11) Man prays for evil as he prays for good, like his prayer for good. وَكَانَ الْإِنْسَانُ عَجُولًا and man is hasty. In Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "And know the path of your salvation and your destruction, so that you do not call upon Allah for something that might contain your destruction while you think that it contains your salvation." The Almighty Allah said: وَيَدْعُ الْإِنْسَانُ (Man prays) [the rest of] the Ayah. And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) that he said: "When Allah created Adam and breathed into him of His Spirit, he leaped up to stand before his creation was completed, so he fell. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَكَانَ الْإِنْسَانُ عَجُولًا (and man is hasty)."

(١٢) **وَجَعَلْنَا اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ آيَتَيْنِ فَمَحَوْنا آيَةَ اللَّيْلِ وَجَعَلْنا آيَةَ النَّهارِ مُبْصِرَةً لِتَبْتَغُوا فَضْلاً مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ** (12) We made the night and the day two signs. Then We blotted out the sign of the night, and made the sign of the day bright, that you may seek the bounty of your Lord,

and that you may seek in the brightness of the day the means of your livelihoods وَلِتَعْلَمُوا ** (and that you may know) through their difference and their measures, the number of years and the reckoning (الْحِسابَ), and everything (وَكُلَّ شَيْءٍ) that you are in need of concerning the matter of religion and the world. فَصَّلْناهُ تَفْصِيلاً ** (We have elaborated it with a detailed elaboration), meaning: We have clarified it with an unambiguous clarification.

In Nahj al-Balaghah (نهج البلاغة): He made its sun a bright sign for its day, and its moon a blotted-out sign from its night. He caused them both to run in the courses of their running, and He measured their journey in the tracks of their orbits, so that the night and the day might be distinguished by them, and so that the number of years and the reckoning (الْحِسابَ) might be known by their measures.

And in al-Ilal (العلل), narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): He was asked why the sun and the moon are not equal in radiance (الضّوء) and light (النّور). He said: "When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, created them, they obeyed and did not disobey anything. Then Allah commanded Gabriel (جبرئيل) to blot out the radiance (ضوء) of the moon, so he blotted it out. The blotting left black lines (خطوطاً سوداء) on the moon. If the moon had been left in its state, like the sun, unblotted, the night would not have been distinguished from the day, nor the day from the night, nor would the fasting person know how long he should fast, nor would people know the number of years. That is the saying of Allah, the Exalted: وَجَعَلْنَا اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ آيَتَيْنِ (We made the night and the day two signs) [the rest of] the Ayah."

And in al-Ihtijaj (الإِحتجاج): Ibn al-Kawwa said to the Commander of the Faithful (عليه السلام): "Inform me about the blotting (المحو) that is in the moon." He (عليه السلام) said: "Allah is Greater! Allah is Greater! A blind man asks about a blind matter! Have you not heard Allah say: وَجَعَلْنَا اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ آيَتَيْنِ فَمَحَوْنا آيَةَ اللَّيْلِ وَجَعَلْنا آيَةَ النَّهارِ مُبْصِرَةً. (We made the night and the day two signs. Then We blotted out the sign of the night, and made the sign of the day bright.)"

And narrated from al-Sadiq (عليه السلام): When Allah created the moon, He wrote upon it: لا إله إلّا الله مُحمّدٌ رسول اللهِ عليٌّ أمير المؤمنين (There is no god except Allah, Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, Ali is the Commander of the Faithful), and that is the blackness (السّواد) which you see.

And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated that which is close to these two narrations.

(١٣) وَكُلَّ إِنسانٍ أَلْزَمْناهُ طائِرَهُ ** (13) And We have attached every person’s omen to his neck. his deed (عمله) and what was decreed for him (وما قدر له), as if it were a bird (طير) for him from the nest of the Unseen (عشّ الغيب) and the lair of Destiny (ووكر القدر) فِي عُنُقِهِ ** like the permanence of a collar (الطّوق) around his neck. Al-Ayyashi narrated it from both of them (peace be upon them). And al-Qummi said: It is the destiny (قدره) of Allah which He decreed upon him. And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): His good and his evil (خيره وشرّه) are with him wherever he is; he cannot separate from it until he is given his book (كتابه) on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) containing what he did (بما عمل). وَنُخْرِجُ لَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ كِتاباً ** (13) and We shall bring it out for him on the Day of Resurrection as a book It is the scroll (صحيفة) of his deed. أقولُ : It is precisely his soul (نفسه) itself, in which the effects (آثار) of his deeds have become firmly rooted (رسخت), such that they are inscribed upon it (انتقشت بها). يَلْقاهُ مَنْشُوراً ** (13) which he will find spread open. due to the removal of the veil (كشف الغطاءِ). And it was read yulaqqāhu (يُلَقَّاهُ) with tashdid (بالتشديد) and built for the passive voice (والبناءِ للمفعول).

(١٤) اقْرَأْ كِتابَكَ ** (14) اقْرَأْ كِتابَكَ (Read your book) implying the saying: كَفى بِنَفْسِكَ الْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكَ حَسِيباً** (Your soul suffices today as a reckoner against you.)

In المجمع and العيّاشيّ, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: The servant remembers everything he did and everything that was written against him, until it is as if he did it that very hour. That is why they say: يا وَيْلَتَنا ما لِهذَا الْكِتابِ لا يُغادِرُ صَغِيرَةً وَلا كَبِيرَةً إِلَّا أَحْصاها. (Woe to us! What is the matter with this book? It has not left out any minor or major sin, but has enumerated it!)

(١٥) مَنِ اهْتَدى فَإِنَّما يَهْتَدِي لِنَفْسِهِ وَمَنْ ضَلَّ فَإِنَّما يَضِلُّ عَلَيْها وَلا تَزِرُ وازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرى ** (15) Whoever is guided, is guided only for his own sake, and whoever goes astray, goes astray only to his own detriment. No bearer shall bear another’s burden. And no soul bearing a burden shall bear the burden of another soul; rather, it only bears its own burden. وَما كُنَّا مُعَذِّبِينَ حَتَّى نَبْعَثَ رَسُولاً ** And We would not punish [anyone] until We have sent a messenger. who clarifies the proofs (الحجَجَ) and establishes the laws (الشرايع), thereby making the proof (الحجّة) binding upon them. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He (peace be upon him) was asked: "Has a means (أداة) been placed among people by which they attain knowledge (المعرفة)?" He said: "No." It was asked: "Are they then obligated (كلّفوا) with knowledge (المعرفة)?" He said: "No. The clarification (البيان) is incumbent upon Allah." لا يُكَلِّفُ اللهُ نَفْساً إِلَّا ما آتاها. Allah does not task any soul except according to what He has given it.

(١٦) وَإِذا أَرَدْنا أَنْ نُهْلِكَ قَرْيَةً ** (16) When We desire to destroy a town, And when Our Will relates to the destruction of a people due to the approach of its decreed time, أَمَرْنا مُتْرَفِيها ** We command its affluent ones, its luxurious ones (متنعّميها), فَفَسَقُوا فِيها. and they commit transgression in it. Al-Qummi said: We multiplied (كثرنا) its tyrants (جبابرتها). And Al-Ayyashi narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): (أَمَرْنا) with the mīm (ميم) intensified (مشدّدة), its interpretation is: We multiplied (كثرنا). And He said: I did not read it with the mīm lightened (مخففة). And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): أَمَرْنا ** means its great ones (أكابرها). And in al-Majma', narrated from Him (peace be upon him): that He read (أَمَّرْنَا) with the intensification (بتشديد) of the mīm (الميم). And narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): that it was read (آمرنا) on the pattern (وزن) of (عامرنا). It is said: (أمرت) the thing and (آمرته), so it became (أمر), meaning when you multiplied it (كثرته). And in the Hadith: The best wealth is a row of date palms (سكة) that is pollinated (مأبورة) (1) and a mare (مهرة) that is commanded (مأمورة), meaning one that is abundant in offspring (كثيرة النِّتاج). And the sikkah (السكة) is the date palm, and the muhrah (المهرة) is the horse. And it was said that the specification of the affluent (المترفين) is because others follow them, and because they are quicker to foolishness (الحماقة) and more capable of immorality (الفجور). فَحَقَّ عَلَيْهَا الْقَوْلُ ** the sentence becomes due against it, meaning the word of punishment (كلمة العذاب). فَدَمَّرْناها تَدْمِيراً ** and We destroy it utterly. We annihilated it (أهلكناها).

(١٧) وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنا And many We destroyed مِنَ الْقُرُونِ مِنْ بَعْدِ نُوحٍ ** of the generations after Noah! such as 'Ād and Thamūd. وَكَفى بِرَبِّكَ بِذُنُوبِ عِبادِهِ خَبِيراً بَصِيراً ** Your Lord suffices as an all-aware, all-seeing observer of the sins of His servants. He perceives their inner and outer aspects, and thus punishes for them. (17) How many generations We have destroyed after Noah! Your Lord suffices as an all-aware, all-seeing observer of the sins of His servants.

(١٨) مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ الْعاجِلَةَ ** Whoever desires the fleeting world, the worldly bounty, confining his aspiration to it, عَجَّلْنا لَهُ فِيها ما نَشاءُ لِمَنْ نُرِيدُ We readily grant him in it whatever We wish for whomever We want. He restricted what is readily granted and the one for whom it is granted by the Will (المشيئة) and the Intention (الإِرادة), because not every wisher finds what he wishes for, nor does everyone attain all that he desires, and so that it may be known that the matter is subject to the Will (المشيئة). ثُمَّ جَعَلْنا لَهُ جَهَنَّمَ يَصْلاها مَذْمُوماً مَدْحُوراً ** Then We appoint hell for him, where he shall enter, disgraced and rejected. expelled from the mercy of Allah. (18) Whoever desires the fleeting world, We readily grant him in it whatever We wish for whomever We want. Then We appoint hell for him, where he shall enter, disgraced and rejected.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: The meaning of the Ayah is: "Whoever desires the reward of this world through an action that Allah has made obligatory upon him, not desiring thereby the Face of Allah and the Hereafter, We readily grant him whatever Allah wills of the transient things (عَرَض) of this world, and he shall have no reward in the Hereafter." That is because Allah, the Glorified, grants him that so that he may seek help through it for obedience (الطاعة), but he uses it in disobedience (مَعْصِيَة) to Allah, so Allah punishes him for it.

(١٩) وَمَنْ أَرادَ الْآخِرَةَ وَسَعى لَها سَعْيَها ** (19) But whoever desires the Hereafter and strives for it with its due striving its due striving (حقّها من السعي), which is performing what He commanded and refraining from what He forbade, not seeking nearness through what they invent based on their opinions (بآرائهم). And the benefit of the lām (in لَها) is the consideration of intention (النّيّة) and sincerity (الإخلاص). وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ ** while he is a believer with a faith (إيماناً) in which there is neither polytheism (شرك) nor denial (تكذيب). فَأُولئِكَ كانَ سَعْيُهُمْ مَشْكُوراً ** the striving of such ones will be appreciated [by Allah]. by Allah, accepted by Him, and rewarded for. It is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: وَمَنْ أَرادَ الْآخِرَةَ ** (But whoever desires the Hereafter) — let him abandon the adornment of the worldly life (الحيوة الدنيا).
