footnote (١) أبر فلان نخله اي لقّمه وأسلمه ومنه سكة مأبورة. (1) Abar (أبر) so-and-so his date palm, meaning he pollinated it (لقّمه) and handed it over (أسلمه). From this comes the phrase sikkah ma’būrah (سكة مأبورة) (a pollinated furrow/row).

(٢٠) كُلًّا نُمِدُّ هؤُلاءِ وَهَؤُلاءِ مِنْ عَطاءِ رَبِّكَ ** (We provide aid to all, these and those, out of the bounty of your Lord) Every single one of the two groups—We bestow favor upon him with the giving, time after time. We make the recent (الآنف) of it an aid (مدداً) for the preceding (السالف); We do not cut it off. Thus, We provide sustenance to the obedient (المطيع) and the disobedient (العاصي) all together. وَما كانَ عَطاءُ رَبِّكَ مَحْظُوراً ** (and the bounty of your Lord is not confined.) prevented (ممنوعاً). He does not prevent the disobedient person due to his disobedience.

(21) انْظُرْ كَيْفَ فَضَّلْنا بَعْضَهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ (21) Observe how We have favored some of them over others. in this world وَلَلْآخِرَةُ أَكْبَرُ دَرَجاتٍ وَأَكْبَرُ تَفْضِيلاً But the Hereafter is surely greater in respect of degrees and greater in respect of preference. meaning, the disparity (التفاوت) in the Hereafter is greater.

In al-Majma', it is narrated: That which is between the highest degrees (درجات) of Paradise and its lowest is like that which is between the heaven and the earth.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Do not say that Paradise is one (واحدة). Indeed, Allah says: وَمِنْ دُونِهِما جَنَّتانِ (And besides them there are two gardens). And do not say that the degree (درجة) is one. Indeed, Allah says: فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ دَرَجاتٍ (degrees above some others)." Some of them. Indeed, people are favored over one another by their deeds (بالأعمال).

It was said to him: If two believers enter Paradise, and one of them is in a higher place than the other, and he desires to meet his companion. He said: "The one who is above him is permitted to descend, but the one who is below him is not permitted to ascend, because he has not reached that place. However, if they desire that and are attracted to it (واستهووه), they will meet upon the couches (على الأسرّة)."

And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Indeed, the servants will rise tomorrow in degrees (في الدرجات) and attain closeness (الزلفى) to their Lord according to the measure of their intellects (عقولهم)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, the reward (الثواب) is according to the measure of the intellect (العقل)."

(٢٢) لا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ اللهِ إِلهاً آخَرَ ** (Do not set up another god besides Allah) The address is to every individual, or to the Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) while intending his community (أمّته), as al-Qummi said. فَتَقْعُدَ مَذْمُوماً مَخْذُولاً ** (or you will sit down despised and forsaken). Meaning, if you do that, you will remain, as long as you live, despised on the tongues of the wise (العقلاء), forsaken (مَخْذُولاً), having no helper (ناصر). He only expressed this using the term "to sit/remain" (القعود) because "sitting/remaining" (القعود) carries the meaning of humiliation (الذلّ), incapacity (العجز), and disgrace (الهوان). It is said: weakness (الضّعف) caused him to sit/remain (قعد به).

(٢٣) وَقَضى رَبُّكَ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا إِيَّاهُ ** (23) Your Lord has decreed that you shall worship none except Him, And He commanded a decisive command that you should not worship except Him, because the utmost degree of veneration (غاية التعظيم) is not rightful except for the One Who possesses the utmost degree of greatness (غاية العظمة) and the limit of bestowal of favors (نهاية الأنعام). And it is permissible that أن (an) is explanatory (مفسّرة) and لا (lā) is prohibitive (ناهية). A narrative concerning this will come after eighteen verses. وَبِالْوالِدَيْنِ إِحْساناً ** and [He has enjoined] kindness to parents. Meaning: that you should be kind (أن تحسنُوا) or be kind (أحسنوا) to the parents with kindness, because they are the apparent cause (السببَ الظاهِرُ) of existence and livelihood (التعيّش). إِمَّا (immā) is the conditional إِن (in) upon which ما (mā) has been added for emphasis (للتأكيد), and for this reason, the attachment of the نُون (nūn) is valid. عِنْدَكَ الْكِبَرَ ** If either of them or both of them reach old age with you, meaning, under your protection (كنفك) and guardianship (كفالتكَ). أَحَدُهُما أَوْ كِلاهُما فَلا تَقُلْ لَهُما أُفٍ ** do not say to them, 'Fie!' even if they cause you distress (أضجراك). وَلا تَنْهَرْهُما ** and do not chide them, and do not rebuke them (تزجرهما). Al-Qummi (said): meaning, do not dispute with them (تخاصمهما). وَقُلْ لَهُما قَوْلاً كَرِيماً ** but speak to them with kind words. Good (حسناً) and beautiful (جميلاً).

(٢٤) وَاخْفِضْ لَهُما جَناحَ الذُّلِ ** (24) Lower to them the wing of humility, out of mercy, and say: 'My Lord! Have mercy on them, just as they nurtured me when I was a child.' [meaning] your humble wing, or [it is] making humility a wing for emphasis, meaning: be submissive and humble towards them. مِنَ الرَّحْمَةِ ** [meaning] out of the excess of your mercy upon them, due to their need for one who was the poorest of Allah's creation towards them. وَقُلْ رَبِّ ارْحَمْهُما ** and supplicate to Allah that He may have mercy on them with His everlasting mercy, and do not suffice with your transient mercy. كَما رَبَّيانِي صَغِيراً ** [This is] a recompense for their mercy upon me, their nurturing, and their guidance of me in my childhood.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this kindness (الإحسان). He said: "It is that you treat them well (تُحْسِن صُحْبَتَهُمَا) and that you do not burden them to ask you for anything, even if they are self-sufficient. Does not Allah say: لَنْ تَنالُوا الْبِرَّ حَتَّى تُنْفِقُوا مِمَّا تُحِبُّونَ (You will never attain piety until you spend out of what you hold dear)? So, do not say to them 'Fie!' nor scold them." He (peace be upon him) said: "If they annoy you, فَلا تَقُلْ لَهُما أُفٍّ وَلا تَنْهَرْهُما ** [He said]: "If they strike you, and speak to them noble words." He (peace be upon him) said: "If they strike you, say to them: 'May Allah forgive you both,' for that is a noble word (قَوْل كَرِيم) from you. وَاخْفِضْ لَهُما جَناحَ الذُّلِّ مِنَ الرَّحْمَةِ **" He (peace be upon him) said: "Do not fill your eyes with looking at them except with mercy and tenderness, and do not raise your voice above their voices, nor your hand above their hands, nor walk ahead of them."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Had Allah known of anything lesser than أُفٍ ** He would have forbidden it, and it is among the lowest forms of disobedience (العقوق)."

And it is added in al-Kafi: "And among the forms of disobedience (العقوق) is that a man looks at his parents and stares sharply at them."

And narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): A man asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "What is the right of the father upon his child?" He said: "He should not call him by his name, nor walk in front of him, nor sit before him, nor cause others to curse him (يَسْتَسِبّ لَهُ)."

And in al-Jawami': The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "May his nose be rubbed in the dust (رَغِمَ أَنْفُهُ)," three times. They asked: "Whose, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "The one who reaches his parents **عِنْدَكَ الْكِبَرَ أَحَدُهُما أَوْ كِلاهُما ** when they are old, either one of them or both of them, and yet does not enter Paradise."

And narrated from Hudhayfah: He sought permission from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to kill his father while he was in the ranks of the polytheists. He said: "Leave him; let someone else take care of him (يَلِهِ غَيْرُك)."

(٢٥) رَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِما فِي نُفُوسِكُمْ إِنْ تَكُونُوا صالِحِينَ فَإِنَّهُ كانَ لِلْأَوَّابِينَ غَفُوراً. (25) Your Lord knows best what is in your souls. If you are righteous, then He is indeed forgiving toward the penitent.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the frequently repentant (التّوّابون), the devoted worshippers (المتعبّدون).

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): The al-Awwāb (الأوّاب) is the frequently repentant (التواب), the devoted worshipper (المتعبد), the one who returns from his sin.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): A prayer of four cycles (ركعات), in each cycle (ركعة) you recite fifty times: قُلْ هُوَ اللهُ أَحَدٌ. (Say, 'He is Allah, the One.)

(٢٦) وَآتِ ذَا الْقُرْبى حَقَّهُ وَالْمِسْكِينَ وَابْنَ السَّبِيلِ ** (26) And give the relative his due, and the needy and the traveller,**

It was said in the interpretation of the general populace (العامّة) that the Glorified One commanded concerning relatives other than the parents, and the needy (المساكين), and the travelers (أبناءِ السّبيل), that their rights be given after He had commanded concerning the parents. And it was said regarding it that the intended meaning of بِذِي الْقُرْبى ** (the relative) is the kin (قرابة) of the Prophet صلى اللهُ عليه وآله وسلم.

And al-Qummi (said): It means the kin (قرابة) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and it was revealed concerning Fatimah, so Fadak was assigned to her. وَالْمِسْكِينَ ** (and the needy) refers to the descendants of Fatimah. وَابْنَ السَّبِيلِ ** (and the traveller) refers to the Household of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) and the descendants of Fatimah عليهَا السلام. He mentioned the story of Fadak in detail in Surat al-Rum in the interpretation of a similar verse.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) in a tradition he had with al-Mahdi: When Allah the Almighty granted His Prophet the conquest of Fadak and what adjoined it, no horse or camel was spurred against it (لَمْ يُوجِفْ عَلَيْهِ بِخَيْلٍ وَلَا رِكَابٍ). Then Allah sent down to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: وَآتِ ذَا الْقُرْبى حَقَّهُ ** (And give the relative his due). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not know who they were, so he consulted Gabriel (جبرئيل) regarding that, and Gabriel consulted his Lord. So Allah revealed to him: "Hand over Fadak to Fatimah عليهَا السلام." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم then called her and said: "O Fatimah, Allah the Almighty has commanded me to hand Fadak over to you." She said: "I have accepted, O Messenger of Allah, from Allah and from you." (The rest of the tradition follows).

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a tradition he had with al-Ma’mun: "And the fifth verse is the saying of Allah the Almighty: وَآتِ ذَا الْقُرْبى حَقَّهُ ** (And give the relative his due). This is a distinction (خصوصيّة) with which Allah, the All-Mighty, the Compeller (العزيز الجبّار), distinguished them and chose them above the Ummah. When this verse was revealed to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: 'Call Fatimah عليهَا السلام for me.' So she was called for him. He said: 'O Fatimah.' She replied: 'I am at your service (لبيّك), O Messenger of Allah.' He said: 'This is Fadak. It is among that against which no horse or camel was spurred, and it belongs specifically to me, exclusive of the Muslims. I have assigned it to you because Allah commanded Me to do so. So take it for yourself and your children.'"

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Allah revealed وَآتِ ذَا الْقُرْبى حَقَّهُ وَالْمِسْكِينَ ** (And give the relative his due, and the needy), the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Gabriel (جبرئيل), I know the needy (المسكين), but who is the relative (ذو القربى)?" He replied: "They are your close relatives (أقاربك)." So he called Hasan, Husayn, and Fatimah, and said: "Indeed, My Lord has commanded me to give you from what Allah has bestowed upon me as fay' (أفاء الله عليّ)." He said: "I give you Fadak." (Along with other narrations concerning this meaning).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): He said to one of the Syrians (الشاميينّ): "Have you not read this verse: وَآتِ ذَا الْقُرْبى حَقَّهُ (And give the relative his due)?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Then we are those whom Allah commanded His Prophet to give their due right."

In al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him) through the narration of the general populace (العامّة): That which is similar in meaning. And narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri: When this verse was revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم gave Fadak to Fatimah عليهَا السلام.

In summary, the narrations concerning this meaning are widespread (مستفيضة). And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a tradition: Then He, majestic is His mention, said: وَآتِ ذَا الْقُرْبى حَقَّهُ ** (And give the relative his due). And that was Ali (peace be upon him), and His right was the trusteeship (الوصيّة) that was assigned to him, and the Greatest Name (الاسم الأكبر), and the inheritance of knowledge, and the signs (آثار) of the knowledge of prophethood.

I say: There is no contradiction between this tradition and the preceding traditions, nor between them and the two interpretations of the general populace (العامّة), nor between their two interpretations, as becomes clear to the reflective person who is knowledgeable of the addresses of the Quran and the meaning of rights (الحقوق), and who is the one who possesses the right and who is the one who does not possess the right. And all praise belongs to Allah.

وَلا تُبَذِّرْ تَبْذِيراً ** (27) and do not squander wastefully.**

By spending wealth on what is inappropriate and expending it in a manner of extravagance (الإسراف). The origin of al-tabdhir (التّبذير) is scattering (التفريق). In al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He passed by Sa'd while he was performing ablution (يتوضّأ) and said: "What is this extravagance (السّرف), O Sa'd?" He said: "Is there extravagance in ablution?" He replied: "Yes, even if you are by a flowing river."

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He said to a man: "Fear Allah, and do not be extravagant (تسرف), and do not be stingy (تقتر), but be moderate (قواماً) between the two. Indeed, al-tabdhir (التّبذير) is a form of extravagance (الإِسراف)." Allah said: وَلا تُبَذِّرْ تَبْذِيراً. (and do not squander wastefully.)

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: "Whoever spends anything in disobedience to Allah is a squanderer (مبذّر), and whoever spends in the way of Allah is moderate (مقتصد)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked: "Can there be squandering (تبذير) in what is lawful (حلال)?" He said: "Yes."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He called for fresh dates (رطب), and some of them began throwing away the pits (النّوى). He said: "Do not do that. Indeed, this is part of squandering (التبذير), and Allah does not love corruption (الفساد)."

And in al-Majalis, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah: وَلا تُبَذِّرْ تَبْذِيراً ** (and do not squander wastefully). He said: "Do not squander in the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him)."

(٢٧) إِنَّ الْمُبَذِّرِينَ كانُوا إِخْوانَ الشَّياطِينِ ** (27) Indeed the wasteful are brothers of the devils, (They are) their likes (أمثالهم), those who follow their path. And this is the utmost degree of censure (غاية الذّمّ). وَكانَ الشَّيْطانُ لِرَبِّهِ كَفُوراً and Satan is ungrateful to his Lord. (He is) excessive (مبالغاً) in disbelief (الكفر). Thus, he ought not to be obeyed.

(٢٨) وَإِمَّا تُعْرِضَنَّ عَنْهُمُ ابْتِغاءَ رَحْمَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّكَ تَرْجُوها فَقُلْ لَهُمْ قَوْلاً مَيْسُوراً ** (28) But if you have to disregard them, seeking a mercy that you expect from your Lord, then speak to them a gentle word. And if you turn away from these people whom We commanded you to give their rights (حقوقهم) to, out of shame (حياءً) from refusing (them), so that you may seek the bounty (الفضل) from your Lord and the abundance (السعة) with which you can afford the giving (البذل), then speak to them a soft word (قولاً ليّناً) and promise them a beautiful promise (عِدة جميلة). Thus, the seeking (الابتغاء) was placed in the position of the lack of provision (فقد الرّزق), because the one lacking provision is seeking it (مبتغ له).

وفي المجمع والعيّاشيّ : It is narrated in al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi: It is narrated that when this Ayah was revealed, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, if he was asked (for something) and did not have anything to give, would say: "May Allah provide for us and for you from His bounty (فضله)."

(٢٩) وَلا تَجْعَلْ يَدَكَ مَغْلُولَةً إِلى عُنُقِكَ (١) (1) Do not keep your hand chained to your neck, وَلا تَبْسُطْها كُلَّ الْبَسْطِ (٢) (2) nor stretch it out all together, This is a metaphor for the withholding of the miser (الشحيح) and the extravagance of the squanderer (المبذّر). He forbade both of them and commanded moderation (الاقتصاد) between them, which is generosity (الكرم) and munificence (الجود). فَتَقْعُدَ مَلُوماً مَحْسُوراً. lest you sit blameworthy and regretful.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): No one would ask the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم for anything of this world except that He would give it to him. So, a woman sent her son to Him and said: "Go to Him and ask Him. If He says, 'We do not have anything,' then say, 'Give me your shirt.'" He (the narrator) said: So, He took His shirt and gave it to him. Then Allah disciplined Him regarding moderation (القصد), and He said: **وَلا تَجْعَلْ يَدَكَ ** (Do not keep your hand chained to your neck) the Ayah.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him) in a hadith: Then Allah taught His Prophet how to spend. This was because He had an ūqiyah (أوقيَة) of gold, and He disliked that it should remain with Him overnight, so He gave it away in charity. He woke up and had nothing. Then someone came asking Him, and He had nothing to give him, so the asker blamed Him, and He (the Prophet) became distressed because He had nothing to give, for He was compassionate (رحيماً) and tender-hearted (رقيقاً). So, Allah disciplined His Prophet with His command, saying: وَلا تَجْعَلْ يَدَكَ ** (Do not keep your hand chained to your neck) the Ayah. He says: "They may ask you and not excuse you. If you give away all the wealth you possess, you will have been stripped (حسرت) of wealth."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: al-Ihsār (الإِحسار) is poverty (الفاقة).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said regarding this Ayah: al-Ihsār (الإِحسار) is scarcity (الإقتار).

And al-Qummi said: The reason for its revelation was that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would not turn away anyone who asked Him for something He possessed. So, a man came to Him and asked Him, but He did not have anything present, so He said: "It will be, if Allah wills." The man said: "O Messenger of Allah, give me your shirt." So, He gave him His shirt. Then Allah revealed وَلا تَجْعَلْ (Do not keep) the Ayah. So, Allah forbade Him from being stingy and from being extravagant, and from sitting stripped (محسوراً) of clothes. Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: al-Mahsūr (المحسور) is the naked one (العريان).

And in al-Tahdhib and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: وَلا تَجْعَلْ يَدَكَ مَغْلُولَةً إِلى عُنُقِكَ ** (Do not keep your hand chained to your neck), He said, folding His hands: "Like this." وَلا تَبْسُطْها كُلَّ الْبَسْطِ ** (nor stretch it out all together), He said, spreading His palm: "And like this."

(١) Meaning: Do not be among those who give nothing and bestow nothing, so that you are like one whose hand is chained to his neck, unable to give or spend. This is an exaggeration in the prohibition of stinginess (الشّح) and withholding (الإِمساك).

(٢) Meaning: And also, do not give away everything you possess, so that you are like one who stretches out his hand until nothing remains in it. This is a metaphor (كناية) for extravagance (الإِسراف).

(30) إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ** (30) Indeed your Lord expands the provision for whomever He wishes, and tightens it. He expands it and tightens it according to the expediency (المَصْلحة). إِنَّهُ كانَ بِعِبادِهِ خَبِيراً بَصِيراً (30) Indeed He is all-aware, all-seeing of His servants. So He knows their interests and what is proper for them and what is not proper, as was mentioned in the Sacred Narration (الحَديثِ القدسيّ): "And indeed, among My servants are those whom nothing sets right except poverty, and were I to enrich him, that would surely corrupt him. And indeed, among My servants are those whom nothing sets right except wealth, and were I to impoverish him, that would surely corrupt him." And He said: "And indeed, I am most knowledgeable of the interests of My servants." The completion of the narration is to be sought from al-Kafi (الكافي). And in Nahj al-Balagha (نهج البلاغة): "He decreed the provisions, so He increased them and decreased them, and divided them between scarcity and abundance, and He was just in doing so, so that He may test whom He wills with their ease and their hardship, and so that He may test thereby gratitude and patience from their rich and their poor."

(٣١) وَلا تَقْتُلُوا أَوْلادَكُمْ (١) خَشْيَةَ إِمْلاقٍ. (31) Do not kill your children for the fear of destitution.

Al-Qummi said: It means the fear of poverty (الفقر) and hunger (الجُوع), for the Arabs used to kill their children for that reason.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The pilgrim (الحاجّ) will never become destitute (يملِق) ever." He was asked: "What is al-Imlaq (الاملاق)?" He replied: "Bankruptcy (الإِفلاس)." Then He (peace be upon him) recited this Ayah:

نَحْنُ نَرْزُقُهُمْ وَإِيَّاكُمْ إِنَّ قَتْلَهُمْ كانَ خِطْأً كَبِيراً (31) We will provide for them and for you. Indeed, killing them is a great sin.

(It means) a great sin (ذنباً كبيراً). And it was recited with the fathah on the khā’ and the ṭā’ (خَطَأً), which is the opposite of correctness (الصواب) or means error (الخطاءِ). And (it was recited) with the kasrah and the madd (خِطْأً), which is either a dialect (لغة) concerning it or a verbal noun (مصدر).

(32) وَلا تَقْرَبُوا الزِّنى إِنَّهُ كانَ فاحِشَةً ** (Do not approach adultery. It is indeed an indecency) ugly, exceeding the limit of ugliness, وَساءَ سَبِيلاً **(2) (and a bad way.)

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: "disobedience and hatred (مقتاً), for Allah hates Him and detests Him." He said: وَساءَ سَبِيلاً (and a bad way), "and it is the severest punishment in the Fire, and الزّنا is one of the greatest major sins (أكبر الكبائر)."

In al-Faqih and al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from his grandfather, from Ali (peace be upon them), from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, in his testament to him: "O Ali, in الزّنا there are six characteristics (خصال): three of them are in this world (الدنيا) and three are in the Hereafter (الآخِرة). As for those in this world, it removes splendor (البهاءِ), hastens annihilation (الفناءَ), and cuts off provision (الرّزق). As for those in the Hereafter, they are the evil reckoning (سوء الحساب), the wrath of the All-beneficent (سخط الرّحمن), and eternal dwelling in the Fire (الخلود في النّار)."

And from him (peace be upon him): "If الزّنا spreads, earthquakes (الزّلازل) appear."

(٣٣) وَلا تَقْتُلُوا النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللهُ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِ ** (33) (Do not kill any soul whom Allah has forbidden, except rightfully.) except by one of three [reasons]: disbelief after faith (كفر بعد الإِيمان), adultery after chastity (زنا بعد إحصان), and the intentional killing of a believer (قتل مؤمن عمداً). وَمَنْ قُتِلَ مَظْلُوماً ** (Whoever is killed wrongfully) [meaning] one who is not deserving of being killed (غير مُستوجب للقتل). فَقَدْ جَعَلْنا لِوَلِيِّهِ ** (We have certainly given his heir) [meaning] to the one who manages his affairs after his death (لمن يلي أمره بعد وفاته), authority (سُلْطاناً), [which is] dominance through retribution (تسلطاً بالمؤاخذة). فَلا يُسْرِفْ فِي الْقَتْلِ ** (so let him not commit excess in killing.) And it was recited with the tā’ (وقرئ بالتّاءِ). إِنَّهُ كانَ مَنْصُوراً. (Indeed, he is helped.) Al-Qummi [said]: meaning He helps the child of the slain against the killer. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah. It was said: "What is this excess (الإسراف) that Allah has forbidden?" He said: "He forbade that one should kill other than his killer, or mutilate (يمثّل) the killer." It was asked: "What is the meaning of His saying: إِنَّهُ كانَ مَنْصُوراً (Indeed, he is helped)?" He said: "And what help (نصرة) is greater than that the killer is handed over to the heirs (أولياءِ) of the slain, so they kill him, and no consequence (تبعة) is incumbent upon them for his killing, neither in religion (دين) nor in this world (دنيا)." And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi: If a group (العدّة) gathers to kill one man, the ruler (الوالي) judges that they may kill whichever of them they wish, and they are not permitted to kill more than one, for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: وَمَنْ قُتِلَ مَظْلُوماً (Whoever is killed wrongfully) up to His saying: فَلا يُسْرِفْ فِي الْقَتْلِ. (so let him not commit excess in killing.) And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning al-Husayn (peace be upon him): If the people of the earth were killed on account of him, it would not be excess (سرفاً).

footnote (١) Allah only forbade them from that because they used to bury daughters alive (*يئدون البنات يدفنونهنّ أحياء*).

footnote (٢) In it is an indication that the intellect deems adultery (*الزّنا*) repugnant because the child would have no lineage (*نسب*), since no adulterer has a greater claim to him than another, which leads to the severing of lineages (*قطع الأنساب*), the nullification of inheritances (*إبطال المواريث*), the nullification of kinship ties (*صلة الرّحم*), and the rights of fathers over children, and that is rejected by the intellects (*مستنكر في العقول*).

(٣٤) وَلا تَقْرَبُوا مالَ الْيَتِيمِ ** (Do not approach the orphan’s property) — much less dispose of it — إِلَّا بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ ** (except in the best manner) — meaning, except by the method which is best, which is preserving it for him — حَتَّى يَبْلُغَ أَشُدَّهُ. (until he comes of age.)

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The cessation of the orphan's state of being an orphan (يُتْم) is puberty (الاحتلام), and that is his coming of age (أشدّه).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): When the boy reaches his coming of age (أشدّه)—thirteen years and enters the fourteenth year—what is incumbent upon those who have reached puberty (المحتلمين) becomes incumbent upon him, whether he has reached puberty or not (احتلم أو لم يحتلم). The evil deeds (السيئات) are written against him, and the good deeds (الحسنات) are written for him, and everything is permissible for him, unless he is weak (ضعيفاً) or foolish (سفيهاً).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): [A narration] close to this.

وَأَوْفُوا بِالْعَهْدِ إِنَّ الْعَهْدَ كانَ مَسْؤُلاً. (And fulfill the covenant. Indeed, the covenant is bound to be questioned.)

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Three things in which Allah has not granted a concession (رخصة) to any person, and he counted among them the fulfillment of the covenant (الوفاء بالعهد).

(٣٥) وَأَوْفُوا الْكَيْلَ إِذا كِلْتُمْ ** (And fill up the measure when you measure) and do not diminish it وَزِنُوا بِالْقِسْطاسِ الْمُسْتَقِيمِ ** (and weigh with the straight balance) with the level balance (بالميزان السّويّ). And it was read with the kasrah on the letter qāf (بكسر القاف). Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is the balance (الميزان) which has a tongue (لسان). ذلِكَ خَيْرٌ وَأَحْسَنُ تَأْوِيلاً ** (That is better and fairer in the outcome.) and better in consequence (عاقبة).

(٣٦) وَلا تَقْفُ ** And do not pursue. Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, do not say ما لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ.** (36) And do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge.

Al-Qummi (said): Do not accuse anyone with ما لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: Whoever slanders (بَهَتَ) (1) a believing man or a believing woman will be made to stand in ṭīnat khabāl (طِينَةِ خَبَال) (2) until he retracts what he said. إِنَّ السَّمْعَ وَالْبَصَرَ وَالْفُؤادَ كُلُّ أُولئِكَ كانَ عَنْهُ مَسْؤُلاً. Indeed, the hearing, the sight, and the heart—all of them are accountable.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding this verse, the hearing will be questioned about what it heard, the sight about what it looked at, and the heart about what it resolved upon (عَقَدَ عَلَيْهِ).

And in al-Kafi, al-Faqih, al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): A man said to him: "I have neighbors, and they have female slaves (جَوَارٍ) who sing and play the lute (الْعُود). Sometimes I enter the lavatory (الْمَخْرَج) and prolong my stay, listening to them." Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "Do not do that." The man said: "By Allah, it is not something I go to with my feet; it is merely a sound I hear with my ears." Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said to him: "By Allah, have you not heard Allah say: إِنَّ السَّمْعَ وَالْبَصَرَ وَالْفُؤادَ كُلُّ أُولئِكَ كانَ عَنْهُ مَسْؤُلاً (Indeed, the hearing, the sight, and the heart—all of them are accountable)?" The man said: "It is as if I have never heard this verse from the Book of Allah, neither from an Arab (عَرَبيّ) nor a non-Arab (عَجَمِيّ). I swear that I have abandoned it, and I seek Allah's forgiveness." (The rest of the narrative).

And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): It is not for you to speak whatever you wish, because Allah says: **وَلا تَقْفُ ما لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ ** (And do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge). And because the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "May Allah have mercy upon a servant who spoke good and profited, or remained silent and was safe." And it is not for you to hear whatever you wish, because Allah says: إِنَّ السَّمْعَ وَالْبَصَرَ (Indeed, the hearing, the sight...) (the rest of the verse).

And in Miṣbāḥ al-Sharī‘ah, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever sleeps after completing the performance of the obligatory duties (الْفَرَائِض), the recommended practices (السُّنَن), and the necessary rights (الْوَاجِبَات), that sleep is praiseworthy (مَحْمُود). And I do not know for the people of our time, if they fulfill these characteristics (الْخِصَال), anything safer than sleep. This is because people have abandoned the observance of their religion and the monitoring of their conditions, and they have taken the left side of the path (شِمَال الطَّرِيق). And if the servant strives not to speak, how can he manage not to hear, unless he has a barrier (مَانِع) against that? And indeed, sleep is one of those instruments (الآلات). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: **إِنَّ السَّمْعَ وَالْبَصَرَ ** and He recited the verse.

(٣٧) وَلا تَمْشِ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَحاً ** (37) And do not walk exultantly on the earth, (meaning) possessing marah (مَرَحاً), which is arrogance (الاختيال). Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, insolence (بطراً) and excessive joy (فرحاً). إِنَّكَ لَنْ تَخْرِقَ الْأَرْضَ ** You will not make a hole (خرقاً) in it due to the severity of your treading. Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, you will not reach all of it. وَلَنْ تَبْلُغَ الْجِبالَ طُولاً ** nor will you reach the mountains in height. by your haughtiness (بتطاولك). Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, you will not be able to reach the peaks (قلل) of the mountains. It was said: This is mockery (تهكّم) of the arrogant person (بالمختال) and a justification (تعليل) for the prohibition, because arrogance (الاختيال) is sheer foolishness (حماقة مجرّدة) that yields no benefit (لا يعود بجدوى). And there is no [benefit] in humiliation (التذلّل).

In al-Faqih, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in his testament (وصيّته) to Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyyah: "And He imposed upon the two feet that you should weigh them down in obedience to Him, and that you should not walk the gait (مشية) of a disobedient person (عاصٍ)." Then He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَلا تَمْشِ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَحاً (And do not walk exultantly on the earth).

(٣٨) كُلُّ ذلِكَ ** (All of that) is a reference to the twenty-five characteristics mentioned starting from His saying: وَلا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ اللهِ إِلهاً آخَرَ (And do not set up another god besides Allah) And it is narrated from Ibn Abbas: that they are what was written on the Tablets of Moses. كانَ سَيِّئُهُ ** (its evil was) meaning that which was forbidden from it, and it was recited سيئة. عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ مَكْرُوهاً ** (with your Lord, hateful) meaning detested (مبغوضاً).

(٣٩) ذلِكَ مِمَّا أَوْحى إِلَيْكَ رَبُّكَ مِنَ الْحِكْمَةِ وَلا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ اللهِ إِلهاً آخَرَ ** (39) That is part of the wisdom which your Lord has revealed to you. And do not set up another god besides Allah, He repeated it to draw attention to the fact that al-Tawhid (Divine Unity) is the beginning and the end of the matter, and the head (رأس) and essence (ملاك) of wisdom. فَتُلْقى فِي جَهَنَّمَ مَلُوماً ** lest you are cast into hell, blamed, blaming yourself. **مَدْحُوراً ** rejected. banished from the mercy of Allah.

Al-Qummi (said): The address is directed to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but the meaning is for the people.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام in a hadith: Then Allah sent Muhammad while He was in Mecca for ten years. No one died in Mecca during those ten years who bore witness that there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم except that He admitted him into Paradise by his affirmation, and this is the faith of confirmation (إيمان التصديق). Allah did not punish anyone who died while following Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم upon that, except for those who associated partners with the All-beneficent (الرحمن). The confirmation of this is that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to Him in the Surah of Bani Isra'il (The Children of Israel) in Mecca: وَقَضى رَبُّكَ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا إِيَّاهُ وَبِالْوالِدَيْنِ إِحْساناً ** (23) Your Lord has decreed that you shall not worship anyone except Him, and [that you shall be good] to the parents. up to His saying: إِنَّهُ كانَ بِعِبادِهِ خَبِيراً بَصِيراً ** (30) Indeed He is well-aware and watchful of His servants. (This was) discipline (أدب), admonition (وعظة), instruction (تعليم), and a gentle prohibition (نهي خفيف). He did not promise (reward) for it, nor did He threaten punishment for committing anything He forbade. He warned against things He cautioned against, but He did not make it severe (يغلظ فيها) nor did He threaten punishment for them. And He said: وَلا تَقْتُلُوا أَوْلادَكُمْ خَشْيَةَ إِمْلاقٍ ** (31) Do not kill your children for the fear of destitution. and recited the verses up to His saying: مَلُوماً مَدْحُوراً.** blamed, rejected.

(٤٠) أَفَأَصْفاكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ بِالْبَنِينَ وَاتَّخَذَ مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ إِناثاً. (Did your Lord single you out for sons, and take daughters for Himself from among the angels?)

Al-Qummi said: It is a refutation of Quraysh regarding what they said, that the angels are the daughters of Allah: إِنَّكُمْ لَتَقُولُونَ قَوْلاً عَظِيماً (You are indeed uttering a monstrous saying) by attributing a child to Him, and then by preferring yourselves over Him, since you assign to Him what you dislike. Then He makes the angels, who are among the noblest of Allah's creation, inferior to them.

**(٤١) وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنا فِي هذَا الْقُرْآنِ لِيَذَّكَّرُوا وَما يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا نُفُوراً ** (41) Certainly We have elaborated [the signs] in this Quran so that they may take admonition, but it increases them only in aversion.

The Almighty's saying: وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنا ** We repeated the proofs (الدلائل) and detailed the lessons (العبر).

فِي هذَا الْقُرْآنِ لِيَذَّكَّرُوا ** so that they may be admonished (ليتعظوا) (1) and take warning (ويعتبروا).

وَما يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا نُفُوراً ** except aversion (نفوراً) from the truth.

Al-Qummi said: If they listen to the Quran, they turn away from it (ينفروا عنه) and deny it (ويكذّبُوه).

**قُلْ لَوْ كانَ مَعَهُ آلِهَةٌ كَما يَقُولُونَ إِذاً لَابْتَغَوْا إِلى ذِي الْعَرْشِ سَبِيلاً ** (42) Say, 'If there were other gods along with Him, as they say, then they would surely have sought a way to the Lord of the Throne.'

They would have sought a way to the Sovereign of the Dominion (مالك الملك) through drawing near (التقرب) and obedience (الطاعة), as comes later in this Surah:

أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ يَبْتَغُونَ إِلى رَبِّهِمُ الْوَسِيلَةَ أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ. (57) Those whom they invoke seek a means of access to their Lord—whichever of them is nearer—

(٤٣) سُبْحانَهُ وَتَعالى عَمَّا يَقُولُونَ عُلُوًّا كَبِيراً. (43) Immaculate is He, and highly exalted above what they say, a great exaltation! (٤٤) تُسَبِّحُ لَهُ And it was read (وقرئ) with the yā' (الياء) [as يُسَبِّحُ] السَّماواتُ السَّبْعُ وَالْأَرْضُ وَمَنْ فِيهِنَّ وَإِنْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا يُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِهِ وَلكِنْ لا تَفْقَهُونَ تَسْبِيحَهُمْ. (44) The seven heavens glorify Him, and the earth, and whoever is in them. There is not a thing but celebrates His praise, but you do not comprehend their glorification. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The walls creak (تَنقَضُّ) due to their glorification (تَسْبِيحُهَا). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): No bird is hunted except by its neglecting the glorification (التسبيح). And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked, "Does the dry tree glorify [Allah]?" He said: "Yes. Have you not heard how the wood of the house creaks (يَنقَضُّ)? That is its glorification (تَسْبِيحُهُ) of Allah. So, glory be to Allah (فَسُبْحَانَ اللهِ) in every state." I say: That is because the deficiencies (نُقْصَانَات) of creation are proofs (دَلَائِل) of the Creator's perfections (كَمَالَات), and their multiplicity and variations are witnesses to His Oneness (وَحْدَانِيَّتِهِ) and the negation of any partner (الشَّرِيك), opposite (الضِّدّ), or peer (النِّدّ) from Him. Just as the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "By His making the senses (المَشَاعِر) sensitive (بِتَشْعِيرِهِ), it is known that He has no sense (مَشْعَر). By His making the substances (الجَوَاهِر) substantial (بِتَجْهِيرِهِ), it is known that He has no substance (جَوْهَر). By His creating opposition (بِمُضَادَّتِهِ) between things, it is known that He has no opposite (ضِدّ). By His creating correlation (بِمُقَارَنَتِهِ) between things, it is known that He has no correlative (قَرِين)." (The Hadith). This, then, is an innate glorification (تَسْبِيح فِطْرِيّ) and an intrinsic necessity (اقْتِضَاء ذَاتِيّ) arising from a manifestation (تَجَلّ) that manifested to them, so they loved Him and were prompted to praise Him without being obligated (مِنْ غَيْرِ تَكْلِيف). This is the intrinsic worship (العِبَادَة الذَّاتِيَّة) in which Allah established them by virtue of the right (الِاسْتِحْقَاق) which is due to Him, exalted be His Majesty (جَلَّ جَلَالُهُ). And further clarification of this will come in Surat al-Nur, if Allah wills. إِنَّهُ كانَ حَلِيماً (Indeed, He is All-clement); He does not hasten the punishment upon you for your heedlessness and your polytheism (شِرْكِكُمْ); **غَفُوراً ** (All-forgiving) for whoever repents among you.

footnote (١) So that they may know the truth (*الحَقّ*). The mention of the proofs (*الدَّلَائِل*) and lessons (*العِبَر*) was omitted because the context indicates them and the listener knows them. (*Majma' al-Bayan*).

(٤٥) وَإِذا قَرَأْتَ الْقُرْآنَ جَعَلْنا بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ حِجاباً مَسْتُوراً ** (45) When you recite the Quran, We draw a hidden curtain between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter. Narrated from al-Hasan: [It is] from the power (قُدْرَة) of Allah that He shields you from them. (٤٦) وَجَعَلْنا عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ أَكِنَّةً أَنْ يَفْقَهُوهُ ** (46) We have put covers on their hearts lest they should understand it, Meaning: He prevents them from understanding it; the covers (أَكِنَّة) prevent it and intervene against their perception and acceptance of the truth (الحق). وَفِي آذانِهِمْ وَقْراً ** and a deafness in their ears. Prevents them from hearing it. وَإِذا ذَكَرْتَ رَبَّكَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ وَحْدَهُ ** When you mention your Lord alone in the Quran, Not accompanied by their deities, وَلَّوْا عَلى أَدْبارِهِمْ نُفُوراً they turn their backs in aversion. Fleeing from hearing the Oneness [of God] (التوحيد) and in aversion.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم entered his dwelling and Quraysh gathered around him, he would recite aloud بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful) and raise his voice with it. Then Quraysh would turn away in flight. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed concerning that: وَإِذا ذَكَرْتَ رَبَّكَ ** (When you mention your Lord), the Ayah.

And al-Qummi said: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed, he would perform the night vigil (تَهَجُّد) with the Quran, and Quraysh would listen to him because of his beautiful voice. But when he recited **بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful), they would flee from him.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed with the people, he would recite aloud بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful). Those hypocrites (المنافقين) behind him would fall back from the rows. When he passed it [the Basmalah] in the Surah, they would return to their places. Some of them said to others: "He is certainly repeating the Name of his Lord repeatedly; he certainly loves his Lord." So Allah revealed وَإِذا ذَكَرْتَ رَبَّكَ ** (When you mention your Lord), the Ayah.

(٤٧) نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِما يَسْتَمِعُونَ بِهِ ** (47) We know best what they listen to by it because of it (بسببه), such as frivolous talk (اللّغو) and mockery (الاستهزاء) of the Quran. إِذْ يَسْتَمِعُونَ إِلَيْكَ وَإِذْ هُمْ نَجْوى ** when they listen to you, and when they secretly confer. secretly conferring (متناجون) إِذْ يَقُولُ الظَّالِمُونَ إِنْ تَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا رَجُلاً مَسْحُوراً when the wrongdoers say, 'You follow only a man affected by magic.' He has been affected by magic (قد سحر به), so he has become mad (فجنَّ) and his intellect has become confused (واختلط عليه عقله).

(٤٨) انْظُرْ كَيْفَ ضَرَبُوا لَكَ الْأَمْثالَ ** (48) Look how they strike parables for you, They likened you to the sorcerer (الساحر), the poet (الشاعِر), the soothsayer (الكاهن), and the madman (المجنون). فَضَلُّوا ** and thus they have gone astray, from the truth (الحق). فَلا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ سَبِيلاً ** and cannot find a way. to it. (٤٩) وَقالُوا أَإِذا كُنَّا عِظاماً وَرُفاتاً ** (49) They say, 'When we have become bones and dust, (meaning) dust (تراباً) and fine dust (غباراً), and our flesh has scattered. أَإِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ خَلْقاً جَدِيداً ** shall we really be raised in a new creation?' (They said this) out of denial (الإنكار) and deeming it improbable (الاستبعاد).

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Ubayy ibn Khalaf came and took a decayed bone (عظماً بالياً) from a wall and crushed it (1). Then he said: "O Muhammad, إِذا كُنَّا عِظاماً وَرُفاتاً ** (2) أَإِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ خَلْقاً جَدِيداً ** (When we have become bones and dust, shall we really be raised in a new creation?)" So Allah the Almighty revealed: قُلْ مَنْ يُحْيِ الْعِظامَ وَهِيَ رَمِيمٌ قُلْ يُحْيِيهَا الَّذِي أَنْشَأَها أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ خَلْقٍ عَلِيمٌ (Say: 'Who will revive the bones when they are decayed?' Say: 'He will revive them Who originated them the first time, and He has knowledge of all creation.')

(٥٠) قُلْ ** (Say) as a response to them: كُونُوا حِجارَةً أَوْ حَدِيداً. (50) (Say: 'Be stones or iron,) (٥١) أَوْ خَلْقاً مِمَّا يَكْبُرُ فِي صُدُورِكُمْ ** (51) or some creation that is yet greater in your breasts.') For He is capable of returning you alive. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The truth (الحق) which is great (يكبر) in your breasts is death (الموت). فَسَيَقُولُونَ مَنْ يُعِيدُنا قُلِ الَّذِي فَطَرَكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ** (Then they will say, 'Who will bring us back?' Say: 'He who originated you the first time.') For indeed, whoever is capable of origination (الإِنشاءِ) is more capable of repetition (الإِعادة). فَسَيُنْغِضُونَ (١) إِلَيْكَ رُؤُسَهُمْ ** (Then they will shake their heads at you) They will move their heads towards you in astonishment (تعجّباً) and mockery (استهزاءً). وَيَقُولُونَ مَتى هُوَ قُلْ عَسى أَنْ يَكُونَ قَرِيباً ** (and say, 'When will it be?' Say: 'Maybe it is near.') For indeed, everything that is coming (آت) is near (قريب).

(١) Al-Fatt (الفت) is the crushing (الدقّ) and breaking (الكسر) with the fingers, and the splitting (الشقّ) in the rock (الصّخرة). (ق).

(٢) Al-Rifāt (الرفات) is that which is broken (يكسر) and worn out (يبلى) from everything. The pattern fu'āl (فعال) is frequently used for everything that is shattered (يحطم) and crushed (يرضّض). It is said: hutām (حطام), diqāq (دقاق), and turāb (تراب). Al-Mubarrad (المبرّد) said: Everything that is pounded (مدقوق), with exaggeration in its pounding until it is pulverized (انسحق), is rifāt (رفات).

(٥٢) يَوْمَ يَدْعُوكُمْ فَتَسْتَجِيبُونَ ** (The day He calls you, you will respond) meaning, the Day He resurrects you, and you rise up submissively (منقادين). He used the metaphor (استعار) of calling (الدعاء) and responding (الاستجابة) for them to draw attention (للتنبيه) to their swiftness and the ease of their matter. بِحَمْدِهِ ** (with His praise) praising Allah for the perfection of His power (قدرته). In al-Jawami': It is narrated that they shake off (٢) the dust (التراب) from their heads and say: "Glory be to You, O Allah, and with Your praise (سُبْحَانَكَ اللهُمّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ)." وَتَظُنُّونَ إِنْ لَبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** (and you will think that you tarried but a little while.) and you deem the duration of your stay (لبثكم) short.

(٥٣) وَقُلْ لِعِبادِي ** (53) And tell My servants meaning the believers. يَقُولُوا الَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ ** to speak what is best. that is, they should say to the polytheists (المشركين) the word that is best, and they should not address them with that which provokes and angers them. إِنَّ الشَّيْطانَ يَنْزَغُ بَيْنَهُمْ ** Indeed, Satan incites discord among them. He stirs up contention (المراء) and evil (الشرّ) between them, for perhaps harshness (المخاشنة) (3) towards them may lead to obstinacy (العناد) and an increase in corruption (الفساد). إِنَّ الشَّيْطانَ كانَ لِلْإِنْسانِ عَدُوًّا مُبِيناً ** Indeed, Satan is a manifest enemy to man. manifest in enmity (ظاهر العداوة).

رَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِكُمْ إِنْ يَشَأْ يَرْحَمْكُمْ أَوْ إِنْ يَشَأْ يُعَذِّبْكُمْ ** (Your Lord knows you best. If He wishes, He will have mercy on you, or if He wishes, He will punish you.) It was said that this is the interpretation (تفسير) of [the command to use] الَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ (that which is best), and what is between them is an interjection (اعتراض). That is, they should say this word (الكلمة) and its like to them, and they should not state explicitly that they are among the people of the Fire (أهل النار), for that will incite them to evil, despite the fact that the conclusion of their affair is unseen (غيب), which no one knows except Allah. وَما أَرْسَلْناكَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَكِيلاً ** (We have not sent you as a trustee over them,) [Meaning, you are not] entrusted (موكولاً) with their affair, [so that] you compel them to believe (الإِيمان). Rather, We only sent you as a bearer of good news (مبشراً) and a warner (نذيراً). So, leave them and command your companions to endure (بالاحتمال) from them.

(٥٥) وَرَبُّكَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (55) And your Lord knows best those who are in the heavens and the earth. and their conditions, so He chooses from among them for His prophethood and His guardianship (ولايته) whoever is worthy of both of them. This is a refutation of the improbability felt by Quraysh that the orphan of Abu Talib should be a prophet, and that the poor should be His companions. وَلَقَدْ فَضَّلْنا بَعْضَ النَّبِيِّينَ عَلى بَعْضٍ وَآتَيْنا داوُدَ زَبُوراً.** (We have certainly preferred some prophets over others, and We gave David the Psalms.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The masters of the prophets and messengers are five, and they are the possessors of resolve (أولو العزم) among the Messengers, and upon them the millstone revolved: Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and upon all the prophets.

And in al-Ilal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty preferred His Messenger-Prophets over the near-stationed angels (الملائكة المقربين), and He preferred me over all the prophets and messengers. And the preference after me is for you, O Ali, and for the Imams from your progeny. And indeed, the angels are servants to us and servants to those who love us." (The حديث).

footnote (١) It is said: *أنغض رأسه ينغضه* and *نغض برأسه ينغضه نغضاً* when he moved it. They said: And *النّغض* is the movement of the head with rising and falling (*بارتفاع وانخفاض*) (M.N.).

footnote (٢) *نفضت الثوب والشّجر انفضه نفضاً* (I shook the garment and the tree, *أنفضه نفضاً*) means I moved it so that it would shake off (*لينفض*).

footnote (٣) *خشن* (like *كرم*), *خشناً* and *خشانة*, is the opposite of being soft (*لان*). And *خاشنه* is the opposite of being gentle with him (*لا ينه*).

**(٥٦) قُلِ ادْعُوا الَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ فَلا يَمْلِكُونَ كَشْفَ الضُّرِّ عَنْكُمْ وَلا تَحْوِيلاً ** (56) Say, 'Invoke those whom you claim besides Him, yet they cannot remove any distress from you, nor shift it.'

قُلِ ادْعُوا الَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُمْ ** that they are gods (آلهة) مِنْ دُونِهِ ** like the angels (كالملائكة), the Messiah (والمسيح), and Uzayr (وعزيز) فَلا يَمْلِكُونَ ** so they are not able (يستطيعون) كَشْفَ الضُّرِّ عَنْكُمْ ** like sickness (كالمرض), poverty (والفقرِ), and drought (والقحط) **وَلا تَحْوِيلاً ** nor shifting that from you to others besides you.

(٥٧) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ يَبْتَغُونَ إِلى رَبِّهِمُ الْوَسِيلَةَ ** (57) Those whom they invoke seek the means of approach to their Lord— These deities seek nearness (القربة) to Allah through obedience. أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ ** (57 cont.) [competing] as to which of them is nearer— Meaning, the one who is nearer than them to Allah seeks the means of approach (الوسيلة), so how about the one who is not the nearest? وَيَرْجُونَ رَحْمَتَهُ وَيَخافُونَ عَذابَهُ ** (57 cont.) and hope for His mercy and fear His punishment. Like the rest of the servants (العباد), so how can they claim that they are deities (آلهة)? إِنَّ عَذابَ رَبِّكَ كانَ مَحْذُوراً ** (57 cont.) Indeed, the punishment of your Lord is a thing to beware of. Worthy of being feared by everyone, even the angels (الملائكة) and the Messengers (الرسل).

(٥٨) **وَإِنْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ إِلَّا نَحْنُ مُهْلِكُوها قَبْلَ يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ أَوْ مُعَذِّبُوها عَذاباً شَدِيداً كانَ ذلِكَ فِي الْكِتابِ مَسْطُوراً ** (58) There is no town but We will destroy it before the Day of Resurrection or punish it with a severe punishment. That has been inscribed in the Book.

In the Preserved Tablet (اللوح المحفوظ). مَسْطُوراً ** (inscribed) means written (مكتوباً).

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "It is annihilation through death."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The community (أُمّة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is but one of the communities (الأمم), so whoever dies has perished (هلك)."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "By killing, death, and other means."

(٥٩) وَما مَنَعَنا أَنْ نُرْسِلَ بِالْآياتِ which Quraysh proposed إِلَّا أَنْ كَذَّبَ بِهَا الْأَوَّلُونَ ** (59) Nothing prevented Us from sending the signs except that the former peoples denied them. except the denial of the former peoples who are their likes, such as 'Ad and Thamud, and that if they [the signs] were sent, they would deny them just as those denied, and they would necessitate the swift, eradicating punishment (العذاب العاجل المستأصل). And part of His judgment (حكمه) concerning this Ummah is that He does not punish them with the punishment of eradication (الاستيصال), in honor of His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, just as He said: وَما كانَ اللهُ لِيُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَأَنْتَ فِيهِمْ.

Al-Qummi narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was asked by his people to bring them a sign, so Gabriel descended and said: "Indeed, Allah says: وَما مَنَعَنا أَنْ نُرْسِلَ بِالْآياتِ إِلَّا أَنْ كَذَّبَ بِهَا الْأَوَّلُونَ And when We sent a sign to a town and they did not believe in it, We destroyed them. Therefore, We delayed the signs from your people." وَآتَيْنا ثَمُودَ النَّاقَةَ by their request مُبْصِرَةً a clear sign (آية بيّنة) فَظَلَمُوا بِها meaning they wronged themselves because of slaughtering it **وَما نُرْسِلُ بِالْآياتِ إِلَّا تَخْوِيفاً ** (60) We gave Thamud the She-camel as a visible sign, but they wronged by it. We do not send the signs except as a warning. and an admonition regarding the punishment of the Hereafter, for the matter of those to whom you were sent is delayed until the Day of Resurrection.

(60) وَإِذْ قُلْنا لَكَ ** We revealed to you إِنَّ رَبَّكَ أَحاطَ بِالنَّاسِ ** (60) And when We said to you, 'Your Lord encompasses all mankind' for they are within the grasp of His power. And it was said that this refers to Quraysh, meaning He destroyed them, as the enemy encompasses them. That is, He destroyed them, meaning We gave you glad tidings of the Battle of Badr and your victory over them, which is His saying: سَيُهْزَمُ الْجَمْعُ وَيُوَلُّونَ الدُّبُرَ سَتُغْلَبُونَ وَتُحْشَرُونَ إِلى جَهَنَّمَ (The host will be routed and turn their backs. You will be defeated and mustered toward hell). So He, the Glorified, made it as if it had already occurred, according to His custom in His tidings. وَما جَعَلْنَا الرُّؤْيَا الَّتِي أَرَيْناكَ إِلَّا فِتْنَةً لِلنَّاسِ وَالشَّجَرَةَ الْمَلْعُونَةَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ ** (And We did not make the vision that We showed you except as a trial for the people, and the Cursed Tree in the Qur’an) This is grammatically conjoined (عطف) to the vision (الرّؤيا). وَنُخَوِّفُهُمْ (And We warn them) with various types of warnings. فَما يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا طُغْياناً كَبِيراً ** (but it increases them only in great rebellion) except for arrogance (عتوّاً) that exceeds the limit.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He was asked about the Almighty's saying: وَما جَعَلْنَا الرُّؤْيَا الَّتِي أَرَيْناكَ ** (And We did not make the vision that We showed you). He said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saw men from Banu Taym and Banu Adiyy upon the pulpits, turning the people back from the straight path (الصراط) in retreat (القهقرى)." It was asked: وَالشَّجَرَةَ الْمَلْعُونَةَ ** (And the Cursed Tree)? He said: "They are Banu Umayyah." And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), a similar narration, except that He said: "He saw men upon the pulpits turning the people into misguidance (ضلالاً): Zurayq and Zufar." I say: These two (زريق and زفر) are euphemisms (كنايتان) for the first two, and Taym and Adiyy are their grandfathers, He said.

And in another narration from Him (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saw men of fire upon pulpits of fire, turning the people back upon their heels in retreat (القهقرى). He said: "And we do not name anyone." And in another: "We do not name the men, but the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saw a people upon His pulpit misleading the people after Him from the straight path (الصراط) in retreat (القهقرى)." And in another narration, He said: "I saw tonight the boys (صبيان) of Banu Umayyah climbing upon this pulpit of mine. So I said: 'O Lord, is this during my time?' He said: 'No, but after you.'" And in al-Kafi, narrated from one of the two (Imams) (peace be upon them): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم awoke one day distressed (كئيباً) and sorrowful (حزيناً). So Ali (peace be upon him) said to Him: "Why do I see you, O Messenger of Allah, distressed and sorrowful?" He said: "How can I not be so, when I saw in this night of mine that Banu Taym, Banu Adiyy, and Banu Umayyah were ascending this pulpit of mine, turning the people back from Islam in retreat (القهقرى)?" So I said: "O Lord, is this during my life or after my death?" He said: "After your death."

I say: The meaning of this report is widespread (مستفيض) among the Khassah (Shi'a) and the Ammah (Sunnis). However, the Ammah narrated sometimes: that He saw a people from Banu Umayyah climbing His pulpit and leaping upon it like the leaping of monkeys. So He said: "That is their share of the world, which they are given due to their acceptance of Islam." And other times: that monkeys were ascending and descending His pulpit, which displeased Him and caused Him grief. Al-Qummi said: It was revealed when the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saw in his sleep as if monkeys were ascending His pulpit, which displeased Him and caused Him intense grief. So Allah revealed: وَما جَعَلْنَا الرُّؤْيَا الَّتِي أَرَيْناكَ إِلَّا فِتْنَةً ** (And We did not make the vision that We showed you except as a trial) so that they might wander blindly (ليعمهوا) (1). And وَالشَّجَرَةَ الْمَلْعُونَةَ (And the Cursed Tree) was revealed thus, and they are Banu Umayyah. And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): وَما جَعَلْنَا الرُّؤْيَا الَّتِي أَرَيْناكَ إِلَّا فِتْنَةً ** (And We did not make the vision that We showed you except as a trial) for them, so that they might wander blindly (ليعمهوا) in it. وَالشَّجَرَةَ الْمَلْعُونَةَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ ** (And the Cursed Tree in the Qur’an) means Banu Umayyah. And an implicit narration (مضمراً): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم slept and saw that Banu Umayyah were ascending His pulpit, turning the people away. Whenever a man from them ascended, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saw humiliation (الذلّة) and wretchedness (المسكنة). He awoke distressed (جزوعاً) by that. Those whom He saw were twelve men from Banu Umayyah. Then Gabriel came to Him with this Ayah. Then Gabriel said: 'Banu Umayyah will not possess anything except that the dominion of the People of the House (أهل البيت) will be twice that amount.'"

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition, He said: "Indeed, Mu'awiyah and his son will rule it after Uthman. Then seven from the progeny of al-Hakam ibn Abi al-As will rule it, one after the other, completing the twelve Imams of misguidance (أئمة ضلالة). They are those whom the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saw upon His pulpit, turning the Ummah back upon their heels in retreat (القهقرى). Ten of them are from Banu Umayyah, and two men established that for them, and upon these two are the burdens (أوزار) of this Ummah until the Day of Resurrection." And in the introduction of al-Sahifah al-Sajjadiyyah, narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father, from his grandfather: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was overcome by drowsiness (نعسة) (1) while He was upon His pulpit, and He saw in His sleep men leaping upon His pulpit like the leaping of monkeys, turning the people back upon their heels in retreat (القهقرى). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sat up straight, and sorrow was evident on His face. Then Gabriel came to Him with this Ayah: وَما جَعَلْنَا الرُّؤْيَا الَّتِي أَرَيْناكَ ** (And We did not make the vision that We showed you) [the rest of] the Ayah, meaning Banu Umayyah. He said: "O Gabriel, will this be during my covenant and in my time?" He said: "No, but the millstone (رحى) of Islam will turn from the time of your emigration (مهاجرك), and it will remain thus for ten [years]. Then the millstone of Islam will turn at the head of thirty-five [years] from your emigration, and it will remain thus for five [years]. Then there must be a millstone of misguidance (رحى ضلالة) standing upon its axis, followed by the dominion of the Pharaohs (ملك الفراعنة)." He said: And Allah revealed concerning that: إِنَّا أَنْزَلْناهُ فِي لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ وَما أَدْراكَ ما لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ خَيْرٌ مِنْ أَلْفِ شَهْرٍ ** (Indeed, We sent it down on the Night of Ordainment. And what will show you what is the Night of Ordainment? The Night of Ordainment is better than a thousand months) Banu Umayyah will rule during this period, and there is no Night of Ordainment (ليلة القدر) within it. He said: So Allah informed His Prophet that Banu Umayyah would possess the authority (سلطان) of this Ummah, and their dominion would last the length of this period. Even if the mountains were to challenge them, they would overcome them, until Allah permits the removal of their dominion. And with that, they harbor enmity toward us, the People of the House (أهل البيت), and hatred for us. Allah informed His Prophet of what the People of the House of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and those who love them and their followers (شيعتهم) would suffer from them during their days and their rule.

I say: The reason He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was shown the people being turned back from Islam in retreat (القهقرى) is that the people were outwardly manifesting Islam and praying toward the Qiblah. Yet, despite this, they were departing from Islam little by little, like one who retreats backward (القهقرى) from the straight path (الصراط السّويّ), with his face toward the truth, until, when he reaches the limit of his striving, he finds himself in Hellfire (الجحيم). And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Hasan ibn Ali (peace be upon them) in a tradition: He said to Marwan ibn al-Hakam: "As for you, O Marwan, it was not I who cursed you (2) nor cursed your father, but Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, cursed you and cursed your father, your household (أهل بيتك), and your progeny, and whatever issues from your father's loins until the Day of Resurrection, upon the tongue of His Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. By Allah, O Marwan, neither you nor anyone present denies this curse upon you and your father before you from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And Allah has not increased you, O Marwan, by what He warned you of, except in great rebellion (طغياناً كبيراً). And Allah spoke the truth, and His Messenger spoke the truth, by the saying of Allah, the Almighty: وَالشَّجَرَةَ الْمَلْعُونَةَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ وَنُخَوِّفُهُمْ فَما يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا طُغْياناً كَبِيراً (and the Cursed Tree in the Qur’an. And We warn them, but it increases them only in great rebellion). And you, O Marwan, and your progeny, are الشَّجَرَةَ الْمَلْعُونَةَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ ** (the Cursed Tree in the Qur’an), according to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." And from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition: He made the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) who uphold it and act upon its manifest and hidden meanings to be from a tree: أَصْلُها ثابِتٌ وَفَرْعُها فِي السَّماءِ تُؤْتِي أُكُلَها كُلَّ حِينٍ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّها ** (whose root is firm and whose branches are in the sky. It yields its fruit every season by the leave of its Lord) That is, knowledge such as this appears to those who bear it, time after time, by the leave of its Lord. And He made its enemies the people of the Cursed Tree, who attempted to extinguish the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah refuses except to perfect His light. If the hypocrites (may Allah curse them) knew what they would incur by abandoning these verses whose interpretation I have clarified for you, they would have removed them along with what they already removed from it. I say: And in His saying, the Glorified: فَما يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا طُغْياناً كَبِيراً ** (but it increases them only in great rebellion) there is a subtlety (لطافة) that is not hidden.

(1) It is said: ‘amaha (عمَهَ) in his rebellion (طغيانه) ‘amahan (عمهاً) from the category (بَاب) of ta‘iba (تعب) when he hesitated, bewildered. From this comes ‘āmih (عامِه) and ‘amih (عمِه), meaning bewildered, deviating from the path. Thus, al-‘amah (العمه) is specifically in opinion (الرأي).

(1) Al-Nu‘ās (النّعاس) with a ḍammah (بالضّم) is drowsiness (الوسن) and the beginning of sleep. It is a gentle breeze that comes from the brain, covering the eye but not reaching the heart. If it reaches the heart, it is sleep (نوماً). And na‘astu (نعست) with a fatḥah (بالفتح) an‘asu nu‘āsan (انعس نعاساً), and na‘isa yan‘asu (نعس ينعس) from the category (باب) of qatala (قتل). And a man who is nā‘is (ناعس) is drowsy (وسنان).

(2) Sabbahu (سبّه) means he cut him off, and he stabbed him in the subbah (السّبة), meaning the anus (الاست). And he insulted him sabban (سبّاً).

(٦١) وَإِذْ قُلْنا لِلْمَلائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِآدَمَ فَسَجَدُوا إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ قالَ أَأَسْجُدُ (١) **لِمَنْ خَلَقْتَ طِيناً ** (1) And when We said to the angels, 'Prostrate before Adam,' they prostrated, but not Iblis. He said, 'Shall I prostrate (1) before him whom You created out of clay?' Its interpretation has preceded.

(٦٢) قالَ أَرَأَيْتَكَ هذَا الَّذِي كَرَّمْتَ عَلَيَ ** meaning, inform me concerning this one whom You honored above me—that is, You preferred him and chose him above me—[why] You chose him above me while I am better than him. So, [the question] was omitted for brevity. لَئِنْ أَخَّرْتَنِ إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ ** is a new statement, and the ل (lām) is for oath. لَأَحْتَنِكَنَ (٢) ذُرِّيَّتَهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** (2) He said, 'Do You see this one whom You have honoured above me? If You respite me until the Day of Resurrection, I will surely bring his progeny under my sway, all but a few.' meaning, I will surely root them out through misguidance (الإغواء) and I will surely gain dominance over them, except for a few whose tranquility (سكينتهم) I am unable to resist.

(63) He said: قالَ اذْهَبْ ** (He said, 'Begone!) Proceed (امض) for what you intended, which is expulsion and leaving him [Iblis] alone with what his soul tempted him to do. A narration concerning this meaning has already preceded in Surat al-A'raf. فَمَنْ تَبِعَكَ مِنْهُمْ فَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ جَزاؤُكُمْ ** (So whoever of them follows you, indeed hell shall be your requital,) meaning: your requital (جزاؤك) and their requital (جزاؤهم). Thus, He made the second person plural (المخاطب) prevail over the third person plural (الغائب). جَزاءً مَوْفُوراً ** (an ample requital.) meaning: completed (مُكمّلاً).

footnote (١) So it is an interrogative statement (*استفهام*) carrying the meaning of denial (*الإنكار*), meaning: How can I prostrate to him when I am superior to him, and my origin is nobler than his origin? In this, there is an indication that Iblis understood from that [command] the preference of Adam over the angels. Were it not for that, his refusal to prostrate would have no justification (*وجه*). The reason it was permissible for Him, glory be to Him, to command them to prostrate to Adam, but not permissible for Him to command them to worship him, is that prostration (*السجود*) is arranged in reverence (*التّعظيم*) according to what is intended by it. Worship (*العبادة*) is not like that, as it is submission (*خضوع*) of the heart above which there is no submission, because it is arranged in reverence for its own kind (*لجنسه*). What clarifies this is that if one were to prostrate forgetfully (*ساهياً*), it would not hold a status of reverence (*منزلة في التّعظيم*) compared to other actions of the limbs (*أفعال الجوارح*). (*م ن*).

footnote (٢) *Al-Ihtinak* (*الاحتناك*) is the cutting off from the root (*الأصل*). It is said: "So-and-so performed *ihtinak* (*احتنك*) on what so-and-so possessed of wealth or knowledge" if he thoroughly investigated it and took all of it. And the locusts performed *ihtinak* (*احتنك*) on the crops if they ate all of it. It is also said that it derives from their saying: "He *hanaka* (*حنك*) the beast" if he placed a rope in its lower jaw (*حنكها الأسفل*) by which he leads it. (*م ن*).

(٦٤) وَاسْتَفْزِزْ And make light of (or: incite) مَنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ مِنْهُمْ ** whom you are able to incite. And al-fazz (الفزّ) is the light/agile one. بِصَوْتِكَ (with your voice) by your call to corruption. وَأَجْلِبْ عَلَيْهِمْ ** And shout upon them, from al-jalabah (الجلبة) which is shouting, بِخَيْلِكَ وَرَجِلِكَ ** with your horsemen and your footmen, so make them bold against them. This is a metaphor for his dominance over those whom he misleads, likened to one who shouts at a people, incites them from their places, and shouts upon them with his soldiers until he uproots them. وَشارِكْهُمْ فِي الْأَمْوالِ ** by inducing them to earn it and gather it from the unlawful (الحرام) and spend it on what is inappropriate وَالْأَوْلادِ.

وَاسْتَفْزِزْ مَنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ مِنْهُمْ بِصَوْتِكَ وَأَجْلِبْ عَلَيْهِمْ بِخَيْلِكَ وَرَجِلِكَ وَشارِكْهُمْ فِي الْأَمْوالِ وَالْأَوْلادِ. (64) Incite with your voice whomever of them you can, and rally against them your cavalry and infantry, and share with them in [their] wealth and children.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) said: "Indeed, Allah has forbidden Paradise to every foul-mouthed, obscene (بذيّ) (1) person who has little modesty, who cares not what He says nor what is said to him. If you investigate him, you will find him only to be a product of fornication (لغيّة) (2) or the partnership of Satan." It was asked: "O Messenger of Allah, is there a partnership of Satan among people?" He replied: "Do you not read the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فِي الْأَمْوالِ وَالْأَوْلادِ. (in [their] wealth and children)?"

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He recited this verse, then said: "Indeed, Satan comes until he sits with the woman just as the man sits with her, and he ejaculates (يحدث) just as the man ejaculates, and he copulates (ينكح) just as the man copulates." It was asked: "By what is that known?" He said: "By love for us and hatred for us. So, whoever loves us is the seed (نطفة) of the servant, and whoever hates us is the seed (نطفة) of Satan."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "If the Name of Allah is mentioned, Satan withdraws. But if he performs the act and does not mention [the Name of Allah], he (Satan) inserts his penis (ذكره), and the act is performed by both of them together, while the seed (النطفة) is one."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the two seeds (النطفتين), that of the human and that of Satan, when they partner. He replied: "Sometimes [the child] is created from one of them, and sometimes it is created from both of them together."

And al-Qummi said: "Whatever wealth is unlawful (حرام) is the partnership of Satan. If he buys slave-girls (الإماء) with it and copulates with them, and a child is born to him, then everything she gives birth to is the partnership of Satan. He (Satan) is with the man when he has intercourse, and the child is from his seed (نطفته) and the man's seed (نطفة الرجل) if the act was unlawful (حراماً)."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The like of it.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "If a man commits fornication (زنى), Satan inserts his penis (ذكره), then they both act together, then the two seeds (النطفتان) mix, and Allah creates from them, and thus it is the partnership of Satan." The narrations concerning this meaning are numerous. وَعِدْهُمْ (And promise them) (1) the false promises, such as the intercession of the deities (الآلهة) and the delaying of repentance due to prolonged hope. وَمَا يَعِدُهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ إِلَّا غُرُورًا (and Satan promises them nothing but delusion). This is an interjection (اعتراض). And al-ghurūr (الغرور) (delusion) is the beautification of error with what suggests that it is correct.

(1) Al-Badhī (البذيّ), like (رضيّ), is the foul man. And (بذوت عليهم) and (ابذيتهم) derive from al-badhā’ (البذاء), which is ugly speech. (ق)

(2) The author (may Allah have mercy on him) said in al-Wafi, explaining li-ghiyyah (لغيّة) (with a kasrah on the dotted letter (الغيّة) and a shaddah on the lower muthannā (التحتانية)): it means fornication (الزّنا). It is said: "So-and-so is li-ghiyyah" (لغيّة) in contrast to "So-and-so is li-rushdah" (لرشدة) (with a kasrah on the rā’). The meaning of Satan's partnership with man in wealth is his inducing him to acquire it from the unlawful (الحرام) and spend it on what is impermissible, and on what is impermissible in terms of extravagance (الإِسراف), stinginess (التقتير), miserliness (البخل), and wastefulness (التّبذير). And his partnership in children is his insertion with him during intercourse (النّكاح) if he does not mention the Name of Allah, while the seed (النطفة) is one.

(65) إِنَّ عِبادِي (Indeed, My servants) means the sincere ones (المخلصين), based on the context of the attribution (الإضافة) to Himself, and due to His saying: إِلَّا عِبادَكَ مِنْهُمُ الْمُخْلَصِينَ* لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطانٌ (except Your servants among them, the sincere ones. You have no authority over them) meaning, you are not able to mislead them because they are not deceived by you. وَكَفى بِرَبِّكَ وَكِيلاً (Your Lord suffices as a guardian.) for them. They rely upon Him in seeking refuge from you, so He protects them from your evil.

Al-Ayyashi narrated, implicitly regarding this verse: "It was revealed concerning Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), and we hope that it applies to whoever among His servants Allah loves."

In Nahj al-Balagha: "So beware of the enemy of Allah, lest he misleads you with his affliction (دائه) and lest he incites you with his cavalry (خيله) and his infantry (رجله)." He said: "By Allah, he has indeed boasted over your origin (أصلكم), attacked your nobility (حَسَبكم), and disparaged your lineage (نسبكم). He has gathered his cavalry (خيله) against you and directed his infantry (رجله) toward your path. They hunt you down (2) in every place and strike every fingertip (بنان) of yours. You cannot defend yourselves with a stratagem (حيلة) nor repel them with resolve (عزيمة) in the midst of humiliation (ذلّ), a circle of distress (ضيق), a field of death (موت), and a round of tribulation (بلاءٍ)."

(٦٦) رَبُّكُمُ الَّذِي يُزْجِي (66) Your Lord is He who drives

*He is the One Who makes [it] run *لَكُمُ الْفُلْكَ فِي الْبَحْرِ the ships for you in the sea,

(٣) لِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** (3) that you may seek of His bounty.**

[This refers to] the wind and the types of goods (الأمتعة) which are not available to you. إِنَّهُ كانَ بِكُمْ رَحِيماً Indeed, He is merciful to you, since He prepared for you what you need and made easy for you what was difficult among its means (أسبابه).

(Your Lord is He who drives the ships for you in the sea, that you may seek of His bounty. Indeed, He is merciful to you.)

(٦٧) وَإِذا مَسَّكُمُ الضُّرُّ فِي الْبَحْرِ ** (67) And when distress befalls you in the sea,** (fear of drowning)

ضَلَّ مَنْ تَدْعُونَ إِلَّا إِيَّاهُ ** those whom you invoke vanish, except Him. Everyone whom you invoke during your calamities vanishes from your minds, Him alone, so you do not hope for salvation there except from Him. A narrative concerning this meaning has already preceded in Surat al-Fatihah.

Then when He saved you from drowning إِلَى الْبَرِّ أَعْرَضْتُمْ ** to the land, you turn away from Monotheism (التوحيد) and you expand in ingratitude for the favor.

وَكانَ الْإِنْسانُ كَفُوراً ** and man is ungrateful. This is like a justification (التعليل) for the turning away.

footnote (١) This is a rebuke (*زجر*) and a threat (*تهديد*) in the form of a command (*الأمر*). (م.)

footnote (٢) *قنصه يقنصه* means He hunted him (*صاده*). (ق.)

footnote (٣) [This refers to] what He created of the winds and by making the water in a manner that allows ships to sail upon it, **that you may seek His grace** (لِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ), meaning: that you may seek the grace of Allah, the Exalted, by riding the ships upon the surface of the water for that which contains the rectification (*صلاح*) of your worldly life, such as trade (*التّجارة*), or the rectification (*صلاح*) of your religion, such as raiding (*الغزو*). (*Majma‘ al-Bayan*).

(٦٨) أَفَأَمِنْتُمْ Did you escape from drowning, so you felt secure? أَنْ يَخْسِفَ بِكُمْ جانِبَ الْبَرِّ That Allah should overturn it while you are upon it. For indeed, He Who was able to destroy you in the sea by drowning is able to destroy you on land by swallowing up (بالخسف) and other means. And it was recited with the nūn (بالنون) in this [verb] and in the four [verbs] that follow it. And in mentioning the 'side' (الجانب) there is an alert that as soon as they reached the shore (الساحل), they disbelieved and turned away. أَوْ يُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ حاصِباً A wind that taḥṣub (تحصُب), meaning it throws pebbles (بالحصاة). ثُمَّ لا تَجِدُوا لَكُمْ وَكِيلاً Who protects you from that, for there is no repeller of His action.

أَفَأَمِنْتُمْ أَنْ يَخْسِفَ بِكُمْ جانِبَ الْبَرِّ أَوْ يُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ حاصِباً ثُمَّ لا تَجِدُوا لَكُمْ وَكِيلاً (Do you feel secure that He will not make the side of the land swallow you up, or send against you a stone-shower, then you will not find for yourselves a guardian?)

(٦٩) أَمْ أَمِنْتُمْ أَنْ يُعِيدَكُمْ فِيهِ تارَةً أُخْرى ** (Or do you feel secure that He will not send you back into it another time) (The pronoun فِيهِ refers) to the sea (في البحر), (meaning) by strengthening your motives (دواعيكم) to return and ride the sea. فَيُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ قاصِفاً مِنَ الرِّيحِ ** (and send against you a violent wind) which does not pass by anything except that it shatters (قصفته) it, meaning breaks it (كسرته). Al-Qummi narrates from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It (هي) is the violent wind (العاصف). فَيُغْرِقَكُمْ بِما كَفَرْتُمْ ** (and drown you because of your ingratitude) because of your polytheism (اشراككم) or your ingratitude (كفرانكم) for the blessing of salvation (نعمة الانجاءِ). ثُمَّ لا تَجِدُوا لَكُمْ عَلَيْنا بِهِ تَبِيعاً ** (Then you will not find for yourselves any avenger against Us for it.) a claimant (مطالباً) who follows Us seeking vengeance (بانتصار) or averting (صرف) (the punishment).
