(٧٠) وَلَقَدْ كَرَّمْنا بَنِي آدَمَ ** (70) And We have certainly honored the Children of Adam by intellect (العقل), speech (النطق), beautiful form (الصورة الحسنة), moderate stature (القامة المعتدلة), managing the affairs of livelihood (المعاش) and the Hereafter (المعاد), dominance over what is on the earth, subjugation of all other animals, and the ability to perform crafts (الصناعات), besides other things that cannot be enumerated. وَحَمَلْناهُمْ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ ** and carried them on land and sea, upon beasts (الدوابّ) and ships (السفن). وَرَزَقْناهُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّباتِ ** and provided them with good things, delightful things (المستلذات). وَفَضَّلْناهُمْ عَلى كَثِيرٍ مِمَّنْ خَلَقْنا تَفْضِيلاً and preferred them with a decided preference over many of those whom We have created. In al-Amali, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: "We preferred the Children of Adam over the rest of creation. وَحَمَلْناهُمْ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ (and carried them on land and sea,) He says: upon the wet (الرطب) and the dry (اليابس). وَرَزَقْناهُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّباتِ (and provided them with good things,) He says: from all the good fruits (طيبات الثمار). وَفَضَّلْناهُمْ (and preferred them) He says: There is no beast (دابّة) or bird (طائر) except that it eats and drinks with its mouth (فيها), and does not raise food or drink to its mouth with its hand, except for the son of Adam, for he raises his food to his mouth with his hand. This is part of the preference (التفضيل)." And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): وَفَضَّلْناهُمْ عَلى كَثِيرٍ ** (and preferred them over many) He said: "Everything was created bent over (منكباً) except for man, who was created upright (منتصباً)." And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah does not honor the soul (روح) of a disbeliever (كافر), but Allah has honored the souls (أرواح) of the believers (المؤمنين). The honor (كرامة) of the soul (النفس) and the blood (الدم) is only through the spirit (الرّوح), and the good provision (الرزق الطيب) is knowledge (العلم)." And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) regarding the form (صورة) of human beings (الآدميّين): "It is the most honored form (أكرم صورة) in the sight of Allah."

(٧١) يَوْمَ نَدْعُوا كُلَّ أُناسٍ بِإِمامِهِمْ ** (The day We shall call every group of people by their leader) By the one whom they followed, whether a Prophet, or a successor (وصيّ), or a wretched one (شقي). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: بِإِمامِهِمْ ** (by their leader) means the one who is present among them, and He is the Qā’im (قائم) of the people of his time. And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم will come with his people, and ‘Ali (peace be upon him) with his people, and al-Hasan (peace be upon him) with his people, and al-Husayn (peace be upon him) with his people. And everyone who died among the midst of a people will come with them. And in al-Ayyashi: That which is close to its meaning. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When this Ayah was revealed, the Muslims said: "O Messenger of Allah, are You not the leader (إمام) of all people, all of them?" He said: "I am the Messenger of Allah to all people, all of them. But there will be leaders (أئمّة) after me over the people, appointed by Allah, from my Household (أهل بيتي). They will rise among the people, but they will be denied, and the leaders (أئمّة) of disbelief (الكفر) and misguidance (الضلال) and their followers (أشياعهم) will oppress them. So, whoever loves them (والاهم), follows them, and believes them, he is from me and with me, and he will meet me. Beware! And whoever oppresses them and denies them, he is not from me nor with me, and I am innocent of him." And in al-Majalis, narrated from al-Husayn (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "A leader (إمام) who called to guidance (هدىً), and they responded to him; and a leader (إمام) who called to misguidance (ضلالة), and they responded to him. These are in Paradise, and those are in the Fire." And this is the Almighty's saying: فَرِيقٌ فِي الْجَنَّةِ وَفَرِيقٌ فِي السَّعِيرِ. (A group will be in paradise and a group in the Blaze.) And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Every group of people will be called by their leader (إمام): the followers of the sun (أصحاب الشمس) by the sun, the followers of the moon (أصحاب القَمَر) by the moon, the followers of the fire (أصحاب النار) by the fire, and the followers of the stones (أصحاب الحجارة) by the stones. And in al-Mahasin, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "By Allah, you are upon the religion of Allah." Then he recited this Ayah. Then he said: "‘Ali (peace be upon him) is our leader (إمامنا), and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is our leader (إمامنا). And how many a leader (إمام) will come on the Day of Resurrection cursing his followers, and they cursing him." And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Do you not praise Allah? When the Day of Resurrection comes, every people will be called to the one whom they followed (يتولّونه). And we will hasten to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and you will hasten to us. So, where do you think He will take you? To Paradise, by the Lord of the Ka‘bah!" He said this three times. فَمَنْ أُوتِيَ كِتابَهُ بِيَمِينِهِ فَأُولئِكَ يَقْرَؤُنَ كِتابَهُمْ ** (But as for him who is given his book in his right hand, they will read their book) Rejoicing in what they see therein. وَلا يُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلاً ** (and they will not be wronged so much as a thread.) And they will not be diminished in their rewards by the slightest thing. And al-fatīl (الفتيل) is the twisted thread (المفتول) that is in the groove of the date stone (شقّ النّواة).

(٧٢) وَمَنْ كانَ فِي هذِهِ أَعْمى ** (72) And whoever has been blind in this [world] [Meaning] blind of heart (أعمى القلب), who does not perceive his guidance (رشده) nor is guided to the path of salvation (طريق النّجاة). فَهُوَ فِي الْآخِرَةِ أَعْمى وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلاً he will be blind in the Hereafter, and further astray from the way. He will not be guided to the path of Paradise (طريق الجنّة).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: "Whoever is not guided by the creation of the heavens and the earth, the alternation of the night and the day, the rotation of the celestial sphere (دوران الفلك), the sun and the moon, and the wondrous signs (الآيات العجيبات), to the fact that beyond that is an affair greater than it, فَهُوَ فِي الْآخِرَةِ أَعْمى وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلاً. he will be blind in the Hereafter, and further astray from the way."

And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): "Beware of the saying of the ignorant (الجهال), the people of blindness (العمى) and misguidance (الضلال), who claim that Allah, majestic and sanctified is He, is existent in the Hereafter for the reckoning (الحساب), reward (الثواب), and punishment (العقاب), but is not existent in this world for obedience (الطاعة) and hope (الرجاء). And if there were any deficiency (نقص) or degradation (اهتضام) for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in existence, He would never be found in the Hereafter. But the people have wandered, become blind, and become deaf to the truth (الحق) from where they do not know. And that is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَمَنْ كانَ فِي هذِهِ أَعْمى فَهُوَ فِي الْآخِرَةِ أَعْمى وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلاً (And whoever has been blind in this [world] he will be blind in the Hereafter, and further astray from the way), meaning: blind to the existing realities (الحقائق الموجودة)."

And in al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "The severest blindness (أشدّ العمى) is that of one who is blind to our merit (فضلنا) and harbors enmity (العداوة) toward us without any sin preceding it from us, except that we called [people] to the truth (الحق) while others besides us called him to discord (الفتنة) and the world (الدنيا). So he came to both of them [discord and the world] and established disavowal (البراءة) of us and enmity."

And in al-Kafi, al-Ayyashi, and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "That is the one who procrastinates regarding the Hajj (الحجّ)—meaning the obligatory Hajj of Islam (حجّة الإسلام)—until death comes to him."

(٧٣) وَإِنْ كادُوا لَيَفْتِنُونَكَ ** (73) They were indeed about to tempt you They came close, through their exaggeration, to causing you to fall into temptation by seeking to make you deviate عَنِ الَّذِي أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ ** away from what We have revealed to you, meaning: away from its ruling, لِتَفْتَرِيَ عَلَيْنا غَيْرَهُ ** so that you might fabricate against Us something other than it, [meaning] other than what We revealed to you. Al-Qummi said: It means concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And Al-Ayyashi [narrated] that which is similar in meaning in the coming Ayah: وَإِذاً لَاتَّخَذُوكَ خَلِيلاً ** and then they would have taken you as a friend. And had you followed their desire, they would have shown their friendship. Al-Qummi [said]: meaning they would have taken you as a friend (صديقاً) had you established something other than Him.

** وَلَوْ لا أَنْ ثَبَّتْناكَ لَقَدْ كِدْتَ تَرْكَنُ إِلَيْهِمْ شَيْئاً قَلِيلاً ** (Had We not fortified you, you had almost inclined toward them a little) You had almost inclined toward following their desire.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: When the Day of Conquest (يَوْمُ الْفَتْحِ) occurred, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم removed idols from the Mosque, and among them was an idol upon al-Marwah. Quraysh requested Him to leave it, and it was a transformation (مَسْخاً). So He intended to leave it, then He ordered it to be broken, and then [the Ayah] was revealed.

And in al-Majma': It was said that when this Ayah was revealed, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Allah, never entrust me to my soul for the blink of an eye."

(٧٥) إِذاً لَأَذَقْناكَ ضِعْفَ الْحَياةِ وَضِعْفَ الْمَماتِ ** (75) If that were the case, We would surely have made you taste double [punishment] in life and double [punishment] in death. It was said: meaning, the punishment of the Fire and the punishment of the Hereafter, double what anyone else would be punished with in the two abodes (الدارين) for an action like this, because the error of the eminent (الخطير) is more serious (أخطر). And the original wording was (عذاباً ضعفاً في الحيوة وعذاباً ضعفاً في الممات), meaning multiplied (مضاعفاً). So, the attribute (الصفة) was substituted for the described noun (الموصوف) and was annexed (أضيفت) just as its described noun is annexed. ثُمَّ لا تَجِدُ لَكَ عَلَيْنا نَصِيراً ** then you would not find any helper against Us. who could repel [punishment] from you.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in the tradition concerning al-Ma’mun regarding the infallibility (عصمة) of the Prophets: when he asked him about His saying: عَفَا اللهُ عَنْكَ لِمَ أَذِنْتَ لَهُمْ (May Allah forgive you! Why did you give them leave?), he said: "This is from what was revealed in the manner of 'I mean you, but listen, O neighbor (إِيَّاكَ أَعْنِي وَاسْمَعِي يَا جَارَة)'. Allah the Almighty addressed His Prophet with that, but the intended meaning is his community (أمّته)." And likewise, His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: لَئِنْ أَشْرَكْتَ لَيَحْبَطَنَ عَمَلُكَ وَلَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ ** (65) If you ascribe partners [to Allah], your work shall certainly fail and you shall surely be among the losers.** And the Almighty's saying: لَوْ لا أَنْ ثَبَّتْناكَ لَقَدْ كِدْتَ تَرْكَنُ إِلَيْهِمْ شَيْئاً قَلِيلاً (Had We not fortified you, you were certainly about to incline towards them a little).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whatever Allah reproached His Prophet for in the Quran, He means by it those who have passed away, such as His saying: **وَلَوْ لا أَنْ ثَبَّتْناكَ لَقَدْ كِدْتَ تَرْكَنُ إِلَيْهِمْ شَيْئاً قَلِيلاً ** He meant someone other than him by that."

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in the tradition concerning the Zindiq (الزّنديق) who questioned him about certain matters in the Quran, and among the things he asked him (peace be upon him) about was this verse. [The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said:] "As for what you mentioned regarding the address (الخطاب) that indicates the disparagement (تهجين), contempt (الإِزراء), and reprimand (التأنيب) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, despite what Allah the Almighty has manifested of His preference for him over all other Prophets, [know that] Allah appointed for every Prophet an enemy from among the polytheists (المشركين)." Then he (peace be upon him) mentioned the efforts (مساعي) of his enemies to alter his religion (ملّته), distort the Book (كتابه) he brought, omit what was in it concerning the merit (فضل) of those possessing merit and the disbelief (كفر) of those possessing disbelief, and their abandoning from it what they presumed was for them but was actually against them, and their adding to it what made its inconsistency (تناكره) and contradiction (تنافره) apparent. Then he said: "And that which appeared in the Book concerning the contempt (الإِزراء) towards the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is from the fabrication (فرية) of the atheists (الملحدين)." And this tradition has already passed in full, along with the explanation of the preceding tradition narrated in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi in the Sixth Introduction (المقدّمة السادسة) of this book, together with what constitutes the verification (التحقيق) in this chapter (الباب).

(٧٦) وَإِنْ كادُوا لَيَسْتَفِزُّونَكَ ** (76) And they were about to provoke you (They would disturb you with their enmity) مِنَ الْأَرْضِ. out of the land. Al-Qummi said: Meaning the people of Mecca. لِيُخْرِجُوكَ مِنْها وَإِذاً لا يَلْبَثُونَ خِلافَكَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** to expel you from it; and then they would not have remained after you but for a little while. Meaning: if you were to leave, they would not remain after your departure except for a short time. Al-Qummi said: Meaning until they were killed at Badr. It was said that this was one year after the Hijra (الهجرة). And it was read خلفك (خلفك).

(٧٧) سُنَّةَ مَنْ قَدْ أَرْسَلْنا قَبْلَكَ مِنْ رُسُلِنا ** (77) ** The wont of those whom We have sent before you of Our apostles ** That is, Allah established that as a wont (سُنَّة), which is that He destroys every nation that expelled their messenger from their midst. وَلا تَجِدُ لِسُنَّتِنا تَحْوِيلاً ** (77) ** and you will not find any change in Our wont. ** A change (تغييراً). (٧٨) أَقِمِ الصَّلاةَ لِدُلُوكِ الشَّمْسِ ** (78) ** Maintain the prayer from the declining of the sun ** [Meaning] its passing (لزوالها). إِلى غَسَقِ اللَّيْلِ ** (78) ** until the darkness of the night, ** until its darkness (ظلمته), which is its midpoint (انتصافه). وَقُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ ** (78) ** and the [recitation of] dawn. ** [Meaning] its prayer (صلاته). إِنَّ قُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ كانَ مَشْهُوداً ** (78) ** Indeed the [recitation of] dawn is witnessed. ** by the angels of the night and the day.

In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, al-Tahdhib, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): that He was asked about what Allah has obligated of the prayers. He said: "Five prayers in the night and the day." He was asked: "Did He name them and clarify them in His Book?" He said: "Yes. Allah the Almighty said to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: أَقِمِ الصَّلاةَ لِدُلُوكِ الشَّمْسِ إِلى غَسَقِ اللَّيْلِ ** (78) ** Maintain the prayer from the declining of the sun until the darkness of the night, ** And its duluk (دلوكها) is its passing (زوالها). So, between the declining of the sun (دلوك الشمس) until the darkness of the night (غَسَقِ اللَّيْلِ) are four prayers which Allah named, clarified, and timed. And غَسَقِ اللَّيْلِ ** is its midpoint (انتصافه). Then He said: **وَقُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ إِنَّ قُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ كانَ مَشْهُوداً ** (78) ** and the [recitation of] dawn. Indeed the [recitation of] dawn is witnessed. ** So this is the fifth [prayer].

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He was asked about the best times (المواقيت) for the Fajr prayer. He said: "With the rising of the dawn (طلوع الفجر). Indeed, Allah says: **وَقُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ إِنَّ قُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ كانَ مَشْهُوداً ** (78) ** and the [recitation of] dawn. Indeed the [recitation of] dawn is witnessed. ** meaning the Fajr prayer, which is witnessed by the angels of the night and the angels of the day. So, if the servant prays the Subh [prayer] with the rising of the dawn, it is recorded for him twice: the angels of the night record it for him, and the angels of the day record it [for him].

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from the two of them (peace be upon them): regarding this verse, He said: "It gathered all the prayers. And the declining of the sun (دلوك الشمس) is its passing (زوالها), and غَسَقِ اللَّيْلِ ** is its midpoint (انتصافه)."

And He said: "Indeed, a caller from the heaven calls out every night when the night reaches its midpoint: 'Whoever slept through the 'Isha' prayer until this hour, may his eyes not sleep!'" وَقُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ ** (78) ** and the [recitation of] dawn. ** He said: "The Subh prayer." As for His saying ** كانَ مَشْهُوداً , He said: "The angels of the night and the day attend it." And there are many narratives (أخبار) with the same meaning as these narratives (الأخبار).

(٧٩) وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَتَهَجَّدْ بِهِ ** (79) And keep vigil for a part of the night, praying therein And for a part of the night, abandon sleep (الهجُود) for the prayer with the Qur'an, as a supererogatory act (نافِلَةً) for you—an additional obligation (فريضة زائدة) for you beyond the obligatory prayers (الصلوات المفروضة). In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the supererogatory prayers (النوافل), and He said: "An obligation (فريضة)." The listeners were alarmed, so He said: "I only mean the night prayer (صلاة الليل) upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, for Allah says: وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَتَهَجَّدْ بِهِ نافِلَةً لَكَ (And keep vigil for a part of the night, praying therein, as a supererogatory act for you)." In al-Khisal: Among what the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم advised Ali: "O Ali, there are three joys for the believer in this world: meeting brothers (لقاء الإِخوان), breaking the fast (الإِفطار من الصيام), and keeping vigil (التهجد) in the last part of the night." And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Adhere to the night prayer (صلوة الليل), for it is the tradition (سنّة) of your Prophet, the custom (دأب) of the righteous before you, and a repeller of sickness (مطردة الداءِ) from your bodies." And from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): He was asked why those who keep vigil (المتهجّدين) at night have the most beautiful faces among people. He said: "Because they were alone with Allah, and Allah clothed them with His light." The narrations concerning the merit of the night prayer (صلوة الليل) are countless; they should be sought in their respective places. عَسى أَنْ يَبْعَثَكَ رَبُّكَ مَقاماً مَحْمُوداً. (79) so that your Lord may raise you to a praiseworthy station (مَقاماً مَحْمُوداً). In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition mentioning the people of the gathering (أهل المحشر): "Then they will gather in another place where the station of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم will be, and that is the Praiseworthy Station (المقام المحمُود). He will then praise Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, with praise that no one before Him has praised Him with. Then He will praise every believing man and believing woman, starting with the truthful ones (الصّدّيقين) and the martyrs (الشهداءِ), then the righteous ones (الصالحين). So the inhabitants of the heavens and the inhabitants of the earth will praise Him. That is the saying of the Almighty, the Glorious: عَسى أَنْ يَبْعَثَكَ رَبُّكَ مَقاماً مَحْمُوداً (so that your Lord may raise you to a praiseworthy station). So blessedness (طوبى) is for the one who has a share (حظّ) and a portion (نصيب) on that Day, and woe (ويل) to the one who has neither a share nor a portion on that Day." And al-Ayyashi narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both) regarding His saying: عَسى أَنْ يَبْعَثَكَ رَبُّكَ مَقاماً مَحْمُوداً (so that your Lord may raise you to a praiseworthy station). He said: "It is intercession (الشفاعة)." And in Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "It is the station where I shall intercede for my community (أمّتي)." He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم also said: "When I stand at the Praiseworthy Station (المقام المحمُود), I shall intercede for those who committed major sins (أصحاب الكبائر) among my community, and Allah will grant me intercession concerning them. By Allah, I shall not intercede for anyone who harmed my progeny (ذرّيّتي)." And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "If I stand at the Praiseworthy Station (المقام المحمُود), I shall intercede for my father, my mother, my uncle, and a brother of mine who lived during the Era of Ignorance (الجاهليّة)." And from Him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the intercession (شفاعة) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم on the Day of Resurrection. He said: "On the Day of Resurrection, sweat will overwhelm (أملج) the people. They will say: 'Let us go to Adam to intercede for us.' So they will come to Adam and say to him: 'Intercede for us with your Lord.' He will say: 'I have a sin (ذنباً) and a transgression (خطيئة). You must go to Noah.' So they will come to Noah, and he will refer them to the one after him, and every prophet will refer them to the one after him until they reach Jesus (peace be upon him). He will say: 'You must go to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the Messenger of Allah.' So they will present themselves to Him and ask Him. He will say: 'Let us go.' So He will proceed with them to the gate of Paradise (باب الجنّة), face the Gate of the All-merciful (باب الرحمن), and fall down in prostration (ساجداً). He will remain [in prostration] for as long as Allah wills. Then [Allah] will say: 'Raise your head, intercede and you shall be granted intercession, ask and you shall be given.' That is His saying, the Almighty: عَسى أَنْ يَبْعَثَكَ رَبُّكَ مَقاماً مَحْمُوداً. (so that your Lord may raise you to a praiseworthy station)." And al-Ayyashi narrated from Him [al-Sadiq] and from al-Kazim (peace be upon them both) something close to this, and from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) a tradition concerning this which contains elaboration (بسط) and detail (تفصيل) regarding this meaning, which should be sought from that source.

** وَقُلْ رَبِّ أَدْخِلْنِي مُدْخَلَ صِدْقٍ وَأَخْرِجْنِي مُخْرَجَ صِدْقٍ وَاجْعَلْ لِي مِنْ لَدُنْكَ سُلْطاناً نَصِيراً ** (80) And say, 'My Lord! Make me enter a truthful entry, and make me exit a truthful exit, and appoint for me a helping authority from Yourself.' A proof (حجة) that helps me.

Al-Qummi: It was revealed on the day of the Conquest of Mecca, when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم intended to enter it. Allah revealed: قُلْ ** (Say), O Muhammad, أَدْخِلْنِي مُدْخَلَ صِدْقٍ ** (Make me enter a truthful entry) [the rest of] the Ayah. And it was said: meaning, make me enter into everything You have sent me with, an entry that is pleasing (مرضيّاً), and make me exit an exit that is pleasing, the consequence of which is praiseworthy.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked, "Does gratitude (الشّكر) have a limit, such that if the servant performs it, he is considered grateful (شاكراً)?" He said: "Yes." It was asked: "What is it?" He said: "He praises Allah for every blessing upon him concerning family and wealth, and if there is a right (حقّ) concerning the wealth He has blessed him with, he fulfills it." And from this [gratitude] is the Almighty's saying: سُبْحانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنا هذا ** (Glory be to Him who has harnessed this for us) [the rest of] the Ayah, and His saying: رَبِّ أَنْزِلْنِي مُنْزَلاً مُبارَكاً ** (My Lord! Land me at a blessed landing place) [the rest of] the Ayah, and His saying: رَبِّ أَدْخِلْنِي مُدْخَلَ صِدْقٍ وَأَخْرِجْنِي مُخْرَجَ صِدْقٍ (My Lord! Make me enter a truthful entry, and make me exit a truthful exit) [the rest of] the Ayah.

In al-Mahasin, narrated from him عليه السلام: If you enter a place (مدخلاً) that you fear, recite this Ayah: رَبِّ أَدْخِلْنِي مُدْخَلَ صِدْقٍ ** (My Lord! Make me enter a truthful entry) [the rest of] the Ayah. And if you confront that which you fear, recite the Verse of the Throne (آية الكرسيّ).

(81) وَقُلْ جاءَ الْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ الْباطِلُ ** (81) and say, 'The truth has come, and falsehood has vanished.' Islam has come, and polytheism (الشرك) has departed. إِنَّ الْباطِلَ كانَ زَهُوقاً ** Indeed falsehood is bound to vanish. vanishing/perishing (مضمحلًّا).

In al-Amali, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from his forefathers (peace be upon them): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم entered on the Day of the Conquest of Mecca (فتح مكّة), and the idols (الأصنام) were around the Ka'bah, and they were three hundred and sixty idols (صنماً). He began to strike them with a short staff (مخصرة) in His hand and say: **جاءَ الْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ الْباطِلُ إِنَّ الْباطِلَ كانَ زَهُوقاً ** (The truth has come, and falsehood has vanished. Indeed falsehood is bound to vanish.) And falsehood neither originates nor returns. So they began to fall upon their faces.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: When the Riser (القائم) rises, the dominion (دولة) of falsehood departs.

And in al-Khara'ij, narrated from Hakimah: When the Riser (القائم) was born, He was clean (نظيفاً), finished (with the birth process, مفروغاً منه), and upon His right arm was written: جاءَ الْحَقُ (The truth has come) [the rest of] the Ayah.

(٨٢) وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ ما هُوَ شِفاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (82) And We send down in the Qur’an that which is a cure and a mercy for the faithful In its meanings is the cure (شفاء) for the souls (الأرواح), and in its words (ألفاظه) is the cure (شفاء) for the bodies (الأبدان). وَلا يَزِيدُ الظَّالِمِينَ إِلَّا خَساراً ** and it increases the wrongdoers only in loss. due to their denial (تكذيبهم) and their disbelief (كفرهم) in it (1).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith whose beginning was mentioned in Surat al-Nahl: The cure (الشفاء) is only in the knowledge (علم) of the Quran, due to His saying: وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ ما هُوَ شِفاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ ** (And We send down in the Qur’an that which is a cure and a mercy) for its people (أهله). There is no doubt (شكّ) in it, nor uncertainty (مرية). And its people (أهله) are the Imams of Guidance (أئمّة الهدى), concerning whom Allah said: ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا الْكِتابَ الَّذِينَ اصْطَفَيْنا مِنْ عِبادِنا.** (Then We gave the Book as inheritance to those of Our servants whom We chose.)

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Gabriel (peace be upon him) descended upon Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم (saying): and it increases the wrongdoers—meaning those who wronged Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم their right (حقّهم)—إِلَّا خَساراً (only in loss).

In Tibb al-A'immah (طبّ الأئمّة), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): No believer ever complained of an ailment (شكاية) and recited, with sincere intention (إخلاص نيّة), while wiping the site of the illness (موضع العلّة): وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ ما هُوَ شِفاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَلا يَزِيدُ الظَّالِمِينَ إِلَّا خَساراً (And We send down in the Qur’an that which is a cure and a mercy for the faithful and it increases the wrongdoers only in loss), except that he was cured (عوفي) of that illness, whatever illness it may have been. The confirmation (مصداق) of this is in the Ayah where He says: شِفاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (a cure and a mercy for the faithful.)

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): There is no harm (لا بأس) in al-ruqyah (الرُّقْيَةِ) (2), al-ʿawdhah (العَوْذَةِ), and al-nushrah (النُّشْرَةِ) if they are from the Quran. And whoever is not cured (يشفه) by the Quran, may Allah not cure him. Is there anything more eloquent (أبلغ) than the Quran among these things? Does not Allah say: وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ ما هُوَ شِفاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (And We send down in the Qur’an that which is a cure and a mercy for the faithful.)

(٨٣) وَإِذا أَنْعَمْنا عَلَى الْإِنْسانِ ** (83) And when We bestow Our favor upon man with health and abundance, أَعْرَضَ ** he turns away from the remembrance of Allah وَنَأى بِجانِبِهِ ** he twists his side (لَوَّى عِطْفَهُ) and distances himself from Him with his soul, as if he is self-sufficient (مُسْتَغْنٍ) and independent (مُسْتَبِدٌّ بِأَمْرِهِ) in his affair. وَإِذا مَسَّهُ الشَّرُّ ** such as illness or poverty, كانَ يَؤُساً ** he becomes severely despairing (شَدِيدَ الْيَأْسِ) of the mercy (رَوْحِ) of Allah.

(٨٤) قُلْ كُلٌّ يَعْمَلُ عَلى شاكِلَتِهِ ** (84) Say, ‘Everyone acts according to his own disposition.’ [It means] according to that which resembles his state (حاله) in guidance (الهدى) and misguidance (الضّلالة). فَرَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ هُوَ أَهْدى سَبِيلاً. (Your Lord knows best him who is better guided to the way.) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The intention (النية) is better than the action (العمل). Indeed, the intention (النية) is the action (العمل). Then He recited: قُلْ كُلٌّ يَعْمَلُ عَلى شاكِلَتِهِ ** meaning: according to his intention (نيّته). And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The people of the Fire were only made eternal in the Fire because their intentions (نيّاتهم) in the world were that if they were made eternal therein, they would disobey Allah forever. And the people of Paradise were only made eternal in Paradise because their intentions (نيّاتهم) in the world were that if they remained therein, they would obey Allah forever. Thus, by intentions (بالنيّات) were these and those made eternal. Then He recited: قُلْ كُلٌّ يَعْمَلُ عَلى شاكِلَتِهِ. And in al-Faqih, al-Tahdhib, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): That He was asked about praying (الصلوة) in synagogues (البيع) and churches (الكنائس). He said: "Pray (صلّ) in them." I said: "Should I pray in them even if they are praying in them?" He said: "Yes. Do you not read the Quran? قُلْ كُلٌّ يَعْمَلُ عَلى شاكِلَتِهِ فَرَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ هُوَ أَهْدى سَبِيلاً (Say, ‘Everyone acts according to his own disposition.’ Your Lord knows best him who is better guided to the way.) Pray toward the Qiblah (القبلة) and leave them."

(٨٥) وَيَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الرُّوحِ قُلِ الرُّوحُ مِنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّي. (85) And they ask you concerning the Spirit. Say, 'The Spirit is by the command of my Lord.'

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "A creation greater than Gabriel (جبرئيل) and Michael (ميكائيل). It was with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and it is with the Imams عليهم السلام, and it is from the dominion (الملكوت)."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and He said: "A great creation, greater than Gabriel (جبرئيل) and Michael (ميكائيل). It was not with anyone who passed away except Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and it is with the Imams, strengthening them (يسدّدهم). And not everything that is sought is found."

And narrated from both of them (peace be upon them) regarding this Ayah: Verily, the Spirit (الرُّوحِ) is a creation among His creations. It possesses sight (بصر), strength (قوّة), and support (تأييد), which He places in the hearts of the believers and the Messengers.

And narrated from one of them (peace be upon them) regarding this Ayah: He was asked, "What is the Spirit (الرُّوحِ)?" He said: "That which is in the beasts (الدوابّ) and people." He was asked: "And what is it?" He said: "It is from the dominion (الملكوت), from the Power (القدرة)."

I say: The complete discussion regarding the meaning of the Spirit (الروح) has already preceded in Surat al-Hijr, so we shall not repeat it. What is mentioned in the narrations is information about that by which it is distinguished from others, and what is left ambiguous in the Ayah is its reality, so there is no contradiction. وَمَا أُوتِيتُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (and you have been given of the knowledge nothing except a few [things]).

Al-Qummi: Verily, the Jews (اليَهود) asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم about the Spirit (الرّوح). He said: الرُّوحُ مِنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّي وَمَا أُوتِيتُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (The Spirit is by the command of my Lord, and you have been given of the knowledge nothing except a few [things]). They said: "Do you mean us specifically?" He said: "No, rather, mankind generally." They said: "How can these two things be reconciled, O Muhammad? You claim that you have not been given of knowledge except a little, yet you have been given the Quran, and we have been given the Torah, and you have recited: وَمَنْ يُؤْتَ الْحِكْمَةَ فَقَدْ أُوتِيَ خَيْراً كَثِيراً ** (and whoever is given wisdom has certainly been given much good)." So Allah revealed: وَلَوْ أَنَّ مَا فِي الْأَرْضِ مِنْ شَجَرَةٍ أَقْلَامٌ وَالْبَحْرُ يَمُدُّهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ سَبْعَةُ أَبْحُرٍ مَا نَفِدَتْ كَلِمَاتُ اللَّهِ** (Were all the trees on the earth pens, and the sea [ink], with seven more seas added to it, the Words of Allah would not be exhausted). He says: The knowledge of Allah is greater than that, and what you have been given is much among you, but little in the sight of Allah.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Regarding the saying of Allah: وَما أُوتِيتُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** (and you have been given of the knowledge nothing except a few [things]). He said: "Its esoteric interpretation (تفسيرها في الباطن) is that knowledge has not been given except to a few people (أناس يسير)." Then He said: وَما أُوتِيتُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** (and you have been given of the knowledge nothing except a few [things])—meaning, from among you.

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a narration, He said: "And He described those who were not given the benefits of knowledge from Allah, so they described their Lord with base similitudes (أنى الأمثال) and likened Him to those among them who are similar, concerning that which they were ignorant of. Therefore, He said: وَمَا أُوتِيتُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (and you have been given of the knowledge nothing except a few [things]). So He has no likeness (شبه), nor similitude (مثل), nor equal (عدل)."

(٨٦) وَلَئِنْ شِئْنا لَنَذْهَبَنَّ بِالَّذِي أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ ** (86) And if We wish, We will surely take away what We have revealed to you We would take away the Quran and erase it from the written copies (المصاحف) and the breasts (الصدور). ثُمَّ لا تَجِدُ لَكَ بِهِ عَلَيْنا وَكِيلاً **(١) then you will not find for yourself any guardian against Us concerning it. one who relies upon Us (or: acts as a guardian) concerning its retrieval and its return, preserved and protected.

(٨٧) إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** except that your Lord shows mercy to you and restores it to you إِنَّ فَضْلَهُ كانَ عَلَيْكَ كَبِيراً (٨٧)** (87) except as a mercy from your Lord. Indeed, His grace has been great upon you.

(٨٨) قُلْ لَئِنِ اجْتَمَعَتِ الْإِنْسُ وَالْجِنُّ عَلى أَنْ يَأْتُوا بِمِثْلِ هذَا الْقُرْآنِ ** in eloquence, excellence of composition, and profundity (جزالة) of meaning, they would not bring the like of it, even though among them are the eloquent Arabs (العرباء), the masters of rhetoric (أرباب البيان), and the people of verification (أهل التّحقيق) لا يَأْتُونَ بِمِثْلِهِ وَلَوْ كانَ بَعْضُهُمْ لِبَعْضٍ ظَهِيراً (٨٨)** even if they supported one another in producing it. (88) Say, 'Should humans and jinn rally to bring the like of this Qur’ān, they would not bring the like of it, even if they assisted one another.'

In al-Uyun, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah Almighty sent down this Quran with these letters (الحروف) that all Arabs use, then He said: قُلْ لَئِنِ اجْتَمَعَتِ ** (Say, 'Should humans and jinn rally...') the Ayah.

In al-Khara'ij, concerning the signs (أعلام) of al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Ibn Abi al-Awja' and three men from the Materialists (الدّهريّة) agreed that each of them would challenge one quarter of the Quran. They were in Mecca and pledged to return with their challenge in the following year. When the year passed and they gathered at the Station of Ibrahim (peace be upon him), one of them said: "When I saw His saying: يا أَرْضُ ابْلَعِي ماءَكِ وَيا سَماءُ أَقْلِعِي وَغِيضَ الْماءُ ** (O earth, swallow your water, and O sky, cease [raining]! And the water subsided), I refrained from challenging it." And the other said: "Likewise, when I found His saying: فَلَمَّا اسْتَيْأَسُوا مِنْهُ خَلَصُوا نَجِيًّا ** (When they despaired of [getting] him, they withdrew for consultation), I despaired of challenging it." They were concealing this when al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) passed by them. He turned to them and recited to them: قُلْ لَئِنِ اجْتَمَعَتِ الْإِنْسُ وَالْجِنُ ** (Say, 'Should humans and jinn rally...'), the Ayah, and they were dumbfounded (فَبُهِتُوا).

(٨٩) وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنا We have repeated in different ways, increasing the confirmation (التقرير) and clarification (البيان) لِلنّاسِ فِي هذَا الْقُرْآنِ مِنْ كُلِّ مَثَلٍ (89) Certainly We have elaborated for the people in this Quran every kind of parable. meaning, from every meaning (معنى) that is like the parable (المثل) in its strangeness (غرابته) or its impact (وقوعه موقعاً) upon the souls (الأنفس). فَأَبى أَكْثَرُ النَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُوراً ** But most people refuse everything except ingratitude. except denial (جحوداً).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Gabriel descended with this Ayah thus: But most people refused the guardianship (بولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him) إِلَّا كُفُوراً. (except ingratitude).

(٩٠) وَقالُوا لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى تَفْجُرَ لَنا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ يَنْبُوعاً ** (90) And they said, 'We will never believe you until you cause a spring to gush forth for us from the ground (The word ينبوعاً means) a spring (عيناً). They said this out of stubbornness (عناداً), persistence (لجاجاً), seeking difficulty (تعنّتاً), and making a demand (اقتراحاً), after the proof (الحجّة) had become binding upon them through the demonstration of the miraculous nature (إعجاز) of the Quran and the addition of other miracles (المعجزات) to it.

(٩١) أَوْ تَكُونَ لَكَ جَنَّةٌ an orchard (بستان) مِنْ نَخِيلٍ وَعِنَبٍ فَتُفَجِّرَ الْأَنْهارَ خِلالَها تَفْجِيراً. (91) or you have a garden of date palms and vines, and make rivers gush forth in its midst, gushing forth. (٩٢) أَوْ تُسْقِطَ السَّماءَ كَما زَعَمْتَ عَلَيْنا كِسَفاً pieces (قطعاً). They mean the Almighty's saying: وَإِنْ يَرَوْا كِسْفاً مِنَ السَّماءِ ساقِطاً يَقُولُوا سَحابٌ مَرْكُومٌ ** (44) And if they see a fragment of the sky falling, they will say, 'A mass of clouds.' And it was read with the fatḥah (فتح) on the sīn (السّين). أَوْ تَأْتِيَ بِاللهِ وَالْمَلائِكَةِ قَبِيلاً in abundance (كثيراً) or facing (مقابلاً), meaning: while they are facing us, we observe them and witness them. (92) or you make the sky fall upon us in fragments, as you claim, or bring Allah and the angels face to face.

(٩٣) أَوْ يَكُونَ لَكَ بَيْتٌ مِنْ زُخْرُفٍ (93) or you have a house of gold, meaning, of gold (من ذهب). Its origin (أصله) is adornment (الزّينة). أَوْ تَرْقى فِي السَّماءِ ** (or ascend into the sky.) in its ascents (في معارجها). وَلَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لِرُقِيِّكَ ** (Yet we will not believe in your ascension) for your ascent alone (لصُعودك وحدك). حَتَّى تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنا كِتاباً نَقْرَؤُهُ ** (until you bring down to us a book that we may read.) in which is your confirmation (فيه تصديقك). قُلْ سُبْحانَ رَبِّي (Say, 'Immaculate is my Lord!) [This is] a declaration of Allah's transcendence (تنزيهاً لله) from anyone dictating to Him (أن يتحكم عليه أحد) and from bringing what the ignorant propose (بما يقترحه الجهّال). And it was read: He said—meaning the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم—هَلْ كُنْتُ إِلَّا بَشَراً رَسُولاً ** (Was I anything but a human messenger?), like the rest of the Messengers (كسائر الرسل). And they would not come to their people except with the signs (الآيات) that Allah manifested through them, according to what suited the condition of their people. The matter of the signs is not up to me (إليّ); rather, it belongs to Allah (إنّما هو إلى الله), and He is the Knower of what is beneficial (العالم بالمصالح). Thus, there is no justification (فلا وجه) for your requesting them from me.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): يَنْبُوعاً ** (a spring) means a fountain (عيناً). لَكَ جَنَّةٌ ** (you have a garden) means an orchard (بستان). تَفْجِيراً (gushing forth) means from those fountains (من تلك العيون). كِسَفاً ** (a fragment). This is because the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said that a fragment (كسفاً) would fall from the sky, due to His saying: وَإِنْ يَرَوْا كِسْفاً مِنَ السَّماءِ ساقِطاً يَقُولُوا سَحابٌ مَرْكُومٌ ** (4) And if they see a fragment of the sky falling, they say, 'A massed cloud!' He said: And al-Qabīl (القَبيل) means the large group (الكثير), and al-Zukhruf (الزّخرف) means gold (الذهب). كِتاباً نَقْرَؤُهُ ** (a book that we may read). They meant [a book] from Allah to Abdullah ibn Abi Umayyah, [stating] that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is truthful, and that 'I am the One Who sent him,' and that four angels should come with him testifying that Allah is the One Who wrote it. So Allah revealed: قُلْ سُبْحانَ رَبِّي ** (Say, 'Immaculate is my Lord!')—the Ayah.

In al-Ihtijaj and Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him), concerning Surat al-Baqarah, regarding the Almighty's saying: أَمْ تُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَسْئَلُوا رَسُولَكُمْ كَما سُئِلَ مُوسى مِنْ قَبْلُ (108) Do you want to question your Apostle, just as Moses was questioned before? narrated from his father (peace be upon him): That the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was sitting one day in Mecca in the courtyard (بفناء) (1) of the Ka'bah, when a group of the chiefs of Quraysh gathered, among them al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah al-Makhzumi, Abu al-Bakhtari ibn Hisham, Abu Jahl ibn Hisham, al-As ibn Wa'il al-Sahmi, and Abdullah ibn Abi Umayyah al-Makhzumi. With them was a large gathering of those who followed them. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was with a small group (نفر) of his Companions, reciting to them the Book of Allah and conveying to them Allah's command and prohibition (أمره ونهيه). The polytheists said to one another: "The affair of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم has become serious (استفحل) (1) and his matter (خطبه) (2) has grown great. Come, let us begin by reproaching him (بتقريعه), silencing him (وتبكيتِهِ) (3), rebuking him (وتوبيخِه), arguing against him (والاحتجاج عليه), and invalidating what he has brought, so that his matter may become insignificant (يهون خطبه) to his Companions and his status may diminish (ويصغر قدره) in their eyes. Perhaps he will desist (ينزع) (4) from the error (غيّه) (5), falsehood (باطله), rebellion (وتمرّده) (6), and transgression (وطغيانه) in which he is engaged."

(١) Fanā’ al-Dār (فناء الدار), like kisā’ (كساء), is the wide area in front of it. Plural: afniyah (افنية) and fanā (فنى) (Q.).

(٩٤) وَما مَنَعَ النَّاسَ أَنْ يُؤْمِنُوا إِذْ جاءَهُمُ الْهُدى إِلَّا أَنْ قالُوا أَبَعَثَ اللهُ بَشَراً رَسُولاً ** (94) What kept the people from believing when the guidance came to them was only their saying, 'Has Allah sent a human being as [His] apostle?' And nothing prevented them from belief after the truth had appeared except their denial that Allah should send a human being. (٩٥) قُلْ جواباً لشبهتهم لَوْ كانَ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَلائِكَةٌ يَمْشُونَ كما يمشي بنو آدم مُطْمَئِنِّينَ ساكنين فيها لَنَزَّلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ مَلَكاً رَسُولاً ** (95) Say, 'Had there been angels in the earth walking about at ease, We would have surely sent down to them an angel from the sky as an apostle.' Say (in response to their doubt (شبهتهم)): Had there been angels in the earth walking about just as the children of Adam walk at ease dwelling therein, We would have surely sent down to them an angel from the sky as an apostle, so that they could meet with him and receive (the message) from him. As for mankind, the majority of them are blind (عُماة) to perceiving the angel (الملك) and receiving (the message) from him, for that is conditioned upon a type of suitability (التناسب) and homogeneity (التجانس), and that is only for the one who is fit for prophethood (النبوّة).

(٩٦) قُلْ كَفى بِاللهِ شَهِيداً بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ ** (Say, 'Allah suffices as a witness between me and you.') concerning that I am a messenger to you and that I have fulfilled what was incumbent upon me regarding the conveyance (التبليغ). إِنَّهُ كانَ بِعِبادِهِ خَبِيراً بَصِيراً ** (Indeed He is well-aware of His servants, all-seeing.) He knows their inner (الباطنة) and outer (الظاهرة) states, so He will recompense them for it. And in this, there is a consolation for the Messenger and a threat to the disbelievers (الكفّار).

(٩٧) وَمَنْ يَهْدِ اللهُ فَهُوَ الْمُهْتَدِ وَمَنْ يُضْلِلْ فَلَنْ تَجِدَ لَهُمْ أَوْلِياءَ مِنْ دُونِهِ (Whomever Allah guides is rightly guided, and whomever He leads astray—you will never find any guardians for them besides Him) who would guide them. وَنَحْشُرُهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ عَلى وُجُوهِهِمْ. (We shall muster them on the Day of Resurrection on their faces.) In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: A man said, "O Prophet of Allah, how will the disbeliever (الكافر) be mustered on his face on the Day of Resurrection?" He said: "Indeed, the One Who made him walk on his two legs is capable of making him walk on his face on the Day of Resurrection." And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from one of the two Imams عليهما السلام regarding عَلى وُجُوهِهِمْ (on their faces), he said: "On their foreheads (على جباههم)." عُمْياً وَبُكْماً وَصُمًّا (blind, dumb, and deaf.) They will not see what delights their eyes, nor hear what pleases their ears, nor speak what benefits them and is accepted from them. This is because in the world (الدنيا) they did not gain insight (يستبصِروا) through the signs (الآيات) and lessons (العبر), and they feigned deafness (تصامّوا) regarding the hearing of the truth (الحق), and they refused to speak. مَأْواهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ كُلَّما خَبَتْ (Their refuge is hell. Whenever it subsides,) meaning, it is extinguished by having consumed their skins and flesh. زِدْناهُمْ سَعِيراً (We will intensify the blaze for them.) meaning, by replacing their skins and flesh, so that it returns blazing and raging with them. It is as if, because they denied the return (الاعادة) after annihilation (الافناء), Allah recompensed them by ensuring they would perpetually undergo return and annihilation (الإعادة والافناء). And to this He referred with His saying.

(٩٨) ذلِكَ جَزاؤُهُمْ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَفَرُوا بِآياتِنا وَقالُوا أَإِذا كُنَّا عِظاماً وَرُفاتاً أَإِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ خَلْقاً جَدِيداً ** (98) That is their requital because they defied Our signs and said, 'When we have become bones and dust, shall we really be raised up as a new creation?' meaning, then We cause them to perish and We bring them back, so that this increases their regret over the denial of the Resurrection (البعث).

Al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from Al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): That in Hell (جهنّم) there is a valley called al-Sa'ir (السّعير). When Hell (جهنّم) subsides, its Sa'ir (سعيرها) is opened, and that is His saying: **كُلَّما خَبَتْ زِدْناهُمْ سَعِيراً ** (Whenever it subsides, We will intensify the blaze for them.) meaning, whenever it is extinguished.

(٩٩) أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أولم يعلمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ قادِرٌ عَلى أَنْ يَخْلُقَ مِثْلَهُمْ ** (99) Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and the earth, is able to create the likes of them? For they are not a harder creation than them (the heavens and the earth), just as He said: أَأَنْتُمْ أَشَدُّ خَلْقاً أَمِ السَّماءُ ** (Are you a harder creation or the sky?) Nor is the re-creation harder for Him than the initial creation, just as He said, rather: هُوَ أَهْوَنُ عَلَيْهِ وَجَعَلَ لَهُمْ أَجَلاً لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ ** (it is easier for Him. And He has appointed for them a term in which there is no doubt.) That (term) is death or the Resurrection (القيامة). Yet the wrongdoers refused, despite the clarity of the truth, إِلَّا كُفُوراً ** (except ingratitude), meaning: except denial (إلّا جحوداً).

(100) قُلْ لَوْ أَنْتُمْ تَمْلِكُونَ خَزائِنَ رَحْمَةِ رَبِّي ** (100) Say, 'If you possessed the treasuries of my Lord's mercy, (meaning) the treasuries of Allah's provisions and His favors upon His creation, إِذاً لَأَمْسَكْتُمْ خَشْيَةَ الْإِنْفاقِ you would withhold [them] for fear of spending [them] up.' you would be stingy (لَبَخِلْتُمْ) fearing depletion (النَّفَاد) through spending (بِالْإِنْفَاق), since there is no one except that he chooses benefit for himself, and even if he prefers another with something, he only prefers him for a compensation (عِوَض) that surpasses it. Thus, there is no Generous One (جَوَاد) except Allah, Who gives without compensation (عِوَض). وَكانَ الْإِنْسانُ قَتُوراً Indeed man is stingy. stingy (بَخِيلًا), because the foundation of his affair is built upon need (الْحَاجَة), and miserliness (الضِّنَّة) regarding what he needs, and observing compensation (الْعِوَض) in what he expends.

Al-Qummi, regarding this Ayah, said: "If the affairs were in the hands of people, they would not give people anything for fear of annihilation (الْفَنَاءِ)."

وَكانَ الْإِنْسانُ قَتُوراً ** Indeed man is stingy. meaning stingy (بَخِيلًا).

(101) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا مُوسى تِسْعَ آياتٍ بَيِّناتٍ. (101) And We certainly gave Moses nine manifest signs. In al-Khisal and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the locusts (الجراد), the lice (القمل), the frogs (الضفادع), the blood (الدّم), the flood (الطوفان), the sea (البحر), the stone (الحجر), the staff (العَصا), and his hand (يده). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) and al-Qummi: The like of it. And in Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), when a group of Jews asked Him about them, He said: The staff (العصا), his bringing forth his hand from his pocket white, the locusts (الجراد), the lice (القمّل), the frogs (الضفادع), the blood (الدّم), the raising of Mount Tur (رفع الطّور), the manna (المنّ) and the quails (السّلوى) as a single sign, and the splitting of the sea (فلق البحر). They said: "You have spoken the truth." And in al-Majma': A Jew asked the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) about these signs, and He said: They are that you do not associate anything with Him (لا تشركوا به شيئاً), nor commit excess (لا تسرفوا), nor commit fornication (لا تزنوا), nor kill the soul which Allah has forbidden except by right (إلّا بالحقّ), nor walk with an innocent person to a ruler (سلطان) so that he may be killed, nor practice sorcery (لا تسحروا), nor consume usury (الرّبا), nor accuse a chaste woman (المحصنة), nor turn your backs in flight on the day of battle (يوم الزّحف). And upon you specifically, O Jews, that you do not transgress concerning the Sabbath (السّبت). So he kissed His hand and said: "I bear witness that you are a Prophet." فَسْئَلْ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ إِذْ جاءَهُمْ ** So ask the Children of Israel when He came to them. It is said: Meaning, O Muhammad, ask the Children of Israel about what transpired between Moses and Pharaoh when He came to them, or about the signs, so that your truthfulness may become manifest to the polytheists, your soul may be comforted, and your certainty may increase. Thus, it is an interjection (اعتراض). And إِذْ جاءَهُمْ ** is related to "Our signs" (آياتنا). فَقالَ لَهُ فِرْعَوْنُ إِنِّي لَأَظُنُّكَ يا مُوسى مَسْحُوراً ** Then Pharaoh said to him, 'O Moses, I indeed think you are bewitched.' You have been bewitched, so your mind is confused (فتخبّط عقلك).

**(١٠٢) قالَ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ ما أَنْزَلَ هؤُلاءِ إِلَّا رَبُّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ بَصائِرَ وَإِنِّي لَأَظُنُّكَ يا فِرْعَوْنُ مَثْبُوراً ** (102) He said, 'You certainly know that no one has sent down these [signs] except the Lord of the heavens and the earth, as insights, and indeed I think you, O Pharaoh, are doomed.')

The Almighty's saying: قالَ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ ** (He said: 'You certainly know), O Pharaoh, and it was read with the ḍammah on the tā’ (عَلِمْتُ - I know), ما أَنْزَلَ هؤُلاءِ ** (what has sent down these), meaning the signs, إِلَّا رَبُّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ بَصائِرَ ** (except the Lord of the heavens and the earth, as insights), meaning clear proofs that make you see my truthfulness, but you are obstinate, وَإِنِّي لَأَظُنُّكَ يا فِرْعَوْنُ مَثْبُوراً ** (and indeed I think you, O Pharaoh, are doomed), meaning turned away from [the true good] (الخير الحق) or perished. He countered his false conjecture with His correct conjecture.

In al-Majma', it is narrated that Ali (peace be upon him) said regarding عَلِمْتَ : "By Allah, the enemy of Allah did not know, but rather it was Moses (peace be upon him) who knew, so he said: لَقَدْ عَلِمْتُ (I certainly know)."

I say: This means that it is with the ḍammah on the tā’ (التاء), not with the fatḥah (الفتحة).

(١٠٣) فَأَرادَ ** فرعون (103) So he desired (Pharaoh (فرعون)) أَنْ يَسْتَفِزَّهُمْ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ ** (to provoke them out of the land) That he might treat Moses (peace be upon him) and his people lightly (يَسْتَخِفَّ), and banish them from the land by uprooting them (بالاستيصال). And in the narration of القمّيّ, [it means] from the land of Egypt (مصر). فَأَغْرَقْناهُ (We drowned him) وَمَنْ مَعَهُ جَمِيعاً ** (and those who were with him, all together) So We provoked him and his people by drowning (بالاغراق). القمّيّ narrated from الباقر (peace be upon him): He desired to expel them from the land, and Pharaoh and his people already knew that none had sent down those signs except Allah. I say: And this narration is evidence for the فتح of the تاء (التّاءِ).

(١٠٤) وَقُلْنا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ after Pharaoh and his drowning لِبَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ اسْكُنُوا الْأَرْضَ which he intended to drive you out of فَإِذا جاءَ وَعْدُ الْآخِرَةِ جِئْنا بِكُمْ لَفِيفاً mixed together, then We will judge between you. And al-lafīf (اللفيف) means groups from various tribes. (And We said to the Children of Israel after him: 'Take up residence in the land, and when the promise of the Hereafter comes, We shall bring you in a motley crowd.') In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: regarding لَفِيفاً , He says: all together (جميعاً). And in another narration: from every direction (من كلّ ناحية).

(١٠٥) وَبِالْحَقِّ أَنْزَلْناهُ وَبِالْحَقِّ نَزَلَ ** (105) We have sent it down with the truth, and with the truth it has come down. And We did not send down the Quran except with the truth, and it did not descend except with the truth. وَما أَرْسَلْناكَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّراً ** and We have not sent you except as a bearer of good news and as a warner. as a bearer of good news for the obedient with reward, and as a warner for the disobedient with punishment. (١٠٦) وَقُرْآناً فَرَقْناهُ ** (106) We have divided the Qur’ān into portions We sent it down piecemeal. In al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): (فَرَّقْنَاهُ) with the doubling (بالتّشديد). لِتَقْرَأَهُ عَلَى النَّاسِ عَلى مُكْثٍ ** so that you may recite it to the people slowly, slowly (عَلَى مَهْلٍ) and deliberately, for that is easier for memorization and more helpful for understanding. وَنَزَّلْناهُ تَنْزِيلاً ** and We have sent it down in successive stages. according to the occurrences (الحوادث).

(107) قُلْ آمِنُوا بِهِ أَوْ لا تُؤْمِنُوا ** (107) Say, 'Believe in it, or do not believe in it.' For your belief in the Quran does not increase it in perfection, and your refusal of it does not cause it any deficiency. إِنَّ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ ** Indeed those who were given knowledge before it, meaning the scholars who read the previous books and knew the reality of the revelation (الوحي) and the signs (أمارات) of prophethood (النّبوّة), and were able to distinguish between the truthful (المحقّ) and the false (المبطل). Al-Qummi said: meaning the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) who believed in the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. إِذا يُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ ** (when it is recited to them) — meaning the Quran — يَخِرُّونَ لِلْأَذْقانِ سُجَّداً ** when it is recited to them, fall down on their faces in prostration, they fall down on their faces in glorification of the command of Allah and in gratitude for His fulfillment of His promise in those books by sending Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم after an interval between the Messengers, and sending down the Quran upon him.

(١٠٨) وَيَقُولُونَ سُبْحانَ رَبِّنا إِنْ كانَ وَعْدُ رَبِّنا لَمَفْعُولاً ** (108) And they say, 'Immaculate is our Lord! Indeed, the promise of our Lord is bound to be fulfilled.' (They declare Him immaculate) from breaking the promise. إِنْ كانَ وَعْدُ رَبِّنا لَمَفْعُولاً ** (Indeed, the promise of our Lord is bound to be fulfilled.) meaning: His promise was inevitably existent (كائناً لا محالة). (١٠٩) وَيَخِرُّونَ لِلْأَذْقانِ يَبْكُونَ ** (109) They fall down on their faces, weeping, He repeated it (the description of falling down) due to the difference in the two states (الحالين): the first is their falling down out of gratitude (للشّكر) and the fulfillment of the promise (وانجاز الوعد) while they are prostrating (ساجدين); and the second is their falling down due to the admonitions (المواعظ) that affected them while they are weeping (باكين). And the chin (الذّقن) was mentioned because it is the first part of the prostrator's face (وجه الساجد) that meets the ground. And al-Qummi interpreted الْأَذْقانِ ** (the faces) as the faces (بالوجوه). And the meaning of the lām (in لِلْأَذْقانِ) is specification (الإِختصاص), because they dedicated their chins (أذقانهم) and their faces (وجوههم) to prostration (للسّجُود) and falling down (والخرور). وَيَزِيدُهُمْ ** (and it increases them) meaning: the hearing of the Quran (سماع القرآن) خُشُوعاً ** (in humility) because it increases them in knowledge (علماً) and certainty (يقيناً).

(١١٠) قُلِ ادْعُوا اللهَ أَوِ ادْعُوا الرَّحْمنَ ** (110) Say, 'Invoke Allah or invoke the All-beneficent.' Name Allah by whichever of the two Names you wish, for they are equal (سِيَّان) in the goodness of application (حُسْن الإِطْلاق), and the meaning intended by both of them is one. أَيًّا ما تَدْعُوا فَلَهُ الْأَسْماءُ الْحُسْنى By whichever [Name] you invoke Him, all the Best Names belong to Him. That is, whichever of these two Names you name and mention, it is good. So, فَلَهُ الْأَسْماءُ الْحُسْنى ** (all the Best Names belong to Him) was placed in its stead for emphasis (لِلْمُبَالَغَة) and to indicate that which is the proof for it, for if all His Names are good, then these two Names are also good because they are among them. And the (ما) is extra (مَزِيدَة), confirming the condition (مُؤَكِّدَة لِلشَّرْط). The pronoun in (لَهُ) refers to the One Named (الْمُسَمَّى), because the naming (التَّسْمِيَة) is for Him, not for the Name. And the meaning of His Names being the best of Names is their independence (اسْتِقْلالُهَا) in conveying the meanings of glorification (التَّمْجِيد), magnification (التَّعْظِيم), and sanctification (التَّقْدِيس), and their indication of the attributes of Majesty (الجَلال) and Honor (الإِكْرَام). It is said: It was revealed when the polytheists (الْمُشْرِكُون) heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saying, "O Allah, O All-beneficent (يَا رَحْمَن)." They said, "He forbids us from worshipping two gods, yet he calls upon another god." And it is said: The Jews said to him, "You rarely mention the All-beneficent (الرَّحْمَن), yet Allah mentioned Him frequently in the Torah." So, it was revealed: وَلا تَجْهَرْ بِصَلاتِكَ ** Do not be loud in your prayer, meaning, in its recitation (بِقِرَاءَتِهَا). وَلا تُخافِتْ بِها وَابْتَغِ بَيْنَ ذلِكَ سَبِيلاً. nor be silent in it, but seek a way between these. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام regarding this Ayah: Being loud (الْجَهْر) in it is raising the voice, and being silent (التَّخَافُت) is what you do not hear yourself. And recite between that. And from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام regarding it: Being loud (الإِجْهَار) is that you raise your voice so that those far from you hear it, and being quiet (الإِخْفَات) is that those who are with you do not hear it except slightly (إِلَّا يَسِيرًا). And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Being loud (الْجَهْر) in it is raising the voice, and being quiet (الْمُخَافَتَة) is what your ears do not hear, and what is between that is the amount that your ears hear. And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him عليه السلام: Being quiet (الْمُخَافَتَة) is what is below your hearing, and being loud (الْجَهْر) is that you raise your voice severely (شَدِيدًا). And from him عليه السلام: He was asked whether the Imam must make those behind him hear, even if they are numerous. He said: "Let him recite a moderate recitation (قِرَاءَة وَسَطًا)," then he recited this Ayah. And al-Ayyashi narrated from both of them عليهما السلام: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was in Mecca, he would raise his voice (جَهَرَ صَوْتَهُ), and the polytheists (الْمُشْرِكُون) would know his location, so they would harm him. Thus, this Ayah was revealed at that time. And from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام: He said to Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "O my son, adhere to the good (الْحَسَنَة) between the two evils (السَّيِّئَتَيْن); it will erase them both." He asked: "How is that, O father?" He replied: "Like the saying of Allah: وَلا تَجْهَرْ ** (Do not be loud...) the Ayah; and like His saying: وَلا تَجْعَلْ يَدَكَ مَغْلُولَةً ** Do not keep your hand chained to your neck, the Ayah; and like His saying: وَالَّذِينَ إِذا أَنْفَقُوا لَمْ يُسْرِفُوا ** And those who, when they spend, are neither extravagant the Ayah. For extravagance (إِسْرَاف) is an evil (سَيِّئَة), and stinginess (إِقْتَار) is an evil (سَيِّئَة), and what is between that is moderation (قَوَامًا), which is good (حَسَنَة). So, you must adhere to the good between the two evils." I say: He intended to command him to observe moderation (التَّوَسُّط) in all matters, so that he may be safe from excess (الإِفْرَاط) and deficiency (التَّفْرِيط). And from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام regarding this Ayah: That it was abrogated (نَسَخَتْهَا) by فَاصْدَعْ بِما تُؤْمَرُ So proclaim what you have been commanded And from him عليه السلام: Its interpretation (تَفْسِيرُهَا) is: وَلا تَجْهَرْ ** (Do not be loud) with the Wilayah (وِلايَة) of Ali عليه السلام, nor with what I have honored him with, until I command you to do so. وَلا تُخافِتْ بِها ** (nor be silent in it) means: Do not conceal it from Ali عليه السلام, and inform him of what I have honored him with. وَابْتَغِ بَيْنَ ذلِكَ سَبِيلاً (but seek a way between these) means: Ask Me if I permit you to be loud concerning the matter of Ali and his Wilayah (وِلايَتِهِ). Then He permitted him to manifest that on the Day of Ghadir Khumm.

(١١١) وَقُلِ (1) الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي لَمْ يَتَّخِذْ وَلَداً وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ شَرِيكٌ فِي الْمُلْكِ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ وَلِيٌّ مِنَ الذُّلِ. (And say, 'All praise belongs to Allah, who has not taken a son, and who has no partner in sovereignty, and who has no guardian out of weakness.')

Al-Qummi said: And He has not been abased (يَذِلّ) so as to need a helper (نَاصِر) to assist Him. وَكَبِّرْهُ تَكْبِيراً. (And magnify Him with a magnificent magnification.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): A man said in His presence: الله أكبر (Allah is Greater). He (peace be upon him) said: "Allah is Greater than what thing?" The man replied: "Than everything." He (peace be upon him) said: "You have limited Him (حَدَّدْتَهُ)." The man asked: "How should I say it?" He (peace be upon him) said: "Say: Allah is Greater than can be described (أَكْبَرُ مِنْ أَنْ يُوصَفَ)." And in another narration: He (peace be upon him) said: "Was there something there so that He could be greater than it?" It was asked: "And what is it?" He replied: "Greater than can be described (أَكْبَرُ مِنْ أَنْ يُوصَفَ)."

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): That He commanded whoever reads this Ayah to pronounce the takbir (يُكَبِّر) three times.

And in al-Faqih: In the testament (وَصِيَّة) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to Ali (peace be upon him): "O Ali, the safeguard (أَمَان) for my Ummah against thieves is: قُلِ ادْعُوا اللهَ أَوِ ادْعُوا الرَّحْمنَ (Say, 'Invoke Allah or invoke the All-beneficent,')" until the end of the Surah.

And in Thawab al-A'mal, al-Majma', and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surah Bani Isra'il every Friday night will not die until he reaches al-Qa'im (may Allah hasten His relief) and will be among His companions (peace be upon him).

(1) It is said that in this Ayah there is a refutation of the Jews and Christians when they said that Allah has taken a son, and of the polytheists (مُشْرِكِي) of the Arabs when they said: "Here I am at Your service (لَبَّيْكَ), You have no partner except a partner who belongs to You," and of the Sabeans and the Magians when they said: "Were it not for the friends (أَوْلِيَاء) of Allah, Allah would have been abased (لَذَلَّ اللهُ)." (Majma' al-Bayan).
